Also, instruct her to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as her basis in recording RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of every year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the implementing offices to coordinate and strategize the implementation of all the programs/projects embodied under the 20% DF to attain desirable socio-economic targets and outcomes for the Municipality and intended beneficiaries. Moreover, require them to review the composition of the 20% DF, identify the savings from fully implemented projects and those which are no longer prioritized and, subject to the provisions of the Local Government Code and the DILG-DBM JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020, reappropriate to current/existing priority development projects.

We recommended that Management formulate programs, projects, and activities in accordance with Section 3.0 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017 and ensure that developmental projects are procurement-and-implementation-ready to attain desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes for the municipality and intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the LCE require the concerned implementing offices the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise tax in order to give the constituents, especially the tobacco farmers the early benefits that could be derived therefrom. We further recommended that the LCE advise the Local Development Council and Municipal Accountant to determine the total unexpended balances of the completed and unimplemented projects and inform the LCE for possible reappropriation and the latter to request Sangguniang Bayan to prepare resolution for the reappropriation of the unused funds in accordance with the authorized uses as prescribed in RA Nos. 7171 and 8240 and ensure that only feasible programs/projects/activities are included in the Annual Investment Program and require regular monitoring of implementation as basis in taking appropriate action.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to secure valid official receipts for the abovementioned transactions to properly substantiate the payments made to the contractors. Further, require the Municipal Treasurer to thoroughly examine the contents/ details of the official receipts issued by suppliers/ contractors before accepting the same, thus contractors are reminded of their obligations as required under BIR Regulations No. 18-2012 dated October 22, 2012 so that payments made to them will be properly receipted/ substantiated notwithstanding the accurate taxes due to the BIR.
observations.managementAction: Complied with the audit recommendation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to adopt the required procedures and conditions in the Post-Qualification stage particularly in the verification, validation and ascertainment of all statements made and documents submitted by the bidder with the Lowest Calculated Bid to ensure that they are in accordance with the requirements as stated in the Bidding Documents, thus eligible bidders would ultimately be awarded the contract.
observations.managementAction: Complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the BAC Chairman to follow the prescribed rates in the computation of honoraria based on the successfully completed projects and consider the involvement of every member of the BAC and TWG in the procurement activities. Moreover, require the Municipal Accountant to review the correctness of said claims to avoid overpayment and consequently disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Complied with the audit recommendation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents and justifications on the deficiencies observed for further evaluation of the transactions and avoid suspension in audit. Also, oblige the Municipal Accountant see to it that disbursement vouchers are duly supported with the required basic documents to ensure their validity and propriety.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the required supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE instruct:

The Municipal Treasurer to use separate set of official receipts to acknowledge the receipt of collections from the operations of each economic enterprise and prepare the corresponding RCD daily. Moreover, maintain separate cashbook in recording the transactions for each LEE.

The Municipal Accounting Office to maintain complete subsidiary ledger for each special account under the General Fund and prepare the corresponding financial statements to form part as attachments of the financial statements of the General Fund to serve as a tool in informing the Management on whether the income generated are sufficient to meet their operating costs and enable Management to evaluate them for the purpose of recommending corrective measures for improvement of the LEEs.
observations.managementAction:
Separate bank account, set of official receipts and cashbook was maintained for the operation of the public market



Separate subsidiary ledger is being maintained for the operation of the public market.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:

a. Direct the members of the Local COVID-19 Task Force to prepare the Local COVID-19 Task Force Daily Report and submit the same to the regulatory agencies;

b. Require the Municipal Health Office (MHO) and the Contact Tracing Teams to provide timely and accurate information to the Local COVID-19 Task Force for the preparation of the report; and,

c. Instruct the MHO, the members of the Local COVID-9 Task Force and the Contact Tracing teams to keep abreast with the issuances and requirements of regulatory agencies by attending conferences, browsing agency websites or undertaking other means that may be deemed appropriate.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Health Office is still preparing the reports and submit the same to the regulatory agencies.

Have already provided.

The Local COVID-9 Task Force and the Contact Tracing with the issuances and requirements of regulatory agencies by attending conferences, browsing agency websites or undertaking other means that may be deemed appropriate.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:

a. Require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) through its Secretariat to post the emergency procurement activities under the Bayanihan Act in the GPPB Online Portal; and,

b. Direct the BAC to be abreast with the issuances of the regulatory agencies pertaining to procurement activities.
observations.managementAction: The management shall comply with the standards of reporting.
observations.reason: On-going evaluation by BAC.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:

a. Require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (MDRRMO), Municipal General Service Officer (MGSO) and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records pertaining to the inventory of donated items received;

b. Instruct the MDRRMO, MGSO and Municipal Accountant to review the line of communication and information dissemination within their offices; and,

c. Direct the Municipal General Service Officer to submit a Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances.
observations.managementAction:
Formulated a COVID-19 Waste Management Plan and to incorporate the same in the Local Solid Waste Management Plan; COVID-19 Waste Management Plan implementation in barangay levels and local is satisfactorily determined.

The MENRO to conduct monitoring on each barangay to ensure that COVID-19 related health care wastes are segregated at the household level; Have been requested and ready to comply.

Provided the documentation on the segregation and storage activities for reference and archiving.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:

a) the Inventory Committee to conduct semi-annual count of inventories and to prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI); and,

b) the Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to perform reconciliation of inventory records.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has directed the inventory team to conduct year-end inventory of PPA’s, and was conducted by the inventory team. Yet results to reconcile with Accounting and GSO.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:

a. Direct the official/personnel concerned to comply with the prescribed guidelines set under COA Circular 2018-02 in order to insure the properties and supplies of the Agency and to conform to RA No. 656, as amended by PD No. 245; and,

b. Request the Sangguniang Bayan to provide a supplemental budget for the application of insurance for the properties.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal GSO is still evaluating the various PPE to insured to the GSIS. Due to impairment and obsolescence only identified PPE is covered with insurance.
observations.reason: Obsolete and damaged PPE not covered.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to undertake the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the result of reconciliation with the GSO.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal GSO and Municipal Accounting Office is reconciling the PPE – Inventory per year on the date of purchase or full construction, due to other pressing matters, the reconciliation is yet to be done.
observations.reason: Ongoing process

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounting records with property records of the General Services Office; and, Require the Municipal Accountant to undertake the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the result of reconciliation with the GSO.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant shall take necessary procedures to update the account.
observations.reason: Subject for review and reconciliatio n

observations.recommendation: The Inventory Committee to conduct semi-annual count of inventories and to prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI); and, The Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer to perform reconciliation of inventory records.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Mayor has directed the inventory team to conduct year-end inventory of PPA’s, and was conducted by the inventory team. Yet results to reconcile with Accounting and GSO.

The Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer to perform reconciliation of inventory records.
observations.reason: Records for update and reconciliatio n.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Acting Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned Officials to submit to COA the lacking documents to support the payment of the purchase of lot and to explain in writing why the sale was consummated despite the pronouncement of the MAC that the subject property was also owned by another entity to avoid possible suspension/disallowance in audit. Likewise, work on the immediate transfer of ownership of the property to the Municipality and submit a certified true copy of the new Transfer Certificate of Title to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Notice of Suspension No. 22-001-101(20) dated June 7, 2022.
observations.reason: The Audit Team issued Notice of Suspension No. 22-001-101(20) dated June 7, 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant set up a SAGF to record the receipt and utilization of the Bayanihan grant in accordance with LBC No. 125 dated April 7, 2020 to facilitate monitoring of the receipt and utilization of the grant.
observations.reason: The Office of the Municipal Accountant had not received funding reports from the fund source which hindered the transfer to the special account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Assessor reconcile their records and coordinate with the Registry of Deeds for the titled lots in the name of the Municipality. We further recommended that registration and transfer of title of the land owned by the Municipality be prioritized to fully protect its interest.
observations.reason: The Management had not given a reason for the non-implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit a Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances supported with (a) Proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries and (b) Inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any, in accordance with Section 1 of COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020.
observations.reason: The Management had not submitted the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant capitalize all major repairs or improvements of assets in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles and COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 to present fairly the financial statements of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Major repairs for CY 2019 totaling ₱6,808,988.83 had not yet been capitalized nor included in the PPE schedule.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management return immediately to the source agency the amount representing the full settlement of the audit disallowance on the excessive use of the transferred funds in accordance with Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.
observations.reason: The amount was still included in the “Due to NGAs” account under Trust Fund. Management still had to coordinate with the source agency for the transfer and return of the funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable data on the Municipality’s financial position as of a given period of time.
observations.reason: No adjustments were made to reflect the appropriate and correct due to/from other fund balances.

observations.recommendation: Immediately organize an Internal Audit Service (IAS) to comply with Section 4 of AO No. 70.
observations.reason: The Municipality had not yet established the IAS.

observations.recommendation: Initiate appropriate measures for recovery of individual records of borrowers. Intensify the collection of KKK loan repayment by issuing individual bills of collection. Initiate relending of said assistance to qualified applicants duly screened by a committee created for that purpose. Make representation with the local law-making body for a possible write-off of accounts deemed uncollectible.
observations.reason: The KKK loans still comprised the Other Receivable account. Beneficiaries were not found in the records. These uncollected accounts will be presented to SB for a possible write-off.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require:

The Municipal Accountant to maintain complete PPELCs and continue tracing back the details of prior years’ acquisitions to support the recorded PPE.

The Property Officer to reconcile the inventory report with the accounting records to verify the details and existence of PPE balances in 2007 and previous years. Also, maintain PCs to facilitate the physical count of PPE items. Furthermore, determine the officials/employees to whom the equipment were issued and issue the corresponding PAR to pinpoint the direct person responsible thereof and renew them every three years to update the information of the end-user of the equipment listed in the inventory report. 

If the existence of PPE in CY 2007 and previous years could not be established despite exhaustion of all diligent efforts, Management may follow the procedures on the disposition for non-existing PPE items for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of government agencies as provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accounting Office had exerted efforts in tracing back the details of prior years’ acquisitions, however, records to support the recorded PPE were still not found on file.

The Property Officer is currently locating the files of previous years’ acquisitions because he only assumed office in January 2020 and there was no proper turnover of records. However, he had started maintaining property cards and issuances of PPE to the end-users during the year were covered with PAR.

Management was not able to comply with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason:
PPE acquired in CY 2007 and previous years remained unsubstantiated. 

Not Implemented

Property cards were not maintained for the PPE acquired in the previous years.

Not Implemented

Reiterated in Observation No. 1, page 39 to 41 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and Supply Officer to reconcile their records and adjust them based on valid documents to reflect the correct balances of the Inventory accounts. Also, require the Supply Officer to prepare the RSMI together with RIS and Certified List of Recipients showing recipients’ signatures vis-à-vis the goods received and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant as her basis in recording the issuances of inventories to the end-users.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee already conducted the physical count of inventories and prepared Monthly RSMI for CY 2021, however, the reports were affected by the massive flooding brought by Typhoon Maring on October 12, 2021. They are trying their best to reconstruct the Monthly RSMI. The Supply/Property Officer also informed the end-user or accountable person to submit the list of recipients for the distribution of inventories to the intended beneficiaries as a basis of the Municipal Accountant to record the issuances of the same.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 2, page 41 to 43 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE immediately relieve the two Revenue Collection Clerks from their duties and responsibilities and reiterate the demand for the immediate remittance of the collections and initiate legal action, if warranted. Moreover, instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer, Liquidating Officer and collectors to perform strictly and observe the diligence of a good father of family in doing their duties and functions in the receipt and verification of collections and accountable forms to ensure the timely and intact turn-over and deposit of collections, thus possible loss or misuse of funds will be avoided.
observations.managementAction: The concerned collectors expressed their willingness to remit the collections.
observations.reason:
To date, the Audit Team is still in the process of verifying and establishing the total cash accountabilities of the Municipal Treasurer as the latter was delayed in the submission of transaction documents. The result of cash examination and any noted deficiencies will be immediately communicated to Management upon completion of the same. 

One of the Revenue Collection Clerks was able to remit his collections amounting to ₱775,511.00 as evidenced by the deposit slip dated June 10, 2022 duly verified by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the concerned officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances to settle immediately their accounts, otherwise, consider the imposition of sanctions such as the withholding of their salaries as provided for in Section 9.3.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

Adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances in order to prevent accumulation of unliquidated cash advances and the possible loss or misuse of government funds.
observations.managementAction:
Cash advances amounting to ₱35,000.00 were liquidated during the year.


Cash advances were still granted to the disbursing officer even if the previous cash advances were not yet liquidated.
observations.reason:
Cash advances totaling ₱1,757,073.00 remained unliquidated as of year-end.


Not Implemented

Reiterated in Observation No. 3, page 43 to 45 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents to facilitate the technical evaluation of the projects and goods and to avoid supension in audit. Oblige the  Municipal Accountant to make sure that all necessary documents pertaining to the procurement of goods and implementation of infrastructrure projects shall be attached to the disbursement voucher before payment is made to establish the validity and propriety of transactions.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the required supporting documents.
observations.reason:
Some claims for CY 2021 were still processed and paid despite the absence of complete documentation.

Reiterated in Observation No. 13, page 62 to 65 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents and justifications on the deficiencies observed for further evaluation of the transactions and avoid suspension in audit. Also, oblige the Municipal Accountant see to it that disbursement vouchers are duly supported with the required basic documents to ensure their validity and propriety.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the required supporting documents.
observations.reason:
Some claims for CY 2021 were still processed and paid despite the absence of complete documentation.

Reiterated in Observation No. 13, page 62 to 65 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management consider the more prudent options without sacrificing efficiency and effectiveness in relation to the specific purpose/s instead of purchasing costly models/brands for the optimum utilization of government funds. We also recommend that the Sangguniang Bayan pass a resolution establishing a clear policy on the provision of gadgets to authorized officials to rationalize disbursements of funds allocated for this purpose.
observations.managementAction: Management was not able to establish a clear policy on the provision of gadgets to authorized officials and employees.
observations.reason: Purchased laptops have different specifications. Moreover, high-end mobile phone amounting to P99,990.00  was procured during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer to cause the immediate submission of the disbursement vouchers and strictly observe the timely submission of transaction documents to facilitate the review thereon and avoid the imposition of sanctions as provided under Section 122 (2) of PD No. 1445 and also enable the issuance of audit results to management which could serve as an aid in management decisions and inputs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office submitted 45 DVs for audit.
observations.reason:
Three DVs pertaining to the transactions for CY 2020 with an aggregate amount P119,584.48 were not yet submitted for audit.

Reiterated in Observation No. 5, page 47 to 49 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to strictly observe the posting of requests for price quotation/proposal and the notices of award together with the contract or purchase order for procurement of goods and services through alternative methods of procurement at any conspicuous place in the premises of the Municipality to ensure interested users’ ready access to information.  Also, require the BAC to post in the PhilGEPS website before issuing request for price quotations/proposals to prospective suppliers/service providers for wider information and to give opportunity to other eligible suppliers/service providers to promote transparency and competition in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: Partially complied with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Some RFQs/RFPs and Notices of Award were not posted at conspicuous place reserved for this purpose in the premises of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to strictly comply with the submission of the APP-CSE in consonance with DBM Circular Letter No. 2013-14 dated November 29, 2013 to ensure the inclusion of useful information for consolidation purposes as a basis in its projection of inventory requirements, scheduling of procurement activities and overall management of the central procurement of common-use goods.
observations.managementAction: The APP-CSE for CY 2021 was submitted to the DBM-PS on February 28, 2022.
observations.reason: Submission was not made within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Facilitate the negotiation with the owners of the lot for the execution of usufruct agreement to ensure the completion of the two partially implemented infrastructure projects and to avoid their deterioration. 



For the two infrastructure projects which remained unimplemented, Management look for other lot owners who are willing to provide the needed site for the implementation of the proposed projects. 




Require the Municipal Engineering Office to conduct all the necessary detailed engineering activities before the conduct of procurement process to avoid the issuance of suspension orders and ensure the timely completion of the projects. Issue only suspension order when necessary to avoid delay in the implementation of infrastructure projects.

Thus, the timely implementation and completion of the projects and the supposed beneficiaries would enjoy the benefits that could have been derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction:
Usufruct agreement was executed for the implementation of the project: Construction of Multi-Purpose Drying Pavement (MPDP) of various barangays amounting to ₱1,996,127.42.


The implementation of the project: Construction of Concrete Deck Girder Bridge amounting to ₱36,479,083.35 is ongoing.



Management was not able to comply with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason:
Negotiation with the owner of the lot for the implementation of the project: Construction of Farm to Market Road and Multi-Purpose Drying Pavement/ Completion of Multi-Purpose Hall amounting to ₱797,911.22 is ongoing.

Not Implemented

Management will exert best effort to convince the lot owner for the immediate implementation of the project: Construction of Multi-Purpose Drying Pavement /PCCP amounting to ₱997,314.40.

Not Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following to Management:

Require the concerned Municipal Office to prepare the Summary/ List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances, and be submitted to the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, through the Office of the Civil Defense, copy furnished the Office of the Auditor; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to record any balances of donations received be costed and recorded in the books or in the registries.

We recommended Management to require and direct  the Municipal Accountant in coordination with municipal officials concerned with the distribution of relief goods for the immediate submission of Delivery Receipt duly acknowledged by the Municipality, Sales Invoice, Summary List of Affected Households/ Families who were given the assistance as certified by the barangay/ municipal official concerned and information in the supporting documents as to the quantity of rice (in kilos or in cavans as the case may be) given to each of the affected family/ household pursuant to Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended Management to require the BAC Secretariat through the BAC Chairman to post in the PhilGEPS website, the website of the Procuring Entity (PE) or its electronic procurement service provider, if any; and any conspicuous place in the premises of the PE for information purposes the aforementioned procurement of foods/ food supplies totaling ₱1,456,900.00 for COVID-19 frontliners pursuant to Annex H, item IV, L (2) of the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184 and GPPB NPM No. 003-2020 and submit proof of posting to the Office of the Auditor.

We also recommended Management to instruct the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the BAC Chairman to immediately submit other lacking documents/ information required in the aforementioned procurement to facilitate validation of the propriety of disbursement made.

We recommended that management of LGU Sta. Maria enforce regularly that all procurement of goods and services be paid by checks directly to suppliers/ contractors/ service providers except for petty disbursements out of petty cash fund in pursuance of COA Circular No. 97-002 and to comply with BIR Revenue Memorandum Order No. 23 – 2014 to avoid liabilities and penalties arising from non-compliance as clearly stipulated in the same BIR Revenue Memorandum Order.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor immediately require the BAC Secretariat to consolidate the Municipality’s PPMPs and to stop the practice of procuring supplies and materials by piecemeal through shopping as alternative mode of procurement and adopt Public Bidding as the primary mode of procurement in compliance with Section 10 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended to the management to limit the utilization of the SEF pertaining to the programs and projects defined under and RA 7160 and Joint Circular (JC) No. 1, s. 2017 of DEPED, DBM and DILG in order to maximize the benefits that the students and teachers could avail under this fund.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management prioritize the request for authority to write off the Other Receivables amounting to P383,673.02 following the guidelines and procedures as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005 to present fairly the affected accounts in the books of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Management accepted the audit observation and committed to prioritize the filing of their request for authority to write-off in 2021.
observations.reason: No request of write off has been filed yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) through the Technical Working Group (TWG) of the LGU conduct the post-qualification following the prescribed procedures to serve its purpose, thus, ensure that all projects are awarded to and undertaken by bidders/contractors which do not only offer the lower or lowest bid but which are also found complying and responsive to all the pertinent requirements and conditions.
observations.reason: As shown in the post-qualification reports for some projects in 2021, the post-qualification procedures were still incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management through the BAC Secretariat submit the purchase orders and government contracts including their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within the period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate the systematic and effective review thereof.
observations.reason: Copies of contracts and POs including supporting documents for various procurements of the LGU were still not submitted to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period. Reiterated in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management revisit the allocation of funds for the Pag-IBIG contributions for its employees, i.e. they may consider setting a fixed or maximum amount of additional government share for all the employees regardless of the amount of their increased contributions for purposes of equity and efficiency.
observations.reason: The Pag-IBIG government contributions in 2020 were based on the personal share of the LGU’s officials and employees.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:

     1.    Re-activate the Inventory and Appraisal  Committee  to  identify
        and  appraise  all  properties  whose  cost  cannot   be   reliably
        determined due to the unavailability of financial records.  Require
        the committee to prepare an Appraisal Report and submit it  to  the
        Commission on Audit for Technical Review and Inspection.


     2. Direct the Inventory Committee  to  dutifully  conduct  the  annual
        physical count of all the property, plant and  equipment  owned  by
        the Municipality and to avail the one-time cleansing of PPE per COA
        Circular No. 2020-06.


     3. Direct the  GSO  and  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  maintain  the
        prescribed Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards,  respectively,  and
        to reconcile these with the RPCPPE to establish the correct  amount
        of PPE in the financial statements.

Recommendation:

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal  Accountant  to
compute  for  the  proper  depreciation  of  the  PPE  items  through   the
preparation  of  a  lapsing  schedule  and  to  subsequently  prepare   the
corresponding adjusting entry.

Recommendations:

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the:

        a) Municipal Accountant to:


           1. Prepare and maintain the Local Road Network  Ledger  Card  for
             the Road and its  components  namely:  a)  road  lot,  b)  road
             pavement, c) drainage and slope protection  structures  and  d)
             other miscellaneous structures;


           2. Prepare and submit the lapsing schedule for the computation of
             the depreciation for each component at the end of the year; and


           3. Disclose the  total  road  network  system  in  the  Notes  to
           Financial
               Statements.


   b) Designated Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to:


           1. Prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of  Local
             Road  Network  and  Report  on  Local  Road  Network  in   the
             prescribed format; and its components.
           2. Maintain a complete Local Road Network Property Card for  all
             roads and their components.


   c) Municipal Accountant and the Designated General Services  Officer  to
      secure from the Provincial Engineer the complete description and cost
         segregation of road components of every road project undertaken by
      the  Municipal  Government for them to have a basis in  recording  the
      road    networks in the Local Road Network Ledger Card and Local Road
         Network Project Card, respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct concerned Municipal officials
to study the guidelines under Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006
and avail of the one-time cleansing of PPEs provided therein and thereafter direct
the:
a) Inventory Committee to –
• prepare and submit the PIP for approval of the Municipal Mayor to ensure that the
conduct of inventory is properly and adequately planned;
• submit a copy of the approved Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) to COA at least ten
(10) calendar days before the scheduled inventory taking activities;
• complete the conduct of physical count of all PPE at least once a year and submit
the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE)
classified per category/type to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31 of
each year; and
• follow the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of inventory prescribed by COA
Circular No. 2020-006.
b) MGSO to update the PCs based on the result of the physical inventory and maintain
them per category and with complete information about the PPE;
c) Municipal Accountant to maintain PPELCs for individual property items complete
with details of the PPE; and
d) MGSO and Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of their respective
records of PPE.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. instruct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from processing the grant of
additional cash advances to officers and employees unless their previous
cash advances have been accounted and settled;
b. require all accountable officers to immediately liquidate their respective cash
advances as soon as the purposes for which the advances were granted has
been served by submitting the required Liquidation Reports and supporting
documents to the Accounting Office; and
c. Henceforth, comply strictly with COA Circular No. 97-002 in the granting,
utilization, and liquidation of cash advances.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendation that the Municipal Mayor
direct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the filing of request for authority to
write off the dormant and/or unliquidated cash advances amounting to
P404,467.50 to the Commission on Audit (COA) following the guidelines and
procedures provided by COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
return to the Bureau of Treasury the balances of the fund transfers from various
NGAs that had been dormant for more than five years or the purpose of which
had already been achieved by the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a) Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team for further evaluation all
the Accomplishment Reports on the actual volume of garbage dumped in the
Sanitary Landfill certified correct by the MENRO, the Trip Tickets for Check
Nos. 579360, 579451, 590733, 591018, 600309, 600478 and 600928 relevant to
their respective Billing Statements and the pertinent supporting documents
such as Contractor’s Billing Statement and the trip tickets for Check
No. 600505, to avoid audit suspensions/ disallowances; and,
b) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and other concerned officials to facilitate
the review of the technical aspects of the contracts, detailing the necessary
information in the determination of the reasonableness of the contract
amount, the scope of work of the Contractors, and the supporting documents
to enable the Audit Team to conduct a systematic and effective auditorial
review and technical evaluation of the contracts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and
the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit to the Audit Team for further
evaluation the lacking documents pertaining to the subject expenditures with
total amount of P12,228,182.00 to avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit
and thereafter ensure that all documentary requirements are attached to the
disbursement vouchers prior to payment to establish the validity and propriety of
the claim.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
c) record as inventories the goods procured for distribution/consumption upon
receipt and to recognize expense only when the goods are distributed/issued
to end-users or beneficiaries, in accordance with IPSAS 12 and Section 114 of
the MNGAS for LGUs; and,
d) properly record the purchase and receipt of welfare goods for distribution to
various beneficiaries to Welfare Goods for Distribution account and the
corresponding issuance thereof to Welfare Goods Expense account, in
accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GFPS to:
a) monitor, through its Executive Committee and the TWG, the effective and
efficient implementation of all GAD PPAs and the full utilization of the GAD
funds;
b) submit the GPB to the DILG not later than March 31 as required under
4.1.C.5.3 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01; and
c) prepare the GAD AR in accordance with the format provided in Annex E of
PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:

a.  Instruct the MSWDO to take cognizance with the guidelines issued by the regulatory agencies concerned which affect the operations of the MSWD Office through periodic viewing of the portal of these agencies or through other activities that may be deemed appropriate; and,

b. Require the MSWDO and the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Office of the Auditor the: (a) Memorandum of Agreement between the DSWD and the Municipality; (b) OSCA Identification of the individuals identified; and, (c) Photographs of beneficiaries during the actual receipt of the assistance.
observations.managementAction:
The Office of the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office of LGU Sta. Maria received an audit observation with noted deficiencies of having no OSCA Identification attached in the payroll of 6 beneficiaries and no photo of the beneficiary during the actual receipt of the Social Pension.

The OSCA and MSWDO strictly observe the proper attachment during the pay out of the Social Pension. Each Beneficiary is required to submit photocopy of their ID’s with 3 signatures.

Photos of the beneficiary during actual receipt of the Social Pension were submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:

a.) To address the deficiencies in the documentary requirements Require the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO) and Municipal Accountant to submit the valid identification of individuals who were granted with assistance but has no valid identification attached in the payroll and the photograph of beneficiaries upon receipt of the assistance.

b.) To address the issue of multiple entries in the Payroll Direct the MSWDO to create a task force to validate the qualification of the beneficiaries who have multiple entries in the payroll and to review the entries in the SAC Forms.

c.) To address the issue on the non-submission of the MOA Require the MSWDO to submit a copy MOA from the DSWD and to provide justification as to the delayed submission.
observations.managementAction:
The MSWDO in coordination with DSWD convergence assigned in Sta. Maria, during implementation, strictly observes the proper attachment during the pay out of Social Amelioration. Each beneficiary is required to submit photocopy of their IDs with 3 signatures and being photographed during the receipt of the Social Amelioration Program were submitted last Jan. 25, 2021. Have already validated the qualified beneficiaries.

Already submitted.

Have already submitted the documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:

To comply with the Infrastructure Requirements:

Require the Municipal Health Officer (MHO) and the Municipal Engineer to review the compliance of the existing COVID-19 facilities with the DOH guidelines and to provide proposals for its improvements to fit with the required technical specifications; and,

Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to identify the sources of funds that may be used for the renovation of COVID-19 facilities subject to the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan.

b.) To comply with the Supplies and Other Resources Requirements:
Instruct the MHO to: (a) identify the supplies and other resources needed for the operation of the COVID-19 facilities; and, (b) submit the PPMP to the Bids and Awards Committee intended for the procurement of materials; and,

Request the BAC to facilitate the expeditious procurement of the supplies and other resources needed for the operation of the COVID-19 facilities.

c.) To comply with the Service Requirements:
Coordinate with the Department of Health, through the MHO, in providing for mental health and psychosocial support services (MHPSS) in COVID-19 facilities as required under DOH-DILG JAO No. 2020-001.

d.) To comply with the Staffing Requirements:
Require the MHO to: (a) identify the staffing requirements for the COVID-19 facilities; (b) provide for the estimated funding requirements; and, (c) conduct pre-deployment orientations and basic trainings to the health care providers; and

Instruct the Municipal Human Resource Office with the coordination of the Municipal Health Office to facilitate the expeditious recruitment of staff for the COVID-19 facilities; and,

Request the Department of Health, with the coordination of the Municipal Health Office, for the deployment of deputized doctors and nurses required for the operation of the COVID-19 facility.

e.) To comply with the Reportorial Requirements:
Instruct the COVID-19 facility manager, through the Municipal Health Office to prepare and submit the draft daily brief reports and submit the same to the offices concerned; and

Require the MHO to ensure that registration in the Temporary Treatment and Monitoring Facility (TTMF) Tracker is undertaken; and, ensure that the DDC TTMF Application is updated.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Health Office has identified infrastructures and buildings which they considered as COVID 19 Facilities (School premises, Day Care Centres, Multipurpose Bldgs, ect,) since this pandemic occurred recently, existing facilities were utilized since these facilities ceased operating due to localized lockdowns and no face to face policy.

It have been instructed the MBO to identify the source of funds.

The Municipality of Sta. Maria has provided necessary supplies and equipment to make these existing facilities usable as COVID 19 facilities. Already requested the BAC.

It have been coordinated with the MHO.

The Municipality of Sta. Maria have hired and train additional manpower to facilitate contact tracing, as well as additional health workers and personnel to facilitate prevention and rehabilitation of COVID 19 patients.

Have already facilitated the expeditious recruitment of Staff.

Submitted the daily brief reports and subkit the same to the offices concerned.

The DDC TTMF Application is updated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Health Officer to direct the MESU, Local Contact Tracing Team and BHERTs to observe the timely submission of daily reports and to establish clear delegation of tasks and responsibilities in accordance with existing guidelines.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Health Office has directed and required BHEARTs for timely submission of required reports to update and quantify the number of positive COVID 19 Infections, rate of infections as well as rate of recovery.

The Municipality of Sta. Maria has passed resolution and ordinance on COVID 19 Protocols to have social distancing and requiring the constituents to always wear face masks when outside their houses and in public places.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:

a. Direct the members of the Local COVID-19 Task Force to create a centralized information and recording committee which will take charge in the pooling of information, reports and documentation relating to the activities undertaken to address the pandemic; and,

b. Require the members of the Local COVID-19 Task Force to provide the documents pertaining to the implementation of the mandatory wearing of face masks and other personal protective equipment.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality of Sta. Maria has passed resolution and ordinance on COVID 19 Protocols to have social distancing and requiring the constituents to always wear face masks when outside their houses and in public places.

Already provided the documents pertaining to the implementation of the mandatory wearing of face masks and other PPE.

Formulated a COVID-19 Waste Management Plan and to incorporate the same in the Local Solid Waste Management Plan; COVID-19 Waste Management Plan implementation in barangay levels and local is satisfactorily determined.

The MENRO to conduct monitoring on each barangay to ensure that COVID-19 related health care wastes are segregated at the household level; Have been requested and ready to comply.

Provided the documentation on the segregation and storage activities for reference and archiving.

Formulated the policies on the collection and transport of COVID-19 related health care wastes; The MENDO have manage the collection and transport of COVID-19 related health waste.

Already Conducted an orientation to waste collectors on the proper handling of COVID-19 related health waste; and,

Provide documentation on collection and transportation activities for reference and archiving.

The Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) has provided policies and were approved thru resolution of the Sangguniang Bayan to properly handle wastes on COVID 19 related,

The MENRO and MHO conducted an orientation to waste disposal personnel on the classification of wastes being delivered for precautionary measures, maintaining a safe distance from, and avoid direct contact with the wastes; Instruct the MENRO and Municipal Engineer to provide for the specifications and estimates in the construction of septic vaults for the treatment and disposal of domestic COVID-19 related health care wastes; Require the MENRO to institute a recording mechanism that will monitor the treatment and disposal of COVID-19 related health care wastes; and,

Provide documentation on the activities related to the treatment and disposal activities for reference and archiving.

The Municipality of Sta. Maria thru Memorandum Order has created the Local Solid Waste Management Board (LSWMB) to revise and integrate the COVID 19 waste management plan in the 10 year Solid Waste Management Plan.

The MENRO upon providing concrete policy on its waste management on COVID 19 has conducted public announcement to different barangays to inform and educate locals and officials.

The MENRO has conducted orientation and other information drive about the proper management of COVID-19 related wastes in barangays.

Require the MENRO and the Composite Team to devise activities to support the Barangay Ecological Solid Waste Management Committees in the proper handling of COVID-19 related health care wastes in barangays.

The MENRO has prepared a specific Occupational Health and Safety Plan duly considering the risks faced by each department and has conducted orientation on the proper usage and maintenance of PPEs and trainings on Occupational Health and Safety with emphasis on the hazards in the workplace, environmental and administrative controls and the properties and characteristics of PPEs.

The MENRO provided disinfectants and hand sanitizers for every waste collection vehicle; and,

Provided documentation of activities on Occupational Health and Safety Plan implementation for reference and archiving.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the Sangguniang Bayan to identify the benefits that may be given to employees, if warranted, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer and to provide the benefits due to them subject to the financial capability of the local government unit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality of Sta. Maria has provided the HAZARD PAY to those employees who are physically reported in the office during the declaration of ECQ on the municipality in the accordance with memorandum issued by the office of the President signed by PRRD.

observations.recommendation: To sustain the successful implementation of the program, we recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Agriculturist to: (a) conduct regular monitoring of the beneficiaries and provide them continuous assistance; and, (b) Devise programs that will benefit the agriculture sector of the local government unit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality of Sta. Maria conducts regular monitoring of the beneficiaries and provide continuous assistance and is implemented and provide Devise programs that will benefit the agriculture sector of the local government unit.

The office of the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office of LGU Sta. Maria received an audit observation with noted deficiency of having no halal specifications in the Procurement for food relief. The Local Government of Sta. Maria has given resulted from Mayor’s consultation with the Punong Barangays including the Muslim Community Captain.

Furthermore, list of families in the Muslim areas are already in the data base of the MSWDO. This is also submitted in the Mayor’s Office.

The Office of the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office of LGU Sta. Maria received an audit observation with noted deficiency of having neglected the needs of persons with disabilities in food relief distribution.

The local Government of Sta. Maria has given relief assistance to the entire municipality. All households are enlisted to receive the said relief. We get the list of the head of the Family and provided relief for each household. However the template has not specifically identified if there are members who are differently abled. But nevertheless the relief operation was saturated to all families that include the persons with disabilities. Furthermore the MSWDO was able to give relief specifically for Persons with Disability with the source augmentation coming from DSWD FO XI. We served 159 PWD’s/ beneficiaries that is intended for PWD alone during the time of pandemic.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:

a. Require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit copies of the following: Mayor’s Permit or Business Permit of the Supplier; Notice to Proceed; and, Notice of Award;

b. Direct the BAC to review mechanisms in-place to ensure that documents submitted to the Accounting Office are complete; and,

c. Require the Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal General Services Officer and the Municipal Engineer to submit justification as to why the payment of the supplies was made even though the inspection and acceptance report indicated that delivery was made on a later date.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Government of Sta. Maria has considered the recommendation of the Resident Auditor and has implemented this recommendation prospectively.

The Municipal Accountant has provided details and further elaboration to the financial statements to provide users and stakeholders more comprehensive understanding to the Municipal Operations and performance on its finances.

The Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal General Services Officer and the Municipal Engineer submitted justification as to why the payment of the supplies was made even though the inspection and acceptance report indicated that delivery was made on a later date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances by reviewing the journal entries made in the transactions for each account and to subsequently prepare the corresponding adjusting entry.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has already adjusted in the books to correct the erroneous entry.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances by reviewing the journal entries made in the transactions for each account and to subsequently prepare the corresponding adjusting entry.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has already adjusted in the books to correct the erroneous entry.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:

a. Direct the Municipal Engineer (MEO) to provide for the estimated recoverable service cost of the damaged office building to the Accounting Office; and,

b. Require the Municipal Accountant to compute for the impairment loss upon the submission of the estimates from the MEO and subsequently prepare for the necessary adjusting entries.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer is still in the process of evaluating the damages incurred during the earthquake on our municipal bldg., until such time that the total damage can be fully determine quantifiably the municipal accountant shall not affect any adjustment thereof.

The Municipal GSO and Municipal Accounting Office is reconciling the PPE – Inventory per year on the date of purchase or full construction, due to other pressing matters, the reconciliation is yet to be done.

The MGSO to maintain Property Cards.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:

a. Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; and,

b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize the cost of each component of the local roads with identified cost and subsequently provide the proper depreciation thereof in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer is still evaluating roads under the municipality and the Municipal Accountant and GSO is still in reconciliation of Road Accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:

a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounting records with property records of the General Services Office; and,

b. Require the Municipal Accountant to undertake the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the result of reconciliation with the GSO.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is still evaluating the account details to properly close this account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:

a. Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical inventory count to determine the existence of the property, plant and equipment for the period; and,

b. Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer (GSO) to reconcile their records based on the result of the physical inventory count.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal GSO and Municipal Accounting Office is reconciling the PPE – Inventory per year on the date of purchase or full construction, due to other pressing matters, the reconciliation is yet to be done.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to provide for the required disclosures under the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards in the preparation of the Notes to the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has provided for the required disclosures under the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards in the preparation of the Notes to the Financial Statements and provided details and further elaboration to the financial statements to provide users and stakeholders more comprehensive understanding to the Municipal Operations and performance on its finances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the Sangguniang Bayan to issue a resolution authorizing him, in his capacity as Local Chief Executive, to enter into agreements/contracts during the state of national health emergency.
observations.managementAction: The Sanguniang Bayan has granted the Municipal Mayor the Municipal Mayor to enter into contract thru resolution.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:

a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to observe the sending of notifications to the payees, at least one month before the check becomes stale, informing them about the existence of such checks; and,

b. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the checks issued.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant is awaiting for the advice of Municipal Treasurer to adjust books for the staled checks, provided with proof of checks staled.

The Municipal Treasurer has assigned person responsible informing clients with their checks.
The Municipal Engineering’s Office has implemented the unutilized portion of the 20% development fund on the succeeding period.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to review the monthly computation and remittance of the Retirement and Life Insurance Premiums (50103010) account to ensure compliance with existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: The AAO has updated records of the GSIS as against the records in the books to accurately reflect the GSIS premium and remit the same.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to provide for the required disclosures under the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards in the preparation of the Notes to the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has provided for the required disclosures under the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards in the preparation of the Notes to the Financial Statements and provided details and further elaboration to the financial statements to provide users and stakeholders more comprehensive understanding to the Municipal Operations and performance on its finances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the offices concerned to prioritize the construction of the Sanitary Landfill; Direct the individuals concerned to prepare and submit to the Office of the Audit Team Leader a copy of the approved Solid Waste Management Plan and observe the submission of Utilization Reports within the prescribed period; and, Require the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to monitor the strict adherence of offices concerned with the provisions of the R.A. No. 9003 in the operation and maintenance of the sanitary landfill.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO has proposed alternative method in managing wastes of the municipality. The MEO is currently constructing the proposed facility in lieu of sanitary land fill.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue an Executive Order creating a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation Team pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has Issued GAD Focal Point System in which the MPDO has been task to spear head this team.

The GAD TWG has provided programs that promote and support breast feeding of infant such as having a breast feeding room or space inside the municipal bldg.., and requiring the health workers to promote breast feeding by informing lactating mother to avoid infant formulas.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year's recommendation and Management agreed
that the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant, and the Municipal Assessor
reconcile their records and coordinate with the Registry of Deeds for the titled lots
in the name of the Municipality.
We further recommended and the Management agreed that the registration
and transfer of titles of the land owned by the Municipality be prioritized to fully
protect its interest.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a) Require the Inventory Committee to properly fill out the RPCPPE; and
b) Office of the Municipal Accountant to

a. Collaborate with the PPE Inventory Committee to ensure that all
PPEs included in the RCPPE are duly reconciled with the
accounting records;
b. Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer in tracing the
discrepancies and make the necessary adjustment under the
pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January
31, 2020; and
c. Reconcile the accounts with negative balances and effect the
necessary adjustment.

We reiterated the previous year’s recommendation and Management agreed
that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer, secure all stale checks, and
determine those requiring the issuance of replacement checks;
b. Regularly monitor all outstanding checks, send written notices to payees a
month before these become stale, and prepare the necessary adjustments;
and
c. Prepare the necessary adjustments for the long outstanding reconciling
item amounting to ₱1,700.24.

We recommended further and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer
cancel the stale checks and furnish the Accounting Office with the original
disbursement vouchers as the basis for the preparation of adjusting entry.

We reiterated the prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed to:
(a) Require all concerned accountable officers to immediately settle their
cash advances in accordance with the pertinent sections of COA
Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997;
(b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor and strictly enforce the
liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period, and see to it
that no additional cash advance shall be allowed unless the previous ones
are settled or properly accounted for; and
(c) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular evaluation/
validation of all outstanding accounts; send demand letters to officers
and employees with long outstanding accounts, and impose sanctions
when necessary, such as withholding of their salaries as provided in the
Circular; and, if warranted, request for a write-off of long-overdue cash
advances following the procedures under COA Circular No. 2016-005
dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the suppliers to
submit the Mayor's/Business Permit and OSS in compliance with Section 4.1 of
GPPB Circular 01-2020.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the personnel
concerned or the end-user duly authorized by the Municipal Mayor, as Head of the
Procuring Entity, to fill out the relevant and required information in the PO
pursuant to Item B of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Stop the practice of recording purchases of medical and food supplies to the
related expense accounts and henceforth, record purchases and utilization
thereof in accordance with Sections 114 and 121 of the Manual on NGAS for
LGUs, Volume I;
b. Require the requisitioning office needing supplies and materials to prepare
and submit the RIS to the Municipal Treasurer on a weekly basis;
c. Require the Municipal treasurer to consolidate on the SSMI all RIS and
submit the same to the Accountant weekly; and
d. Require the Accountant to record to the expense accounts the supplies
consumed on the basis of the SSMI and to maintain perpetual inventory
records to be reconciled periodically with the records of GSO, in order to
fairly present the expense and inventory accounts in the financial statements.
e. Require the LDRRMO to submit to the Audit Team the original copies of the
Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) and Relief Distribution Slips (RDS) for the
subject antigen tests kits and relief goods, respectively.

We further recommended and Management agreed that in the distribution of
welfare goods, Management indicate all the necessary information in the
distribution sheet, particularly the specific goods and quantity of items distributed,
as well as the date of receipt, before causing the beneficiaries to sign in the
acknowledgment section thereof, to establish accountability properly and account
for the goods procured.

We recommended and the Management agreed to refrain from using the
LDRRMF for programs, projects, and activities not directly related to pre-disaster
preparedness, mitigation, response, and rescue operation and, henceforth, observe
strictly the provisions of NDRRMC, DBM, and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular
No. 2013-01 dated March 25, 2013, and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73
dated April 17, 2012, on the proper LDRRMF utilization.

We recommended and Management agreed to replenish the portion of the
20% DF inappropriately disbursed by appropriating from the General Fund, and
henceforth, comply with the guidelines set forth in the DILG and DBM JMC No.
2017-1 dated February 22, 2017, as well as the limitations on the use of the 20%
Development Fund outlined under the DBM, DOF, and DILG JMC No. 1, s. 2020
dated November 4, 2020.

We recommended that Management stop collecting fees from procurement
activities that are not in consonance with the provisions of DBM BC No. 2007-3
dated November 9, 2007, and GPPB Resolution No. 04-2012 dated February 24,
2012.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Inventory Committee to follow the guidelines and procedures as provided for in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 in the conduct of physical count of PPE, recognition of those found at station and disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts of the Municipality to establish the existence, condition and accuracy of PPE account balances. For the missing PPE items, require the end-user or accountable officer concerned to submit immediately to the Auditor concerned an application for request for relief from property accountability duly supported with the requisite documents as provided under COA Memorandum No. 92-751 dated February 24, 1992 to facilitate or ensure its evaluation and resolution.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of inventories semestrally and submit the corresponding report to the Auditor not later July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively, for verification. Also, require the Supply Officer to coordinate with the implementing offices/Department Heads for the submission of the RIS and Certified List of Recipients to the Municipal Accountant as her basis in recording the issuances of inventories to the end-users in order to present the correct balances of the inventory accounts.

We recommended that the LCE oblige the concerned officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances to submit immediately the liquidation report and supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books of accounts and submit the same to the Auditor concerned for audit and verification purposes; failure to comply, consider the imposition of sanctions such as the withholding of their salaries as provided for in Section 9.3.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002. We further recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances in order to prevent accumulation of unliquidated cash advances and the possible loss or misuse of government funds.

We recommended that the LCE require the concerned offices to take appropriate actions to enforce the collection of the overdue loans. We further recommended that Management remain resolute in implementing the provisions of the Memorandum of Agreement especially the penalty clause to avoid the continuing accumulation of outstanding receivables. Likewise, we recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to provide allowance for impairment on the estimated percentage of receivable that were expected to be uncollectible in order to fairly present affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the LCE oblige the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of the DVs including the cancelled checks for audit and verification purposes. For the DVs affected by the massive flooding brought by Typhoon Maring, require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer in coordination with the Implementing Offices to exert best efforts in the reconstruction of the transaction documents for submission to the Auditor concerned, thus Management will be immediately informed of any deficiencies noted in the audit of the transactions. Further, Management strictly observe the timely submission of transaction documents to facilitate the review thereon and avoid further audit action as provided under Section 122 (2) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to secure valid official receipts for the abovementioned transactions to properly substantiate the payments made to the contractors. Further, require the Municipal Treasurer to thoroughly examine the contents/details of the official receipts issued by suppliers/contractors before accepting the same, thus contractors are reminded of their obligations as required under BIR Regulations No. 18-2012 dated October 22, 2012 so that payments made to them will be properly receipted/substantiated notwithstanding the accurate taxes due to the BIR.

We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to adopt the required procedures and conditions in the Post-Qualification stage particularly in the verification, validation and ascertainment of all statements made and documents submitted by the bidder with the Lowest Calculated Bid to ensure that they are in accordance with the requirements as stated in the Bidding Documents, thus eligible bidders would ultimately be awarded the contract.

We recommended that the LCE oblige the BAC to require from the winning bidders the submission of the CHSP duly approved by the DOLE which shall form part of the contracts for the implementation of infrastructure projects to ensure the protection and welfare of workers and the general public within and around the immediate vicinity of the construction worksite.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Implementing Offices to formulate their respective PPMPs showing the specific program/project/activity to be implemented and provide the necessary information needed to facilitate the consolidation of the same into APP to ensure transparency and provide a good basis for monitoring the implementation of the procurement activities.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Treasurer to verify the completeness of the RCD of each Collector by checking the statement of accountable forms as to initial balances on hand, receipts, issues and the ending balances on hand and make a physical count of the accountable forms remaining in the custody of the collector/teller to ensure that the accountable forms under the custody of the collectors and collections received are intact. Moreover, require the Municipal Treasurer to consolidate monthly the individual RAAF of the accountable officers and submit the same to the Auditor Concerned to facilitate the verification of accountability for accountable forms under their custody, thus any discrepancy will be communicated to Management for immediate and appropriate action.

We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to comply with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 regarding the submission of copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders with their corresponding supporting documents to the Auditor within five (5) working days from execution and issuance thereof for review and evaluation so that management will be immediately informed of any defects and deficiencies noted and to facilitate the inspection of completed projects and delivered of goods.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the BAC Chairman to follow the prescribed rates in the computation of honoraria based on the successfully completed projects and consider the involvement of every member of the BAC and TWG in the procurement activities. Moreover, require the Municipal Accountant to review the correctness of said claims to avoid overpayment and consequently disallowance in audit.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents and justifications on the deficiencies observed for further evaluation of the transactions and avoid suspension in audit. Also, oblige the Municipal Accountant see to it that disbursement vouchers are duly supported with the required basic documents to ensure their validity and propriety.

We recommended that the LCE instruct:
The Municipal Treasurer to use separate set of official receipts to acknowledge the receipt of collections from the operations of each economic enterprise and prepare the corresponding RCD daily. Moreover, maintain separate cashbook in recording the transactions for each LEE.
The Municipal Accounting Office to maintain complete subsidiary ledger for each special account under the General Fund and prepare the corresponding financial statements to form part as attachments of the financial statements of the General Fund to serve as a tool in informing the Management on whether the income generated are sufficient to meet their operating costs and enable Management to evaluate them for the purpose of recommending corrective measures for improvement of the LEEs.

We recommended that the LCE require the concerned implementing offices the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise tax in order to give the constituents, especially the tobacco farmers the early benefits that could be derived therefrom. We further recommended that the LCE advise the Local Development Council and Municipal Accountant to determine the total unexpended balances of the completed and unimplemented projects and inform the LCE for possible reappropriation and the latter to request Sangguniang Bayan to prepare resolution for the reappropriation of the unused funds in accordance with the authorized uses as prescribed in RA Nos. 7171 and 8240 and ensure that only feasible programs/projects/activities are included in the Annual Investment Program and require regular monitoring of implementation as basis in taking appropriate action.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the implementing offices to coordinate and strategize the implementation of all the programs/projects embodied under the 20% Development Fund to attain desirable socio-economic targets and outcomes for the Municipality and intended beneficiaries. Moreover, require them to review the composition of the 20% Development Fund, identify the savings from fully implemented projects and those which are no longer prioritized and, subject to the provisions of the Local Government Code and the DILG-DBM JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020, reappropriate to current/existing priority development projects.

We recommended that the LCE require the GAD Focal Person to formulate feasible programs/projects/activities in order to fully address gender issues/problems of the constituents in the municipal level and to help the national government attain its goal of addressing gender issues in the country.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant comply with the regular submission of the mandated BRS and their supporting documents for all depository accounts maintained by the Municipality within ten days after the end of each month in compliance with COA Circular No. 96-001.

In addition, the following is the suggested pro-forma adjusting entry:
For Check cancellation:
Cash in Bank-LCCA									xx
Accounts Payable									xx
For Replacement:
Accounts Payable									xx
Cash in Bank-LCCA									xx

We have recommended Management to prepare a Reconciliation between actual amounts on a comparable basis as presented in SCBAA and in the Statement of Financial Performance for the Year Ended December 31, 2021. Entity discrepancies must be disclosed to the Notes to Financial Statements as required by IPSAS 1 to achieve the objectives of general-purpose financial reporting in the public sector.
