We have recommended that the Head of the Agency create Inventory Committee to conduct and complete the physical inventory and warrant full compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 to establish the existence, condition and accountability assertion of the Municipality on Property, Plant and Equipment in the Financial Statements.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant to:
Record inventory using the prescribed perpetual method of accounting on all purchased supplies and use inventory tags or stock cards for monitoring.
Conduct regular reconciliation of the ledger and the stock cards to ensure that all purchases and issuances are properly accounted for.  With this suggested course of action, the Municipality have the assurance that inventories are safeguarded against risk of misapplication or misuse and ensure the reliability and accuracy of the accounts presented in the financial statements.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor together with the Bids and Awards Committee adopt the Competitive Bidding as primary mode in the procurement of birthday cakes to senior citizens in compliance to 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to ensure transparency that the most advantageous price was obtained.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile and effect necessary adjustment in the books. Follow the rules and regulations set forth by IPSAS No. 1 of recording expenses to the year it was incurred to avoid errors in the financial records.

We have recommended that the Management thru the Municipal Accountant reconcile their financial records and reports on the DRRM funds. They shall maintain a separate Subsidiary Ledger for transfers of the LGU’s unutilized DRRM to the special trust fund to lessen incurrence of errors and always disclose to the Notes to Financial Statements the amount and details of the unexpended balance using the required format to establish faithful representation of accounts as mandatory requirement by IPSAS 1.

We have recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity direct the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report approved by him and submit to the GPPB in compliance with Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We have recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) formulate ordinances to carry out the collections and issuance of Occupancy Permit as one of the requirements of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of the National Building Code of the Philippines (NBCP) to avoid non-collection of income that should have been earned by the Municipality.

We have recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan formulate ordinances to carry out the requirements of RA No. 9003 on fines and penalties in accordance with the requirements of RA No. 9003 or “Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000” to strengthen and address all environmental issues for the protection of public health and environment.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Accountant, Municipal Treasurer to and HRMO:
Submit the basic documents enumerated to ensure legality and propriety of the transactions;
Require submission of the accomplishment report and DTRs of job order/contractual employees before the preparation of payroll and payment of services to ensure validity of claims; and
Instruct the HRMO to monitor and validate actual rendition of services.

We have recommended that the local government unit strengthen a GAD Focal Point System who will coordinate, guide and monitor the development and implementation of the agency’s GAD Plan and GAD-related programs, projects and activities, monitor its implementation as well as the submission of accomplishment report as required in Section 4 of Joint Circular No. 2012-01 to deliver fully the benefits of gender and development to the constituents specially women and children.

We have recommended that Management of LGU Sta. Maria enforce regularly that all procurement of goods and services be paid by checks directly to suppliers/ contractors/ service providers except for petty disbursements out of petty cash fund in pursuance of COA Circular No. 97-002 and to comply with BIR Revenue Memorandum Order No. 23 – 2014 to avoid liabilities and penalties arising from non-compliance as clearly stipulated in the same BIR Revenue Memorandum Order. The use of revolving fund is also discouraged.

We have reiterated that the Municipal Mayor immediately require the BAC Secretariat to consolidate the Municipality’s PPMPs and to stop the practice of procuring supplies and materials by piecemeal through shopping as alternative mode of procurement and adopt Competitive Bidding as the primary mode of procurement in compliance with Section 10 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We have recommended the Management to:
Require the procurement officer and end -users to fully accomplish all relevant entries in the purchase request and purchase orders to provide verifiable information to all users of the documents; and
Cause the refund of the excessive amount immediately to avoid the issuance of a disallowance.

We have recommended Management to ensure that all expenses are duly authorized, adequately funded and documented; and to allow expenditures only within the limit of the appropriation pursuant to the provisions of PD No. 1177.

We have recommended Management to:
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the basic documents for payroll/ salaries and wages enumerated under COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Section 191 of GAAM Volume I to ensure legality and propriety of the transactions;
Require the HRMO the submission of employees’ DTRs before the preparation of payroll and payment of services to ensure validity of claims; and
Instruct the HRMO to monitor and validate actual rendition of services of employees

We have recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit all the aforementioned supporting documents to establish propriety of the claims.  For succeeding transactions of the same nature, he shall see to it that supporting documents are complete before certifying the Disbursement Voucher and effecting payment. Strict compliance with the provisions of the law, rules and regulations pertaining to documentation be always adhered for all claims against government funds.

In order to forestall the issuance of Notice of Suspension and/ or Notice of Disallowance, we have recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the submission of documents required under item 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and other documents deemed necessary to transfer the legal ownership of the paid lot to the name of the Municipality.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit documents establishing the identities of the signatories and witnesses in the Contract, payee in the Disbursement Voucher and in the Deed of Absolute Sale as these documents will also be needed by the Municipality when it would process documents for the BIR Certificate Authorizing Registration and Land Registration Authority.   Management shall also ensure that all documents are duly signed by appropriate signatories.

We have recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to immediately comply within the time frame and the required documentary requirements of the paid Disbursement Vouchers to the Auditor for verification and timely conduct of audit as required in Section 100 of PD No. 1445 and Section 7.2.1.a of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 to avoid the issuances of Notice of Suspension and/or Disallowance.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Ensure that all Municipal-owned vehicles are regularly and promptly registered with LTO;
Renewals of registration of all serviceable vehicles be made within the prescribed period to avoid additional cost for fines and penalties; and
Instruct the Property Officer to register and transfer the abovementioned vehicle in the name of the Municipality as prescribed.

We have recommended the Management that the amount of ₱40,000.00 be refunded by the concerned Municipal officials and employees. We further recommend that the Management refrain from giving donations for whatever religious or private purposes pursuant to Section 335 of RA No. 7160.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management prioritize  the  request
   for authority to write off the Other Receivables amounting to P383,673.02
   following the guidelines and procedures as prescribed under COA  Circular
   No. 2016-005 to present fairly the affected accounts in the books of  the
   LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare PPE Ledger Cards and
  reconcile the same with the RPCPPE, thus, present the correct balances of
  the PPE accounts in the books of the LGU.  In  addition,  the  Management
  must require the Supply Officer and Municipal Accountant to maintain  the
  Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards and  record  the  acquisition,
  description, custody, disposal and  other  information  relative  to  the
  assets.

  The Supply Officer shall  likewise  religiously  maintain  the  inventory
  count report and reconcile the same with the accounting  records  of  the
  Municipal Accountant on a regular basis.

We recommended that Management submit justification for the hiring  of  a
   cooperative and business  financial  consultant  and  the  functions  and
   responsibilities listed in the contract of service.

We  recommended  that  Management  stop  the  payment  of  representation
   expenses amounting to P1,000.00 each to the department heads  on  top  of
   their regular representation allowance (RA). Also, considering  that  the
   payments already made were deemed irregular, the representation  expenses
   from January to April 2021 in the amount of P4,000.00 per department head
   as well as payments made thereafter, if any, must be refunded.

We  recommended  that  Management  stop  the  payment  of  representation
   expenses amounting to P1,000.00 each to the members  of  the  legislative
   department on top of their regular representation allowance  (RA).  Also,
   considering that the payments already made  were  deemed  irregular,  the
   representation expenses from January to  April  2021  in  the  amount  of
   P4,000.00 per  legislative  member/official  as  well  as  payments  made
   thereafter, if any, must be refunded.

We  recommended  that  Management  submit  the  lacking   and   necessary
   corrective or  explanatory  documents  and  information  to  support  the
   purchases of medical equipment and  supplies  amounting  to  P332,140.50.
   Should the documents  be  not  submitted  or  found  not  in  order,  the
   corresponding disbursements may be suspended or disallowed in audit.

We  recommended  that  Management  submit  the  lacking   and   necessary
   corrective or  explanatory  documents  and  information  to  support  the
   procurements of various projects costing  P963,400.00  for  verification.
   Should the documents  be  not  submitted  or  found  not  in  order,  the
   corresponding disbursements may be suspended or disallowed in audit.

We  recommended  that  Management  submit  the  lacking   and   necessary
   corrective or  explanatory  documents  and  information  to  support  the
   purchases of various goods for the meals & snacks  of  LSIs,  ROFWs,  and
   frontline workers amounting to P628,965.70. Should the documents  be  not
   submitted or found not in order, the corresponding disbursements  may  be
   suspended or disallowed in audit.

   Moreover, Management may revisit the mode of  procurement  of  goods  for
   COVID 19 purposes and consider the procurement in bulk to ensure  economy
   and efficiency.

We recommended that Management strictly observe  the  provisions  of  the
   Omnibus Rules on Leave, as amended, on  monetization  of  leave  credits.
   With respect to the monetization of 79 days of Mr. Panganiban, Management
   submit justification on the grant thereof, otherwise, the excess  amounts
   may be disallowed in audit.

We  recommended  that  Management  submit  the  lacking   and   necessary
   corrective or  explanatory  documents  and  information  to  support  the
   purchases of assorted goods for use in the isolation  facility  amounting
   to P145,839.40. Should the documents be not submitted  or  found  not  in
   order, the corresponding disbursements may be suspended or disallowed in
   audit.

   Moreover, Management may revisit the mode of  procurement  of  goods  for
   COVID 19 purposes and consider the procurement at one time or in bulk  to
   ensure economy and efficiency.

We recommended that the Municipal Vice Mayor submit a  justification  for
   the hiring of the  84  job  order  workers  for  the  legislative  office
   covering  the  period  August  18  to  October  31,  2021  and  why   the
   corresponding payments of their wages for  the  period  covered  may  not
   disallowed in audit.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management prioritizes the
   filling up of some of its funded vacant plantilla  positions  and  reduce
   the number of job order workers, thus, establish economy, efficiency  and
   effectiveness at work.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the minimum  monthly  net
  take home pay of the officials and employees of the LGU  as  required  by
  law.

We recommended that the Technical Working Group (TWG) of the LGU  conduct
   the post-qualification evaluation following the prescribed procedures  to
   serve its purpose of ensuring that all its projects are  awarded  to  and
   undertaken by bidders/contractors which do not only offer the lowest  bid
   but which are also found complying and responsive to  all  the  pertinent
   requirements and conditions, thus, protect the interest of the  procuring
   entity.   In  this  regard,  Management  submit  a  more  detailed  post-
   qualification evaluation reports for the projects costing  P5,734,780.14,
   if any.

observations.managementAction:
The MGSO commented that the IC is currently conducting a comprehensive physical count of all the property, plant and equipment and when completed the RPCPPE will be prepared and submitted to the Audit Team. His office is working on the completeness of the PCs. He also assured to follow the guidelines and procedures prescribed by COA Circular No. 2020-006 in the conduct of inventory.

Likewise, the Municipal Accountant promised to maintain PPELCs and after the completion of the physical inventory, she will work hand in hand with the MGSO for the reconciliation of the PPE accounts.
observations.reason: RPCPPE was not submitted to the Audit Team as the conducting physical count was not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Acting Municipal Mayor properly record the purchase and receipt of welfare goods for distribution to various beneficiaries to Welfare Goods for Distribution account and the corresponding issuance thereof to Welfare Goods Expense account, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant assured to consistently record the receipts of welfare goods as inventories and recognize expense only when goods are distributed; and record the purchase and receipt of welfare goods for distribution to various beneficiaries to Welfare Goods for Distribution account and the corresponding issuance thereof to Welfare Goods Expense account.
observations.reason: Some welfare goods were directly charged to expense accounts.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous years’ recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the filing of request for authority to write off the dormant and/or unliquidated cash advances amounting to P404,467.50 to the Commission on Audit (COA) following the guidelines and procedures provided by COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant informed the Audit Team that necessary documents will be prepared for the filing of request for write-off to the COA.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant made a letter of inquiry to COA last January 13, 2021. Documents for the filing of request with the COA are being prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Acting Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to inquire from the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) and Bureau of the Treasury (BT) on the proper procedures on how subject dormant funds could be returned to the national government given that pertinent records could not anymore be traced by the Municipality and thereafter return the unexpended balances to the proper national agency.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer sent a letter of inquiry to DBM and BT on June 30, 2021 regarding the unexpended balances of various funds from NGAs.
observations.reason: Pertinent records could not anymore be traced by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Acting Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit the disbursement vouchers, liquidation reports and their supporting documents to the COA to avoid suspension and/or disallowance of the transactions in audit. Henceforth, ensure that all accounts are submitted within the first ten days of the ensuing month pursuant to Section 7.2 of the COA RRSA.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already submitted the disbursement vouchers and liquidation reports and their supporting documents to the COA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Acting Municipal Mayor direct the:
a) Municipal Accountant to submit to COA the Accomplishment Reports on the actual volume of garbage dumped in the Sanitary Landfill certified correct by the MENRO, Trip Tickets relevant to their respective Billing Statements and the pertinent supporting documents; and
b) BAC and other concerned officials to study the legal possibility of amending the contract to clarify scope of work and enable proper auditorial review and technical evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO submitted the Accomplishment Reports on the number of cubic meter of garbage dumped in the Sanitary Landfill certified correct by the MENRO. The Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer submitted justification on the recommendation noted. The Contract for 2021 has already been amended and included sanitation fee.
observations.reason: The Contract for 2021 has already been amended and included sanitation fee.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Acting Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to investigate and account the financial assistance intended for beneficiaries and refund any unaccounted amounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer investigated and accounted the financial assistance intended for 78 beneficiaries amounting to P54,600.00 and refunded the said amount of P26,600.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Acting Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Secretariat to submit to COA the justifications on its inability to comply with the requirement under GPPB Circular No. 01-2020, for further evaluation; and the BAC Secretariat and the Municipal Accountant to submit to COA the Mayor’s/Business Permit, Income/Business Tax Returns and Omnibus Sworn Statement (OSS) for projects with an ABC of P500,000.00, and the Philippine Contractors Accreditation Board (PCAB) license and Net Financial Contracting Capacity (NFCC) of the suppliers/contractors pertaining to the COVID-19 expenditures totaling P57,654,925.49 to avoid issuance of audit suspension.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat submitted a justification letter on its inability to comply with the requirement under the GPPB Circular. The BAC Secretariat and the Municipal Secretariat submitted the requested documents of the Audit Team pertaining to the COVID-19 expenditures.
observations.reason: The BAC Secretariat submitted justification letter on its inability to comply with the requirement under the GPPB Circular. The BAC Secretariat submitted the Mayor’s documents requested by the Audit Team pertaining to the COVID-19 expenditures to avoid issuance of audit suspension.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that in the preparation of the ABC and POW of infrastructure projects, the Municipal Engineer ensure that the basis for computation of the VAT component as well as the rates thereof conform to DPWH Department Order No. 22 to avoid excessive ABCs and contract costs.
observations.managementAction: Subsequent AAR infrastructure projects had already included correct VAT and profit computation in ABC and POW.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management refrain from holding seminars/workshops outside of the province in accordance with pertinent sections of COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012, to avoid extravagant expenditures and wastage of government funds.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2021, PCL seminars/workshops were no longer held outside of the Province.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management adhere to the requirements prescribed under DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 09, series of 2020, in the implementation of the SAP and other similar programs of DSWD to ensure that financial assistance is given to qualified beneficiaries and that the objectives of the program are attained.
observations.managementAction: Thorough validations had already been done to identify the beneficiaries of other similar programs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MDRRMO coordinate with the Punong Barangays for the submission of the list of recipient-beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO submitted listings of the 651 household recipient-beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer strictly observe the PS limitation which shall not exceed 55% of the total annual income from regular sources realized in the next preceding fiscal year pursuant to Section 325(a) of R.A. No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: AAR based on the income for CY 2019 amounted to ₱40,828,924.71 while in CY 2021, the appropriated and actual PS was only ₱40,072,500.00 and ₱34,788,447.00, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC and TWG to prepare and review the checklist in all biddings conducted and ensure that all the official requirements indicated therein are in order to preserve the integrity of the bidding process as mandated under R.A. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Subsequent procurements had already complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer remit taxes withheld from compensation as well as from purchases of goods and services within ten (10) days after the end of each month in accordance with BIR Revenue Regulations No. 2-98 dated April 17, 1998.
observations.managementAction: Taxes due had already been remitted to the BIR.

observations.recommendation: Directed the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to make a quarterly reconciliation of their cash balances so that discrepancies can be adjusted immediately to present accurate and reliable cash balances.
observations.managementAction: The cash balance per GL and Cash Book had already been reconciled.

observations.recommendation: Create the GAD Focal Point System in the municipality in accordance with Section 37, Rule VI of R.A. 9710 to ensure that the programs, projects, and activities in the Municipality are responsive to gender issues and concerns. Direct the designated GAD Focal Person to initiate activities for the establishment of a local GAD code based the gender issues and concerns through consultation with women constituents. Coordinate with the Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) and the Philippine Commission on Women (PCW) in the formulation and development of said Code. Make representation with the local law-making body for the eventual passage and approval of the Local GAD code.
observations.managementAction: The GAD focal point had already been designated.
observations.reason: Management had not formulated the GAD Code.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct (a) the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of inventories and movable properties of the Municipality; (b) the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the report on the results of the count using the prescribed form and reconcile the results with the accounting records; and (c) the Municipal Treasurer, as property custodian, and the Municipal Accountant to maintain stock/property cards and ledgers cards, respectively.
observations.managementAction: The Office of Municipal Treasurer had already prepared stock cards for the newly purchased properties.
observations.reason: The Inventory Committee had not convened for the physical inventory.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer, secure all stale checks, and determine those requiring the issuance of replacement checks. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer cancel the stale checks and furnish the Accounting Office with the original disbursement vouchers as the basis for the preparation of journal entry vouchers debiting the Cash in Bank, and crediting Accounts Payable and Prior Years’ Adjustment for stale checks less than two years and for those more than two years, respectively. We recommended further that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant regularly monitor all outstanding checks, send written notices to payees a month before these become stale and prepare the necessary adjustments for the reconciling items totaling ₱11,041,126.31.
observations.managementAction: Bank Reconciliation Statements had already been submitted to the Audit Team including the financial statements.
observations.reason: Long outstanding reconciling items remained unadjusted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) Require all concerned accountable officers to immediately settle their cash advances in accordance with the pertinent sections of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; (b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor and strictly enforce the liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period, and see to it that no additional cash advance shall be allowed unless the previous ones are settled or properly accounted for; and (c) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular evaluation/ validation of all outstanding accounts; send demand letters to officers and employees with long outstanding accounts, and impose sanctions when necessary, such as withholding of their salaries as provided in the Circular; and if warranted, request for write-off of long-overdue cash advances in accordance with the procedures under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: A considerable portion of prior years’ unliquidated cash advances had been collected which resulted in the current balance of ₱486,294.98.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) on or before the 15th day after the end of each month in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 for proper evaluation and monitoring.
observations.managementAction: Reports were submitted when required.
observations.reason: The Office of the Municipal Accountant had not received reports from the fund source which hindered the transfer to the special account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned LGU official to submit within the reglementary period approved copy of the GAD plan and Budget and ensure that the annual accomplishment report was submitted on time.
observations.managementAction: Management was still in the planning stage and the target submission to DILG was the 30th of September.
observations.reason: Accomplishment Reports had not been submitted.

observations.recommendation: Required the Local School Board to explain and submit the legal basis of including the expense items under the SEF budget. Henceforth, ensure that only those priority expense items as enumerated under Section 4.0 of DEPED/DBM/DILG JMC No. 1 series of 2017 are included in the SEF budget approved and enacted.
observations.managementAction: Per letter received from the District Supervisor, the subject expenditures were allowable charges to the SEF.
observations.reason: The legal basis had not been submitted to substantiate the claims.

observations.recommendation: Cause the immediate refund of the amount of ₱290,000.00 paid to all appointed officials/ employees of the municipality considering that the disbursement of funds was devoid of any legal basis. Henceforth, management shall strictly observe the fifty-five percent (55%) limitation on personal services to prevent disallowances in audit.
observations.managementAction: The payment of the same allowance had already been stopped, and refunds were already started.
observations.reason: The amount refunded was so minimal.

observations.recommendation: Directed the Municipal Accountant to release immediately the barangays’ shares of prior years and succeeding shares on a quarterly basis within five (5) days after the end of each quarter.
observations.managementAction: RPT shares had already been remitted to the respective barangays.
observations.reason: There were still barangays with unremitted shares which amount to ₱21,335.05.

observations.recommendation: Directed all those who received the PEI to refund the amount of ₱10,000.00 each, the payment of which is not legally due them, the PS limitation provided under Section 325(a) of RA 7160, having been not observed by the municipality. Henceforth, management shall strictly observe the fifty-five percent (55%) limitation on personal services to prevent disallowances in audit.
observations.managementAction: Repayments had been deducted from the payroll.
observations.reason: There were still unsettled disallowances that apparently belonged to former employees who were either deceased or separated from the service.

observations.recommendation: Directed all persons held liable therefore to immediately and fully settle the disallowances in audit pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006 to enable the Municipality to utilize the funds for its priority development projects.
observations.managementAction: The grant of the allowance had already been stopped, and partial settlements were already made.
observations.reason: There were still unsettled disallowances that apparently belonged to former employees who were either deceased or separated from the service.

observations.recommendation: Instructed the Supply Officer designee to furnish the auditor with the approved purchase orders and notify her of the deliveries of supplies within 24 hours from issuance of such purchase orders and acceptance of deliveries, so that surprise and selective inspection of the deliveries be made.
observations.managementAction: The PO had already been submitted as an attachment to Disbursement Vouchers.
observations.reason: The submission was not made within the required period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to prepare the necessary transfer of funds and accounting reports to facilitate the said reversion and certification as to the availability of said funds for subsequent reappropriation for other social services.
observations.managementAction: Complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the DTRs and the required attachments to avoid suspension in audit. Likewise, ensure that succeeding payments of salaries and wages are supported with said documents and submit the same to the Auditor within the prescribed period to facilitate the audit thereon.
observations.managementAction:
Management submitted the DTRs and the required attachments.

Management is now applying the “No DTR, No Salary” policy to avoid the delayed submission of DTRs and attachments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management through the Municipal Accountant and MSWDO, cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents and justifications on the deficiencies noted in support of the validity and propriety of the transactions and to avoid suspension in audit. Instruct the MSWDO to review and verify carefully the completeness and authenticity of the eligibility requirements of the proposed beneficiaries to ensure that the social amelioration programs of the national government are granted to the qualified individuals. Moreover, see to it that the beneficiaries as listed in the payroll are based on the DSWD-generated list so as not to deprive the identified beneficiaries of the needed assistance.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO submitted the lacking documents and justifications on the observed deficiencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Supply Officer to notify the Auditor or to furnish her copies of delivery documents within 24 hours from acceptance of delivered items for inspection purposes to ensure that delivered and accepted items agree with the quantity, quality and specifications as ordered by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Supply/Property Officer notified the Audit Team of the scheduled deliveries of goods for distribution.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer to observe closer monitoring and evaluation of all ongoing infrastructure projects to ensure the completion of the projects within the specified contract duration. Moreover, we recommended that LCE through the Municipal Engineer observe and implement the calibrated measures for contracts with negative slippage during the contract duration as provided under GPPB Circular No. 03-2019 dated March 8, 2019 to ensure the timely implementation of the infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer already conducted close monitoring and evaluation of all ongoing infrastructure projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the procuring entity strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 to ensure transparency and accountability in the procurement process. We specifically recommended that Management instruct: 

The BAC comply with the submission of the Final APP to the GPPB on or before the end of January of the budget year and it shall be posted in the GPPB Website to ensure transparency in the procurement process and in the implementation of procurement contracts.

The BAC comply with the posting requirement of the Approved Bidding Documents to ensure timely dissemination of the procurement opportunity.

The BAC conduct the Pre-Bid Conference within the reasonable period to allow the prospective bidders to adequately prepare their bids.

The BAC invite in writing three observers in all stages of the procurement activities to sit in its proceedings and ensure that the invitation is received within the prescribed period.

The BAC adopt the required procedures and conditions in the Post-Qualification stage particularly in the verification, validation and ascertainment of all statements made and documents submitted by the bidder with the Lowest Calculated Bid to ensure that they are in accordance with the requirements as stated in the Bidding Documents.

The BAC to ensure that the required performance security is posted by the winning bidder before the signing of the contract to guarantee the faithful performance of his obligations under the contract.

The BAC properly disclose in the BAC Resolution Declaring LCRB and Recommending Approval by HOPE the procurement activities short of award to ensure that the contract is awarded upon approval of the appropriation ordinance, as the case may be or upon approval of the list of programs, projects and activities funded out of the RA Nos. 7171 and RA 8240.

The BAC, through its Secretariat, see to it that the Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and a copy of the approved contract were posted in the PhilGEPS website within three (3) and fifteen (15) calendar days from their respective issuances for information and  transparency in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction:
The BAC Secretariat submitted the Final APP for CY 2021 to the GPPB on January 28, 2021.



Posted the bidding documents in the PhilGEPS website.


Pre-Bid Conference was conducted within the prescribed period.


Notices of invitation were sent to the observers.


The BAC was able to validate the documents submitted by the bidder with the Lowest Calculated Bid.











Concerned contractors posted their performance bond before the execution of contract.

Complied with the audit recommendation.








Notices of Award, Notices to Proceed and copies of contracts were posted in the PhilGEPS website.
observations.reason:
Fully Implemented































Not Implemented

The required conditions and procedures in the conduct of post-qualification on the bidders who tendered the Lowest Calculated Bid were not observed. In addition, the TWG failed to include in the Post-Qualification Evaluation Report the result of their validation and verification on some of the technical documents of the bidder.

Reiterated in Observation No. 7, page 50 to 54 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the concerned contractors who undertook the implementation of the infrastructure projects to post a warranty security as prescribed under pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 to guarantee the performance of their responsibilities in case of structural defects and failures from the final acceptance of the infrastructure projects. In addition, require the concerned contractors to secure a certification from the Insurance Company on the validity period of the undated warranty securities.
observations.managementAction: Contractors were required to post their warranty security.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the concerned suppliers to post a warranty security which shall be covered by either retention money in an amount equivalent to at least one percent (1%) of every progress payment, or a special bank guarantee equivalent to at least one percent (1%) of the total contract to guarantee the performance of their responsibilities in case of manufacturing defects after acceptance of the delivered goods.
observations.managementAction: Suppliers were required to post their warranty security.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit Financial Statements that shows valid representation of the effects of cash flows in order to avoid wrong information that may affect the decision-making of the intended users.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted a revised Statement of Cash Flows

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to require and direct  the Municipal Accountant for the immediate submission of  the Disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents pertaining to procurement of  several sacks of rice distributed as relief goods and food assistance amounting to ₱7,768,192.00  to facilitate conduct of validation on the regularity of procurement, propriety of utilization and disbursement required under item 5.0 of DBM LBC No. 125 and completeness of delivery and eventual full distribution to the affected families of COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the Disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents pertaining to procurement of several sacks of rice distributed as relief goods and food assistance

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that the 20% Development Fund are utilized for the purposes intended, as cited in the DILG/DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity direct the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report approved by him and submit to the GPPB in compliance with Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal accountant submitted the PMR thru email to pmr@gppb.gov.ph

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be strict in the granting of Cash Advances and observe those allowable transactions that can be paid thru cash advance pursuant to COA Circular 97-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare and sign the check only on duly approved disbursement vouchers. The provisions of Section 40, Volume I of the Manual on New Government Accounting System pertaining to drawing of checks should be adhered to.

We recommended that Management ensure that travelling expenses are made within the prescribed limits in accordance with EO No. 77.  The municipality should find ways to minimize the cost and optimize the performance of duties of its officers and employees for an effective and efficient organization.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile his records with that of the Municipal Engineer to establish the correct balances of the corresponding PPE accounts and present them fairly in the books of the LGU at any given time.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant accepted the audit observation and committed to reconcile his records with that of the Municipal Engineer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the government contracts and the supporting documents for all its projects to the Office of the Auditor within the period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate the systematic and effective review thereof. In this regard, the corresponding government contracts and supporting documents for the projects totaling P9,936,400.00 be submitted for verification and inspection.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor directed all personnel concerned to cause the immediate submission of the required documents and to observe the timely submission thereof in the LGU’s future projects.
observations.reason: In 2021, some of the infrastructure contracts were not submitted on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize the full payment of the unpaid electric bill to avoid surcharges/penalties and to clear the LGU’s accountability, thus, maintain a positive credit standing.
observations.managementAction: Management accepted the audit observation and committed to prioritize the payment of the unpaid balance in prior years.
observations.reason: Partially paid

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management utilize and liquidate all the funds received from NGAs, GOCCs and LGUs to clear the LGU’s obligations and entitle it to additional fund transfers. If the projects could not be implemented or are no longer relevant and necessary, the LGU may request for the realignment of the fund for other purpose/s, otherwise, the entire amounts should be returned immediately.
observations.managementAction: Management refunded the whole amount of P1,000,000.00 received from ECCD and copy of the corresponding official receipt (OR) was submitted to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: Partially refunded

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management account and report all in-kind donations using the documents, reports and procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 2014-002 to establish transparency and to present fairly the donated goods in the books of the LGU. Correspondingly, the proofs of distribution of the 500 bags of rice must also be submitted for verification.
observations.managementAction: Management accepted the audit observation and committed to account and monitor all succeeding donations in kind from various sources. Also, Management will submit the corresponding distribution list for the 500 bags of rice.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all procurement for the Office of the Legislative of the LGU, being an end-user, must conform strictly with the prescribed procedures and guidelines under the procurement law to establish the validity and propriety of the transactions. Accordingly, the Office of the Legislative submit a justification together with the necessary supporting documents for the procurement of the office tables costing P80,400.00 through direct contracting for verification. Should the documents be not submitted or found not in order, the transaction may be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Vice-Mayor explained that an Invitation to Bid was posted in three conspicuous places by the BAC but no supplier responded. Hence, they resorted to negotiate with Domie’s Furniture Shop which is not related to any officials of the LGU. The tables were delivered and have been already used by the SB members. Nonetheless, he committed that the audit recommendations will be observed in their future acquisitions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the lacking documents and necessary justification/explanation for the deficiencies noted in the purchases of meals and snacks costing P56,932.00 for verification. Should the documents/information/justification be not submitted or found not in order, the corresponding disbursements may be suspended or disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management is in the process of gathering the required documents and information, and will submit them immediately to the Office of the Auditor once completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management amend their Reports on the Utilization & Status of Programs/Projects Implementation using the prescribed format under Annex C of LBC 125 and submit the same to DBM. Also, we recommended that Management prioritize the creation of the LGU’s official website. While the website is not yet operational, the Municipal Mayor may consider submitting a written notice to the Secretary of DBM, Speaker of the House of Representatives, President of the Senate of the Philippines, and the Chairpersons of the House Committee on Appropriations and Senate Committee on Finance about the non-posting of the Reports on the Utilization & Status of Programs/Projects Implementation of the Bayanihan Grant in the website and the reason therefor.
observations.managementAction: Accordingly, the corresponding Report on the Utilization of the Status of Project/Program Implementation was revised. Also, Management committed to work out the operation of their LGU website.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the lacking documents and necessary justification for the deficiencies noted in the MOA for verification. Should the documents be not submitted or found not in order, the corresponding disbursement may be suspended or disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management is in the process of gathering the required documents and information, and will submit them immediately to the Office of the Auditor once completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management revisit the system of procurement of supplies for the use of the different end-users (offices). Accordingly, the LGU may consider the hiring of a full-time General Services Officer (GSO) to be in charge of the monitoring, inventory, and control of the supplies and other properties of the LGU.  Also, the procurement can be done one time (say quarterly) based on the consolidated purchase requests of the different end-users/offices (within the approved APP) and be paid directly to suppliers, thus, strengthen internal control and ensure economy and efficiency.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor designated a full-time General Services Office (GSO) who will be in charge of the monitoring, control and inventory of supplies and PPEs of the LGU. Also, he required all department heads to prepare and submit their respective Annual Procurement Plan for 2021 as basis for the quarterly procurement of supplies to promote efficiency and economy.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management revisits the hiring of a Financial Management and Administrative consultant to save resources and to avoid possible overlap of duties and responsibilities.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor requested for consideration on the AOM justifying that the work of Mr. Formoso has not only been confined to financial and administrative work. Nonetheless, effective January 2021, Management has designated Mr. Formoso as the LGU’s consultant for various projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the lacking and necessary explanatory documents for verification, otherwise, the disbursement may be suspended or disallowed in audit.
We recommended that Management submit the lacking documents for verification, otherwise, the disbursements may be suspended or disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Management through the Local School Board and Municipal accountant ensure that the utilization of the Special Education Fund (SEF) should be supported with complete documentation as prescribed to prove the validity, legality and propriety thereof. In this regard, the lacking documents, including the corresponding liquidation documents, amounting to P60,000.00 be submitted for verification, otherwise, the transaction may be suspended or disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management revisit the opening of the new bank account for and in the name of the Offices of the Vice Mayor and Sangguniang Bayan of the LGU, thus, consider what is more practical and efficient for the LGU.
observations.reason: Management committed to close the bank account. However, as of this writing, the resolution to close the account has not yet been passed.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Management look into the nature and condition of the receivable account and prioritize the collection thereof. If warranted, the Municipal Mayor may request for authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off the receivable following the specific guidelines and procedures as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005, thus, present fairly the affected accounts in the books of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Management created a fact-finding committee to investigate on the account. The committee already conducted an investigation and submitted their report.
observations.reason: Management is still in the process of completing the documents for the request for authority to write off the account. Hence, reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management install and maintain a system of internal control for the procurement, recording and monitoring of supplies and property, plant and equipment to ensure that these are duly accounted for.
observations.managementAction: Management designated an acting GSO. Also, a memorandum was issued regarding the new system of procurement of office supplies.
observations.reason: The newly designated GSO has not yet established ledger cards for all supplies and PPEs. Also, a complete physical inventory of all supplies and PPEs has not yet been done.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant ensure timely submission of accounts. Accordingly, she should cause the immediate submission of the unsubmitted accounts of the LGU for 2018 to facilitate the audit of the transactions as well as to avoid the possible suspension or disallowance of the same and the imposition of administrative disciplinary sanctions.
observations.reason: The timeliness of submission of the LGU’s accounts in 2021 has improved. However, still, their accounts in some months were not submitted on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management revisit the grant of honoraria to MLGOO, thus, may consider a lower amount such that the difference may be used to settle the LGU’s other important obligations.
observations.managementAction: Management lowered the amount of honorarium to P5,000.00 per month in 2021. Said amounts was found reasonable for the aforementioned transaction.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct (a) the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of inventories and movable properties of the Municipality; (b) the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the report on the results of the count using the prescribed form and reconcile the results with the accounting records; and (c) the Municipal Treasurer, as property custodian, and the Municipal Accountant to maintain stock/property cards and ledgers cards, respectively.
observations.reason: Inventory Committee not able to convene for physical inventory taking. The balance of movable properties had increased to P6,622,487.96 as compared with the prior year’s balance of P5,540,757.68. Likewise, inventories had increased to P2,059,920.50 from the balance of P1,760,160.50 of the prior year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant capitalize all major repairs or improvements of assets in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles and COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 to present fairly the financial statements of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management return immediately to the source agency the amount representing the full settlement of the audit disallowance on the excessive use of the transferred funds in accordance with Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.
observations.managementAction: Management still have to coordinate with source agency for the transfer and return of the funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable data on the Municipality’s financial position as of a given period of time.
observations.reason: Documents were not available to support the adjustment.

observations.recommendation: Immediately organize an Internal Audit Service (IAS) to comply with Section 4 of AO No. 70.
observations.reason: The Municipality had not yet established the IAS.

observations.recommendation: Directed the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to make a quarterly reconciliation of their cash balances so that discrepancies can be adjusted immediately to present accurate and reliable cash balances.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant did not reconcile their cash balances.

observations.recommendation: Instructed the Supply Officer designee to furnish the auditor with the approved purchase orders and notify her of the deliveries of supplies within 24 hours from issuance of such purchase orders and acceptance of deliveries, so that surprise and selective inspection of the deliveries be made.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer did not furnish the Auditor copies of approved POs.

observations.recommendation: Initiate appropriate measures for recovery of individual records of borrowers. Intensify collection of KKK loan repayment by issuing individual bills of collections. Initiate relending of said assistance to qualified applicants duly screened by a committee created for that purpose. Make representation with the local law-making body for possible write-off of accounts deemed uncollectible.
observations.reason: Program beneficiaries could not be identified due to absence of records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Accountant to 

(a) analyze, review, monitor all dormant asset accounts, to ensure that (i) only substantiated accounts are recognized in the books; and (ii) dormant, long outstanding and unsubstantiated accounts are derecognized accordingly; 

(b) prepare a formal request to the COA through the Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) for the authority to write-off the dormant and  substantiated receivable accounts; and 

(c) provide an allowance for impairment on receivable accounts based on analysis of collection rating and collectability of the accounts to ensure the fairness of accounts presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended that the LCE require:

The Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of PPE and attach property tag/sticker for easy identification of government property and ensure its proper usage. Thereafter, submit the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Auditor for verification on the accuracy, ownership and existence of the reported PPE. Also, the inventory report should be reconciled with the accounting and property records and adjust records based on valid documents to reflect the correct balances of the PPE accounts.

The Municipal Accounting Office to continue tracing back the details of prior years’ acquisitions to support the recorded PPE.

The General Services Office (GSO) to maintain property cards to facilitate the physical count of PPE items. Determine the officials/employees to whom the equipment were issued and issue the corresponding Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) to pinpoint the direct person responsible thereof. Moreover, renew all ARE every three years to update the information of the end-user of the equipment listed in the RCPPE.
observations.managementAction:
Physical count of PPE was conducted and the corresponding report was submitted to the Auditor for verification. Likewise, reconciliation of records was made.

The Municipal Accounting Office had exerted efforts in tracing back the details of prior years’ acquisitions, however, records to support the recorded PPE were still not found on file.

The Property Officer is currently locating the files of previous years’ acquisitions because he only assumed office in January 2020 and there was no proper turnover of records. However, he had started maintaining property cards and issuances of PPE to the end-users during the year were covered with PAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasury Office to monitor regularly the balances of cash in bank accounts before the preparation and issuance of checks to avoid the incurrence of abnormal balances and possible bank charges due to insufficiency of funds. Moreover, it is recommended that at least one month before a check becomes stale, the Municipal Treasurer send a written notice to the payee of the existence of the check to facilitate the replacement thereof. We further recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to take up the previous years’ reconciling items and cancellation of the stale checks.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasury Office is regularly monitoring the balances of cash in bank accounts before the preparation of checks and notices were sent to the concerned payees before checks became stale. 

On the other hand, the Municipal Accountant prepared the necessary adjusting entries to take up the previous years’ reconciling items and cancellation of the stale checks.
observations.reason: Some previous years’ reconciling items were still included in the Bank Reconciliation Statements as of year-end due to the non-submission of the Municipal Treasury Office of the supporting documents for the preparation of necessary adjusting entries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management exert all efforts to locate the supporting documents or records to support the recorded receivables and enforce its collection. However, if remedies had been resorted to and the account remained uncollected, the LCE may request authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off those dormant accounts from the books pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016. Moreover, for those that were just recently granted, the Management must be resolute in implementing the provisions of the Memorandum of Agreement especially the penalty clause to avoid the continuing accumulation of outstanding receivables.
observations.managementAction: No action taken
observations.reason: The Receivable accounts remained undocumented, however, the non-availability of records were stated in the supporting schedules of the said accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the procuring entity strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 to ensure transparency and accountability in the procurement process. We specifically recommended that: 
The BAC comply with the posting requirement to ensure timely dissemination of the procurement opportunity.
observations.managementAction:
Posted the Invitation to Bid in the PhilGEPS website.




Notices of invitation were sent to the observers.









The BAC was able to validate the documents submitted by the bidder with the Lowest Calculated Bid.








Posting of the Notices of Award, Notices to Proceed and copies of the approved contracts were made beyond the precribed period.




The cost of implementing the CHSP was  already integrated in the computation of project’s construction cost.
observations.reason:
Posting of the complete bidding documents in the PhilGEPS website was not complied with.

Not Implemented

There were numerous instances where a duly recognized private group in a sector or discipline relevant to the procurement at hand was not invited to sit in the procurement proceedings. In addition, observers were not invited during the Detailed Evaluation of Bids.

Moreover, some copies of invitation to the observers in other stages of the procurement process did not indicate the date of receipt by their authorized representative casting doubt on whether they were invited at least five calendar days before each procurement activity as required under Section 13.3 of the Revised IRR. The Team further noted that several invitations were not received by the observers or their authorized representative which suggests that the said documents were only submitted for compliance with the requirements for auditorial contract review.

Not Implemented

The required conditions and procedures in the conduct of post-qualification on the bidders who tendered the Lowest Calculated Bid were not observed. In addition, the TWG failed to include in the Post-Qualification Evaluation Report the result of their validation and verification on some of the technical documents of the bidder.

Not Implemented

The BAC was not able to comply with the audit recommendation.

Reiterated in Observation No. 14, page 61 to 68 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned contractors who undertook the implementation of the infrastructure projects to post a warranty security as prescribed under pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 to guarantee the performance of their responsibilities in case of structural defects and failures from the final acceptance of the infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: The posting of warranty security by the required contractors were required since April 2020.
observations.reason: Forty-eight completed infrastructure projects with a total cost of P133,475,168.91 were supported with undated warranty securities in the form of surety bond, thus the validity period could not be determined.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management procure common-use supplies and equipment which are necessary in its operations from the PS-DBM as required under Administrative Order No 17 dated July 28, 2011 not only to contribute to the attainment of a modernized and standardized government procurement system but also to allow personnel involved in procurement to devote their time in performing their regular functions instead of attending to the various stages of procurement from other sources. We also recommended that prior to procurement of common-use supplies from private suppliers, certifications indicating that the supply requirements of the Municipal Government are not available be secured from the depot.
observations.managementAction: Management stated that Agency Procurement Request (APR) was submitted to DBM-PS, however, the common use supplies and equipment indicated in the APR were not available.
observations.reason: The non-availability of inventory items can be attributed to the inability of the  Municipality to submit its APP-CSE to the DBM-PS for consolidation of required data as basis in its projection of inventory requirements, scheduling of procurement activities and overall management of the central procurement of common-use goods.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management submit justification on the deficiencies noted and the lacking documents to determine the urgency and necessity of the rendered overtime services, otherwise, these will be suspended or disallowed in audit. Also, the Municipal Accountant should see to it that future transactions are supported with complete and proper documentation to establish their validity and propriety. 

We also recommended that Management formulate internal rules and policies on the rendition of overtime services, which shall be circumscribed with the policies and procedures under CSC-DBM Joint Circular No. 1 dated November 25, 2015 to avoid the incurrence of irregular expenditures particularly on the granting of overtime pay to the department heads. Except for the entitlement to overtime pay, they are however not precluded from   rendering   overtime   services   whenever   the   exigency of the service requires.
observations.managementAction:
Quantified Accomplishment Reports were submitted and the Municipal Accountant continues to check the completeness of supporting documents.



Management has complied with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Claims for overtime pay during the year were not supported with complete and proper documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer to cause the immediate submission of the sixteen (16) DVs and strictly observe the timely submission of accounts to facilitate review thereon and avoid the imposition of sanctions as provided under Section 39 (3) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Some of the DVs were submitted for evaluation.
observations.reason: Thirty-Seven DVs for CY 2020 with an aggregate amount of P3,225,966.71 were not submitted for audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Bids and Awards Committee to comply with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 regarding the submission of copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders with their supporting documents to the Auditor within five 5 working days from execution and issuance thereof for review and evaluation so that management will be immediately informed of defects and deficiencies noted, if any and to facilitate the inspection of completed projects and deliveries of goods.
observations.managementAction: Submitted various contracts for auditorial review.
observations.reason: Submission of contracts for review was beyond the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management render a written justification on the non-implementation of the programs/projects in accordance with the scheduled implementation period. Moreover, we recommended the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise tax and reappropriate the savings from the previous years’ implemented programs/projects in accordance with the authorized uses as prescribed in RA Nos. 7171 and 8240 in order to give the constituents, especially the tobacco farmers the early benefits that could be derived therefrom.

We further recommended that the LCE advise the Local Development Council to ensure that only feasible programs/projects/activities are included in the Annual Investment Program and require regular monitoring of implementation as basis in taking appropriate action.
observations.managementAction:
Some projects were implemented during the year.









Management was not able to comply with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Ten projects with a total appropriation of P55,131,446.00 from RA Nos. 7171 and 8240 funds received in CY 2018 were not yet implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management submit a written justification on the noted deficiencies. Moreover, we recommended that Management ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund to attain desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes of the Municipality and intended beneficiaries. 

We further recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and other implementing offices to review the composition of the 20% Development Fund (DF), identify the savings from fully implemented projects and those which are no longer prioritized and, subject to the provisions of the Local Government Code and the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1, reappropriate to current/existing priority development projects. It is also recommended that Management ensure that the fund will not be used for any purpose beyond those expressly prescribed therein to avoid possible sanctions.
observations.managementAction:
Funds were not fully utilized during the year.





The amount of P3,408,356.00 were allocated for maintenance and other operating expenses, however, only the amount of P563,467.94 was utilized during the year
observations.reason: The amount of P16,230,060.89, which represents 30.53 percent of the total appropriations of P53,152,714.46 for the said fund was not implemented as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit a written justification on the non-implementation of the programs/projects/ activities. Moreover, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Person to closely monitor the implementation of programs/projects/activities under the GAD Plan in order to fully address gender issues/problems of the constituents in the municipal level and to help the national government attain its goal of addressing gender issues in the country.
observations.managementAction: Funds were not fully utilized during the year.
observations.reason: Programs/projects/ activities totaling P5,319,779.88 or 53.12 percent of the total appropriation for GAD were not implemented during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit a written justification on the non-implementation of the programs/projects/ activities. Moreover, we recommended that the LCE require the LCPC to fully implement programs/projects/activities funded under the one percent (1%) internal revenue allotment geared towards the promotion and protection of the interest and welfare of children.
observations.managementAction: Funds were not fully utilized during the year.
observations.reason: Five  out of the eight programs/ projects/ activities under the one percent (1%) of the internal revenue allotment for the programs of the LCPC for CY 2020 were either partially or not implemented at all.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management establish GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation in order to respond effectively to the identified issues through the implementation of appropriate programs and projects.
observations.managementAction: GAD database was not established due to voluminous workload during the COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.reason: Management was not able to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management enforce the immediate settlement of the audit suspensions and disallowances as prescribed in the RRSA under COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009. Moreover, exhaust all available remedies in enforcing the immediate settlement of the unsettled PEI of retired employees. Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that supporting documents of transactions are complete, proper and valid in order to prevent the same from being suspended/ disallowed in post-audit.
observations.managementAction: No action taken
observations.reason: The outstanding balances remained unsettled by the persons responsible/liable thereof.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE) to create the Bids and Awards Committee with members’ qualifications basically compliant to the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184. The Head of the Procuring Entity shall see to it that the assignment order creating the BAC shall specify the designation of the Chairman and the Vice-Chairman who shall at least possess the required basic qualification.
observations.managementAction: They committed to appoint an interim Bids and Awards Committee Chairman from among the current members to correct the wrong practice of designating a chairperson who is not qualified for the position.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following to the Management

To stop the practice of procuring and payment of goods and services through cash advance;

To enforce that all procurement of goods and services be paid by checks directly to suppliers/ contractors/ service providers except for petty disbursements out of petty cash fund in pursuance of COA Circular No. 97-002 so that appropriate taxes could be withheld from suppliers and could be remitted to the BIR Section 37 of Republic Act No. 10963; and

To instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly abide by the procurement processes provided under the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 such as the mandatory conduct of competitive bidding or the alternative methods of procurement subject to the prior approval of the HoPE and whenever justified by the conditions provided in the said Act.
observations.managementAction: They can only limit but not eliminate cash advances simply because there are suppliers who do not accept check payments. If they do not grant cash advances, it may hamper the decorating process and they may not be able to finish on time, unless they resort to the reimbursement process, a process that was discouraged by previous auditors.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to immediately comply with the prompt submission of  all  paid Disbursement Vouchers and the supporting documents to the Auditor for verification and timely conduct of audit as required in Section 100 of PD 1445 and Section 7.2.1.a of COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 to avoid the issuances of Notice of Suspension and/or Disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The management submitted the vouchers in the list

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity direct the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report approved by him and submit to the GPPB in compliance with Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: They instructed the Bids and Awards committee to look into the matter and prepare the PMR based on a format prescribed by GPPB and submit to that office as soon as the document is available. However, there was a problem on the submission because, under section 12.2 of PD 9184, it has to be submitted in “printed and electronic format” and there is no on-line submission option in the GPPB portal.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the management comply with all the requirements pertaining to procurements set forth under COA Circular No. 2009-001 and Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act No.  9184

We further recommended the BAC to:

Post to PhilGEPS bulletin board all procurement opportunities, results of bidding such as Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and approved contract and other required information within the reglementary period; and
Invite observers in all stages of Bidding Process.
observations.managementAction: They have directed the BAC secretariat to locate all the required missing documents as mentioned in our AOM and submit them as soon as possible.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Management require the Municipal Assessor to coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) on the immediate adoption of resolutions on the tax revisions of real property, to ensure higher RPT collections for the municipality to increase the needed funds for the budgeted programs, projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor told the Audit eam that the updating of their tax assessment on real property is now on process and expected to be used as basis for CY 2020 RPT collections.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
5. Direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounting records with property records of the General Services Office; and,
6. Require the Municipal Accountant to undertake the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the result of reconciliation with the GSO.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant shall take necessary procedures to update the account

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue an Executive Order creating a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation Team pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant and
the Municipal Assessor reconcile their records and coordinate with the Registry of
Deeds for the titled lots in the name of the Municipality. We further recommended
that registration and transfer of title of the land owned by the Municipality be
prioritized to fully protect its interest.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant set up a SAGF to record the
receipt and utilization of the Bayanihan grant in accordance with LBC No. 125
dated April 7, 2020 to facilitate monitoring of the receipt and utilization of the
grant.

We recommended that Management adhere to the requirements prescribed
under DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 09, series of 2020, in the implementation
of the SAP and other similar programs of DSWD to ensure that financial assistance
are given to qualified beneficiaries and the objectives of the program are attained.

We recommended that the MDRRMO coordinate with the Punong
Barangays for the submission of the list of recipient-beneficiaries.
