We recommended that in the preparation of the ABC and POW of
infrastructure projects, the Municipal Engineer ensure that the basis for computation
of the VAT component as well as the rates thereof conform to DPWH Department
Order No. 22 to avoid excessive ABCs and contract costs.

We recommended that Management refrain from holding
seminars/workshops outside of the Province in accordance with pertinent sections of
COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 to avoid extravagant
expenditures and wastage of government funds.

We recommended that (a) the Municipal Accountant prepare a Journal
Entry Voucher to recognize/capitalize the costs of the AC units under the
appropriate asset account in accordance with generally accepted accounting
principles and COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 to present fairly
the financial statements of the Municipality; and (b) the Municipal Treasurer to
prepare and issue AREs to the person having custody and control over the property
to ensure the safety, determine the accountability and facilitate accounting thereof.

We recommended that Management submit a Summary/List of Donations
Received, Distributed and Balances supported with (a) Proof of receipt by and
distribution to the beneficiaries and (b) Inventory of remaining undistributed items,
if any, in accordance with Section 1 of COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21,
2020.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to require the MA to (a) facilitate the request for writing-off of the dormant and unsubstantiated accounts to present fairly the financial condition of the Municipality; (b) provide an allowance for impairment on receivable accounts based on analysis of collection rating and collectability of the accounts; and (c) maintain subsidiary records to support the general ledger accounts to avoid the incurrence of the same deficiency.

2.3.    We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to (a) create an Inventory Committee to undertake the conduct of physical inventory of PPE owned by the Municipality and prepare the RPCPPE for reconciliation with the MAO records; (b) require the MA and GSO Officer to maintain the appropriate property records such as SLCs and PCs, respectively; (c) require the MA to provide the necessary allowance for depreciation on all depreciable assets of the Municipality; and (d) instruct the MTO Officer to facilitate the transfer of ownership of the TCT on parcel of land purchased in CY 2019 under the name of the Municipality.
We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer (MT) to continuously improve taxes collection through conduct of tax campaigns.

We recommended and the LCE concurred to (a) direct the Municipal Engineer to (i) observe strictly the general guidelines on the information and publicity of infrastructure projects undertaken by the Municipality in accordance with  COA Circular No. 2013-004; (ii) verify the completeness of technical documents supporting the implementation of infrastructure projects pursuant to the documentary requirements provided by the DPWH Standard Specifications for Highways, Bridges and Airport; and (iii) require the concerned Contractors to  repair the defective and damaged portion of the road projects pursuant to the one year warranty period  from the date of project completion up to final acceptance or the defects liability period pursuant to Section 62 of the CY 2016 IRR of RA No. 9184; and (b) require the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of supporting documents prior to payment.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) MA to submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents to support the validity and propriety of the disbursements; and (b) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the MA to comply with the posting and reporting requirements, respectively, as prescribed under RA Nos. 11469 and 11494, and DBM LBC No. 125.
We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA, MT and other concerned Municipal officials to continue to be compliant with the rules and regulations on the use of funds.

We recommended and  the LCE agreed to (a) require the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officers to  involve closely the barangay officials on solid waste management activities and activate the Solid Waste Management Committees in every barangays for the strict implementation of the provisions of RA No. 9003 particularly on maintenance of  functional MRF and observance of waste segregation at source; (b) consider extending financial assistance and other resources to two  barangays with no sufficient fund to establish their own MRF; and (c) develop programs on composting, recycling, re-use, and recovery of solid waste generated in order to reduce hauling and disposal costs of the Municipality.

We recommended and the LCE concurred to (a) require the GAD Focal Point Officer(s) and the MBO to continue appropriating at least 5% for GAD-related activities; and (b) require the GAD Focal Point Officers to (i) restate the unimplemented PPAs in the preparation of GPB for the ensuing year; and (ii) prepare and submit to the Audit Team the GAD AR within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the LCE concurred to require the MA and the MT  to continue to be compliant with the withholding of appropriate taxes and remittance thereof to the BIR within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA  and the MT to continue to be compliant with the withholding of GSIS premiums from employees’ compensation and remittance thereof to the GSIS within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA and the MT to continue to be compliant with regulations on the withholding premiums and loan amortizations from employees’ compensation and remittance thereof together with the Municipal share to the HDMF within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA and the MT to continue to be compliant with the regulations on the withholding of monthly PhilHealth premiums and remittance thereof together with the Municipal share within the prescribed period.
13.4	We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the MA to be more diligent in evaluating the supporting papers on claims to avoid suspension or disallowance of transactions.

We recommended that the LCE require:
The Municipal Accountant to maintain complete PPELCs and continue tracing back the details of prior years’ acquisitions to support the recorded PPE.
The Property Officer to reconcile the inventory report with the accounting records to verify the details and existence of PPE balances in 2007 and previous years. Also, maintain PCs to facilitate the physical count of PPE items. Furthermore, determine the officials/employees to whom the equipment were issued and issue the corresponding PAR to pinpoint the direct person responsible thereof and renew them every three years to update the information of the end-user of the equipment listed in the inventory report.
If the existence of PPE in CY 2007 and previous years could not be established despite exhaustion of all diligent efforts, Management may follow the procedures on the disposition for non-existing PPE items for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of government agencies as provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and Supply Officer to reconcile their records and adjust them based on valid documents to reflect the correct balances of the Inventory accounts. Also, require the Supply Officer to prepare the RSMI together with RIS and Certified List of Recipients showing recipients’ signatures vis-à-vis the goods received and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant as her basis in recording the issuances of inventories to the end-users.

We recommended that the LCE immediately relieve the two Revenue Collection Clerks from their duties and responsibilities and reiterate the demand for the immediate remittance of the collections and initiate legal action, if warranted. Moreover, instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer, Liquidating Officer and collectors to perform strictly and observe the diligence of a good father of family in doing their duties and functions in the receipt and verification of collections and accountable forms to ensure the timely and intact turn-over and deposit of collections, thus possible loss or misuse of funds will be avoided.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to prepare the necessary transfer of funds and accounting reports to facilitate the said reversion and certification as to the availability of said funds for subsequent reappropriation for other social services.

We recommended that Management:
Require the concerned officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances to settle immediately their accounts, otherwise, consider the imposition of sanctions such as the withholding of their salaries as provided for in Section 9.3.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
Adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances in order to prevent accumulation of unliquidated cash advances and the possible loss or misuse of government funds.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents to facilitate the technical evaluation of the projects and goods and to avoid supension in audit. Oblige the  Municipal Accountant to make sure that all necessary documents pertaining to the procurement of goods and implementation of infrastructrure projects shall be attached to the disbursement voucher before payment is made to establish the validity and propriety of transactions.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the DTRs and the required attachments to avoid suspension in audit. Likewise, ensure that succeeding payments of salaries and wages are supported with said documents and submit the same to the Auditor within the prescribed period to facilitate the audit thereon.

We recommended that Management through the Municipal Accountant and MSWDO, cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents and justifications on the deficiencies noted in support of the validity and propriety of the transactions and to avoid suspension in audit. Instruct the MSWDO to review and verify carefully the completeness and authenticity of the eligibility requirements of the proposed beneficiaries to ensure that the social amelioration programs of the national government are granted to the qualified individuals. Moreover, see to it that the beneficiaries as listed in the payroll are based on the DSWD-generated list so as not to deprive the identified beneficiaries of the needed assistance.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents and justifications on the deficiencies observed for further evaluation of the transactions and avoid suspension in audit. Also, oblige the Municipal Accountant see to it that disbursement vouchers are duly supported with the required basic documents to ensure their validity and propriety.

We recommended that the LCE require the Supply Officer to notify the Auditor or to furnish her copies of delivery documents within 24 hours from acceptance of delivered items for inspection purposes to ensure that delivered and accepted items agree with the quantity, quality and specifications as ordered by the Municipality.

We recommended that Management consider the more prudent options without sacrificing efficiency and effectiveness in relation to the specific purpose/s instead of purchasing costly models/brands for the optimum utilization of government funds. We also recommend that the Sangguniang Bayan pass a resolution establishing a clear policy on the provision of gadgets to authorized officials to rationalize disbursements of funds allocated for this purpose.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer to cause the immediate submission of the disbursement vouchers and strictly observe the timely submission of transaction documents to facilitate the review thereon and avoid the imposition of sanctions as provided under Section 122 (2) of PD No. 1445 and also enable the issuance of audit results to management which could serve as an aid in management decisions and inputs.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer to observe closer monitoring and evaluation of all ongoing infrastructure projects to ensure the completion of the projects within the specified contract duration. Moreover, we recommended that LCE through the Municipal Engineer observe and implement the calibrated measures for contracts with negative slippage during the contract duration as provided under GPPB Circular No. 03-2019 dated March 8, 2019 to ensure the timely implementation of the infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the procuring entity strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 to ensure transparency and accountability in the procurement process. We specifically recommended that Management instruct:
The BAC comply with the submission of the Final APP to the GPPB on or before the end of January of the budget year and it shall be posted in the GPPB Website to ensure transparency in the procurement process and in the implementation of procurement contracts.
The BAC comply with the posting requirement of the Approved Bidding Documents to ensure timely dissemination of the procurement opportunity.
The BAC conduct the Pre-Bid Conference within the reasonable period to allow the prospective bidders to adequately prepare their bids.
The BAC invite in writing three observers in all stages of the procurement activities to sit in its proceedings and ensure that the invitation is received within the prescribed period.
The BAC adopt the required procedures and conditions in the Post-Qualification stage particularly in the verification, validation and ascertainment of all statements made and documents submitted by the bidder with the Lowest Calculated Bid to ensure that they are in accordance with the requirements as stated in the Bidding Documents.
The BAC to ensure that the required performance security is posted by the winning bidder before the signing of the contract to guarantee the faithful performance of his obligations under the contract.
The BAC properly disclose in the BAC Resolution Declaring LCRB and Recommending Approval by HOPE the procurement activities short of award to ensure that the contract is awarded upon approval of the appropriation ordinance, as the case may be or upon approval of the list of programs, projects and activities funded out of the RA Nos. 7171 and RA 8240.
The BAC, through its Secretariat, see to it that the Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and a copy of the approved contract were posted in the PhilGEPS website within three (3) and fifteen (15) calendar days from their respective issuances for information and  transparency in the procurement process.

We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to strictly observe the posting of requests for price quotation/proposal and the notices of award together with the contract or purchase order for procurement of goods and services through alternative methods of procurement at any conspicuous place in the premises of the Municipality to ensure interested users’ ready access to information.  Also, require the BAC to post in the PhilGEPS website before issuing request for price quotations/proposals to prospective suppliers/service providers for wider information and to give opportunity to other eligible suppliers/service providers to promote transparency and competition in the procurement process.

We recommended that the LCE require the concerned contractors who undertook the implementation of the infrastructure projects to post a warranty security as prescribed under pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 to guarantee the performance of their responsibilities in case of structural defects and failures from the final acceptance of the infrastructure projects. In addition, require the concerned contractors to secure a certification from the Insurance Company on the validity period of the undated warranty securities.

We recommended that the LCE require the concerned suppliers to post a warranty security which shall be covered by either retention money in an amount equivalent to at least one percent (1%) of every progress payment, or a special bank guarantee equivalent to at least one percent (1%) of the total contract to guarantee the performance of their responsibilities in case of manufacturing defects after acceptance of the delivered goods.

We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to strictly comply with the submission of the APP-CSE in consonance with DBM Circular Letter No. 2013-14 dated November 29, 2013 to ensure the inclusion of useful information for consolidation purposes as a basis in its projection of inventory requirements, scheduling of procurement activities and overall management of the central procurement of common-use goods.

We recommended that Management:
Facilitate the negotiation with the owners of the lot for the execution of usufruct agreement to ensure the completion of the two partially implemented infrastructure projects and to avoid their deterioration.
For the two infrastructure projects which remained unimplemented, Management look for other lot owners who are willing to provide the needed site for the implementation of the proposed projects.
Require the Municipal Engineering Office to conduct all the necessary detailed engineering activities before the conduct of procurement process to avoid the issuance of suspension orders and ensure the timely completion of the projects. Issue only suspension order when necessary to avoid delay in the implementation of infrastructure projects.

We recommended Management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit Financial Statements that shows valid representation of the effects of cash flows in order to avoid wrong information that may affect the decision-making of the intended users.

We recommended the following to Management:
Require the concerned Municipal Office to prepare the Summary/ List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances, and be submitted to the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, through the Office of the Civil Defense, copy furnished the Office of the Auditor; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to record any balances of donations received be costed and recorded in the books or in the registries.

We recommended Management to require and direct  the Municipal Accountant in coordination with municipal officials concerned with the distribution of relief goods for the immediate submission of Delivery Receipt duly acknowledged by the Municipality, Sales Invoice, Summary List of Affected Households/ Families who were given the assistance as certified by the barangay/ municipal official concerned and information in the supporting documents as to the quantity of rice (in kilos or in cavans as the case may be) given to each of the affected family/ household pursuant to Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended Management to require the BAC Secretariat through the BAC Chairman to post in the PhilGEPS website, the website of the Procuring Entity (PE) or its electronic procurement service provider, if any; and any conspicuous place in the premises of the PE for information purposes the aforementioned procurement of foods/ food supplies totaling ₱1,456,900.00 for COVID-19 frontliners pursuant to Annex H, item IV, L (2) of the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184 and GPPB NPM No. 003-2020 and submit proof of posting to the Office of the Auditor.
We also recommended Management to instruct the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the BAC Chairman to immediately submit other lacking documents/ information required in the aforementioned procurement to facilitate validation of the propriety of disbursement made.

We recommended Management to require and direct  the Municipal Accountant for the immediate submission of  the Disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents pertaining to procurement of  several sacks of rice distributed as relief goods and food assistance amounting to ₱7,768,192.00  to facilitate conduct of validation on the regularity of procurement, propriety of utilization and disbursement required under item 5.0 of DBM LBC No. 125 and completeness of delivery and eventual full distribution to the affected families of COVID-19 pandemic.

We recommended that Management ensure that the 20% Development Fund are utilized for the purposes intended, as cited in the DILG/DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity direct the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report approved by him and submit to the GPPB in compliance with Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that management of LGU Sta. Maria enforce regularly that all procurement of goods and services be paid by checks directly to suppliers/ contractors/ service providers except for petty disbursements out of petty cash fund in pursuance of COA Circular No. 97-002 and to comply with BIR Revenue Memorandum Order No. 23 – 2014 to avoid liabilities and penalties arising from non-compliance as clearly stipulated in the same BIR Revenue Memorandum Order.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor immediately require the BAC Secretariat to consolidate the Municipality’s PPMPs and to stop the practice of procuring supplies and materials by piecemeal through shopping as alternative mode of procurement and adopt Public Bidding as the primary mode of procurement in compliance with Section 10 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be strict in the granting of Cash Advances and observe those allowable transactions that can be paid thru cash advance pursuant to COA Circular 97-002.

We recommended to the management to limit the utilization of the SEF pertaining to the programs and projects defined under and RA 7160 and Joint Circular (JC) No. 1, s. 2017 of DEPED, DBM and DILG in order to maximize the benefits that the students and teachers could avail under this fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare and sign the check only on duly approved disbursement vouchers. The provisions of Section 40, Volume I of the Manual on New Government Accounting System pertaining to drawing of checks should be adhered to.

We recommended that Management ensure that travelling expenses are made within the prescribed limits in accordance with EO No. 77.  The municipality should find ways to minimize the cost and optimize the performance of duties of its officers and employees for an effective and efficient organization.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Instruct the MSWDO to take cognizance with the guidelines issued by the
regulatory agencies concerned which affect the operations of the MSWD
Office through periodic viewing of the portal of these agencies or through
other activities that may be deemed appropriate; and,
b. Require the MSWDO and the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Office
of the Auditor the: (a) Memorandum of Agreement between the DSWD and
the Municipality; (b) OSCA Identification of the individuals identified; and,
(c) photographs of beneficiaries during the actual receipt of the assistance.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. To address the deficiencies in the documentary requirements
 Require the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO)
and Municipal Accountant to submit the valid identification of individuals
who were granted with assistance but has no valid identification attached in
the payroll and the photograph of beneficiaries upon receipt of the
assistance.
b. To address the issue of multiple entries in the Payroll
 Direct the MSWDO to create a task force to validate the qualification of the
beneficiaries who have multiple entries in the payroll and to review the
entries in the SAC Forms.
c. To address the issue on the non-submission of the MOA
 Require the MSWDO to submit a copy MOA from the DSWD and to
provide justification as to the delayed submission.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Health Officer
to direct the MESU, Local Contact Tracing Team and BHERTs to observe the timely
submission of daily reports and to establish clear delegation of tasks and responsibilities in
accordance with existing guidelines.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Direct the members of the Local COVID-19 Task Force to prepare the Local
COVID-19 Task Force Daily Report and submit the same to the regulatory
agencies;
b. Require the Municipal Health Office (MHO) and the Contact Tracing Teams to
provide timely and accurate information to the Local COVID-19 Task Force
for the preparation of the report; and,
c. Instruct the MHO, the members of the Local COVID-9 Task Force and the
Contact Tracing teams to keep abreast with the issuances and requirements of
regulatory agencies by attending conferences, browsing agency websites or
undertaking other means that may be deemed appropriate.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Direct the members of the Local COVID-19 Task Force to create a centralized
information and recording committee which will take charge in the pooling of
information, reports and documentation relating to the activities undertaken to
address the pandemic; and,
b. Require the members of the Local COVID-19 Task Force to provide the
documents pertaining to the implementation of the mandatory wearing of face
masks and other PPE.

We recommended the following to the Municipal Mayor:
 Require the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO)
to formulate a COVID-19 Waste Management Plan and to incorporate the same
in the Local Solid Waste Management Plan;
 Request the MENRO to draft policies on: (a) recognition of the threats posed
by COVID-19 related health care wastes; and, (b) establishment of separate
storage and collection bins for COVID-19 related health care wastes;
 Direct the MENRO to conduct monitoring on each barangay to ensure that
COVID-19 related health care wastes are segregated at the household level;
 Request the Barangays under the Municipality to conduct an information drive
to raise awareness of households on proper hygiene practices, and safe
handling of infectious and contaminated health care wastes for collection;
 Provide documentation on the segregation and storage activities for reference
and archiving.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Require the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO)
and the Local Solid Waste Management Board to formulate policies on the
collection and transport of COVID-19 related health care wastes;
b. Direct the MENRO to specify in the guidelines on waste management the
important details on the collection and transport of COVID-19 related health
care waste including, but not limited to the following: (a) manner of
disinfection of collection and storage bins; (b) schedule of collection; (c)
prohibition of manual segregation; and, (d) mandatory wearing of personal
protective equipment for sanitation workers;
c. Instruct the Municipal Health Officer and the MENRO to conduct an
orientation to waste collectors on the proper handling of COVID-19 related
health care wastes; and,
d. Provide documentation on collection and transportation activities for reference
and archiving.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Direct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO)
and Municipal Health Officer (MHO) to submit the microbial laboratory tests
results of the treatment facility on COVID-19 related health care wastes to the
DENR and/ or DOH for approval;
b. Require the MENRO and MHO to conduct an orientation to waste disposal
personnel on the classification of wastes being delivered for precautionary
measures, maintaining a safe distance from, and avoid direct contact with the
wastes;
c. Instruct the MENRO and Municipal Engineer to provide for the specifications
and estimates in the construction of septic vaults for the treatment and disposal
of domestic COVID-19 related health care wastes;
d. Require the MENRO to institute a recording mechanism that will monitor the
treatment and disposal of COVID-19 related health care wastes; and.
e. Provide documentation on the activities related to the treatment and disposal
activities for reference and archiving.

We recommended the following undertakings to the Municipal Mayor to efficiently
and effectively manage the COVID-19 related health care wastes:
a. Establish a Composite Team in the Local Solid Waste Management Board
(LSWMB) that will take charge in the development of policies pertaining to
COVID-19 related health care wastes and for its implementation;
b. Require the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer
(MENDRO) and LSWMB to prepare a COVID-19 waste management plan and
integrate the same to the 10- year Solid Waste Management Plan of the
Municipality.
c. Direct the MENRO to conduct orientation and other information drive about
the proper management of COVID-19 related wastes in barangays.
d. Require the MENRO and the Composite Team to devise activities to support
the Barangay Ecological Solid Waste Management Committees in the proper
handling of COVID-19 related health care wastes.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
 Require the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENR))
and the Municipal Health Officer (MHO) to prepare a specific Occupational
Health and Safety Plan duly considering the risks faced by each department in
the Municipality and to submit the same to the Office of the Municipal Mayor
for approval and dissemination;
 Require the MENRO and MHO to conduct: (a) orientation on the proper usage
and maintenance of PPEs; and (b) trainings on Occupational Health and Safety
with emphasis on the hazards in the workplace, environmental and
administrative controls and the properties and characteristics of PPEs;
 Direct the MENRO to provide disinfectants and hand sanitizers for every waste
collection vehicle; and,
 Provide documentation of activities on Occupational Health and Safety Plan
implementation for reference and archiving.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the Sangguninan Bayan to
identify the benefits that may be given to employees, if warranted, in coordination with the
Municipal Budget Officer and to provide the benefits due to them subject to the financial
capability of the local government unit.

We commended the Municipality of Sta. Maria for successfully assisting the
national government in carrying out programs for the farmers.
To sustain the successful implementation of the program, we recommended to the
Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Agriculturist to: (a) conduct regular monitoring
of the beneficiaries and provide them continuous assistance; and, (b) devise programs that
will benefit the agriculture sector of the local government unit.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Require the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO)
and the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer
(MDRRMO) to prepare a database of Muslim families and communities in the
Municipality through the assistance of the Barangays and to develop programs
that will address their needs during the national health emergency.
b. Direct the MSWDO to keep abreast with the guidelines of regulatory agencies
by attending conferences, visiting agency website and other activities that may
be deemed appropriate.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Require the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO)
and the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer
(MDRRMO) to prepare a list of persons with disabilities in the Municipality
through the assistance of the Barangays and to develop programs that will
address their needs during the national health emergency.
b. Direct the MSWDO to keep abreast with the guidelines of regulatory agencies
by attending conferences, visiting agency website and other activities that may
be deemed appropriate.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) through its Secretariat to post
the emergency procurement activities under the Bayanihan Act in the GPPB
Online Portal; and,
b. Direct the BAC to be abreast with the issuances of the regulatory agencies
pertaining to procurement activities.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit copies of the
following: (a) Mayor’s Permit or Business Permit of the Supplier; (b) Notice to
Proceed; and, (c) Notice of Award;
b. Direct the BAC to review mechanisms in-place to ensure that documents
submitted to the Accounting Office are complete; and,
c. Require the Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal General Services Officer and
the Municipal Engineer to submit justification as to why the payment of the
supplies was made even though the inspection and acceptance report indicated
that delivery was made on a later date.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the adjusting entries to present the accurate balance of the RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET Receivables in the financial statements. We further recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant recognize discounts for advance and prompt payment of RPT and the additional one percent tax accruing to the SEF in the year the taxes are due in accordance with Section 22 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume 1.
observations.managementAction: Discounts for RPT and SET were recognized and recorded in the books, contrary to Section 22 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS), Volume I, thereby overstating the RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET Receivables by P72,605.25 at the end of the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management explain in writing why there appeared to be splitting in the procurement of medicines and medical, dental and laboratory supplies. Henceforth, we recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) refrain from splitting requisitions and procure goods through competitive bidding in accordance with Section 10, Rule IV of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184, save only in cases provided under Rule XVI of the IRR.
observations.managementAction: Management stated in their letter that separate requisitions were made as these were charged to the different programs of the Municipality. However, for current year’s procurements, these were consolidated and purchased in bulk.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management stop the practice of spending for catering services in relation to street dancing, games and sports competitions and other activities for entertainment to highlight fiesta celebration to avoid incurring unnecessary expenditures.
observations.managementAction: Fiesta expenses were no longer charged to the municipal funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) consider the immediate hiring of a Municipal Bookkeeper; (b) provide personnel assistance to the Office of the Municipal Accountant; and (c) require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately to the Auditor the financial statements and supporting schedules as of December 31, 2019.
observations.managementAction: Financial Statements submitted on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to coordinate closely with the DENR for the immediate release of the SLUP and CNC. Henceforth, ensure that required permits/clearances from other government agencies are secured prior to project implementation.
observations.managementAction: Funds were immediately returned to the BOT per directive of the DILG.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain GLs and SLs in accordance with Section 04 (e) of the Manual on the NGA) for Use in LGUs, Volume I, to facilitate the preparation of accurate financial statements and verification thereof.
observations.managementAction: General Ledgers and Subsidiary Ledges were prepared to support FS preparation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the contractors to post Warranty Security in the amount provided under Section 62.2.3.3 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Government Procurement Act (RA 9184) in the procurement of infrastructure projects otherwise, structural defects and or failure within the warranty period not covered by the surety bond and not repaired by the contractor shall be borne by persons liable. Any claims for repair of structures within the warranty period will be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Warranty Security posted was compliant with the requirement under the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

observations.recommendation: Instructed the Municipal Engineer to submit all suspension orders to the Office of the Auditor or any document that would warrant the non-imposition of liquidated damages to the concerned contractors, otherwise, direct the Municipal Accountant to compute the liquidated damages.
observations.managementAction: Liquidated damages were imposed on delays without approved suspension orders.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer, secure all stale checks and determine those requiring issuance of replacement checks. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer cancel the stale checks and furnish the Accounting Office with the original disbursement vouchers as basis for the preparation of journal entry vouchers debiting the Cash in Bank, and crediting Accounts Payable and Prior Years’ Adjustment for stale checks less than two years and for those more than two years, respectively. We recommended further that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant regularly monitor all outstanding checks, send written notices to payees a month before these become stale and prepare the necessary adjustments for the reconciling items totaling P11,041,126.31.
observations.managementAction: Bank Reconciliation Statements were submitted together with the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) Require all concerned accountable officers to immediately settle their cash advances in accordance with the pertinent sections of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; (b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor and strictly enforce the liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period, and see to it that no additional cash advance shall be allowed unless the previous ones are settled or properly accounted for; and (c) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular evaluation/ validation of all outstanding accounts; send demand letters to officers and employees with long outstanding accounts, and impose sanctions when necessary, such as withholding of their salaries as provided in the Circular; and if warranted, request for write-off of long-overdue cash advances in accordance with the procedures under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: A considerable portion of prior years’ unliquidated cash advances were unliquidated collected bringing the current balance to P486,294.98.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer strictly observe the PS limitation which shall not exceed 55% of the total annual income from regular sources realized in the next preceding fiscal year pursuant to Section 325(a) of R.A. No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: Mandatory positions were not filled-up. The 55% PS limit based on the income for CY 2018 amounted to P37,281,106.73 while actual PS CY 2020 amounted to P34,847,783.33.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer remit taxes withheld from compensation as well as from purchases of goods and services within ten (10) days after at the end of each month in accordance with BIR Revenue Regulations No. 2-98 dated April 17, 1998.
observations.managementAction: Taxes withheld from compensation were remitted within the required time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC and TWG to prepare and review the checklist in all biddings conducted and ensure that all the official requirements indicated therein are in order to preserve the integrity of the bidding process as mandated under R.A. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Subsequent procurement showed compliance with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) on or before the 15th day after the end of each month in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 for proper evaluation and monitoring.
observations.managementAction: Reports were submitted when required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned LGU official to submit within the reglementary period approved copy of the GAD plan and Budget and ensure that the annual accomplishment report was submitted on time.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Plan and Budget were incorporated in the budgets of the different departments.

observations.recommendation: Required the Local School Board to explain and submit the legal basis of including the expense items under the SEF budget. Henceforth, ensure that only those priority expense items as enumerated under Section 4.0 of DEPED/DBM/DILG JMC No. 1 series of 2017 are included in the SEF budget approved and enacted.
observations.managementAction: Per letter received from the District Supervisor, the subject expenditures were allowable charges to the SEF.

observations.recommendation: Cause the immediate refund of the amount of P290,000.00 paid to all appointed officials/ employees of the municipality considering that the disbursement of funds was devoid of any legal basis.
observations.managementAction: The payment of the same allowance was already stopped and refunds were already made.

observations.recommendation: Directed the Municipal Accountant to release immediately the barangays’ shares of prior years and succeeding shares on a quarterly basis within five (5) days after the end of each quarter.
observations.managementAction: RPT shares were remitted to the respective barangays.

observations.recommendation: Create the GAD Focal Point System in the municipality in accordance with Section 37, Rule VI of R.A. 9710 to ensure that the programs, projects, activities in the Municipality are responsive to gender issues and concerns. Direct the designated GAD Focal Person to initiate activities for the establishment of a local GAD code based on the gender issues and concerns through consultation with women constituents. Coordinate with the Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) and Philippine Commission on Women (PCW) in the formulation and development of said Code. Make representation with the local law-making body for the eventual passage and approval of the Local GAD code.
observations.managementAction: The SWO I was designated as GAD Focal Person.

observations.recommendation: Directed all those who received the PEI to refund the amount of P10,000.00 each, the payment of which is not legally due them, the PS limitation provided under Section 325(a) of RA 7160, having been not observed by the municipality. Henceforth, management shall strictly observe the fifty-five percent (55%) limitation on personal services to prevent disallowances in audit.
observations.managementAction: Repayments were deducted from the payroll.

observations.recommendation: Directed all persons held liable therefore to immediately and fully settle the disallowances in audit pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006 to enable the Municipality to utilize the funds for its priority development projects.
observations.managementAction: The grant of the allowance was already stopped and partial settlements were already made.

Land titles are in process.




Disposal of unserviceable property is in process.

observations.recommendation: We further recommended that the Municipality continuously conduct tax campaigns to increase its revenue collections and source out funds for programs, projects and activities that would   further improve the delivery of basic services to its constituents.
observations.managementAction: Conducted  continuous efforts for effective and efficient collection of taxes.

Committed to observe strictly the guidance on information and publicity of government projects.

Committed to ensure the conformity of projects with the DPWH Standards.






Committed to ensure repair of damage projects by contractor.
Committed to ensure completeness of supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require the concerned officials to

(a) prepare the LDRRMF Utilization Report regularly on a monthly basis; and 

(b) refrain from using the DRRMF for transactions which are not calamity and/or disaster-related.
observations.managementAction:
Monthly reports were already prepared.


Refrained from using DRRM fund for transactions which were not disaster- related.

Ensured involvement of Barangay in solid waste management program.



Continued  the implementation of information education campaign.


 Continued the giving of financial assistance to Barangays.


Continued the implementation of waste segregation and composting.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management 
(a) require the MSWDO to strictly allocate not more than 1% of the Internal Revenue Allotment for Local Council for Protection of Children so as not to deprive other programs, projects and activities (PPAs) of the Municipality of needed funds; and 
(b) direct the MSWDO to execute the PPAs, monitor their implementation, and  provide documented reports on the impact of the PPAs in order to address the various issues and concerns pertaining to the children’s welfare.
observations.managementAction:
Strictly  allocated  the  mandatory requirements for protection of children.




Monitored the implementation of PPA’s and prepared the corresponding reports.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management 
(a) require the Municipal Budget Officer together with the focal person and focal departments/offices to continue appropriating at least 5% for GAD-related activities in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01: Guidelines on the Localization of Magna Carta of Women and 
(b) require the focal persons to follow up from the concerned authorized focal departments the timely submission of accomplishment report, make a clear assessment of the GAD PPAs for monitoring  and evaluation of the GAD implementation, and submit to the Audit Team the GAD Accomplishment Report within five working days from end of January of the ensuing year.
observations.managementAction:
Ensured strict compliance on allocation of 5% for GAD related program and activities.





Submitted the required report to concerned agencies on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management continue to faithfully comply with BIR RMC No. 23-2012 dated February 14, 2012 on the withholding and remittance of taxes within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: Sustained compliance with BIR rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management continue to comply with regulations on the deduction and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations including the government share within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: Sustained compliance with GSIS rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management continue to comply with regulations on the withholding and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: Sustained compliance with Pag-IBIG rules and regulation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management continue to comply with regulations on the withholding and remittance of monthly PhilHealth contributions within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: Sustained compliance with PhilHealth rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
(a) Management   instruct the (i) officials involved in the processing of claims to be more diligent in evaluating supporting papers of claims to avoid suspensions and disallowances in audit; and (ii)  responsible and liable persons require the immediate refund of disallowed claims once these have become final and executory; and 
(b) Municipal Accountant to comply with the revised documentary requirements for common government transactions prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to establish the regularity of transactions.

We recommended that Management submit a written justification on the deficiencies noted. Moreover, we recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineering Office to conduct detailed engineering before approval of the Program of Work for proper identification of the items of work to be done to establish the reasonableness of cost estimates for materials and labor.
observations.managementAction: Program of Works were corrected and the specific items of work to be done were indicated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE:

Enforce the payment of fuel, oil and lubricants directly to the supplier in observance of sound principle of internal control on the disbursement process and in order to withhold the taxes due to the government;

Instruct the GSO to assign a knowledgeable personnel to check the details or review the propriety and completeness of information on the trip tickets prior to official trips and to regularly submit Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to the Auditor thru the Municipal Accountant for purposes of verification on the reasonableness of fuel consumption; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to review and verify the completeness of documentary requirements for claims before affixing her signature on Box A of each disbursement voucher to ascertain the validity and propriety of disbursements made.
observations.managementAction:
Payments were made directly to the suppliers.



Payments of fuel, oil and lubricants were supported with accomplished  driver’s trip tickets.

Maria to submit the utilization/liquidation reports and relevant documents to ensure that the financial assistance given were used for the intended purposes, otherwise, these will be suspended in audit.  Moreover, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that financial assistance granted to other government agencies and non-government organizations are covered with a MOA or its equivalent indicating the conditions, requirements and responsibilities of the recipients on the use of government funds. observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the accounting and reporting guidelines on the Local Roads Asset Management System prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 be strictly complied. Thus, require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network. Likewise, require the General Services Officer or in his absence, the municipal treasurer to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components. Moreover, create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System.
observations.managementAction: In compliance with the Audit Team’s recommendations, they created an Inventory Committee composed of staff from the Engineering, Treasury and Accounting office to conduct a physical inventory of all road networks based on their record. The Engineering Department was also directed to prepare a road map to identify the location of these roads and for the accounting office to prepare and submit the report on the physical count as soon as possible.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the management to strictly adhere to the provisions of PD 1177 that all expenses are duly authorized, adequately funded and documented; and to allow expenditures only within the limit of the appropriation to avoid incurrence of overdraft.

We have recommended the Municipal Accountant to submit the supporting documents establishing the impairment of Property Plant and Equipment totaling P391,852.05. Otherwise, reversal of impairment loss shall be effected.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the management the following:

Ensure that the PCF custodian closely monitor the utilization of the PCF and  replenishment  is made on a monthly basis or when it reaches the 25% or the need requires,  in accordance  with COA Circular 97-002 in order to avoid delays in the payment of obligations involving small amount.

The Municipal Accountant to adhere to the provisions of Section 48, Chapter 3, Volume I of the NGAS Manual in setting up and maintenance of Petty Cash Fund using the Imprest System. Upon replenishment, the expenses paid out of the petty cash fund shall be debited and credit to Cash in Bank.  The amount of the check to be issued shall be equal to the total amount disbursed out of the amount set up as Petty Cash Fund for the year.

We have recommended and the management agreed to furnish the Auditors copies of contracts together with all the supporting documents to the COA within five (5) working days from execution thereof pursuant to COA Circular No. 87-278 and COA Memorandum No. 2005-027 as reiterated in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We have recommended the following:

The accountable officers strictly adhere to the above cited provision to avoid the possible fraudulent use of accountable forms and ensure that all accountable forms in the custody of the accountable officers are kept diligently; and 

The Municipal Mayor direct the accountable officer to locate the missing Plates and Stickers. In the event that these can no longer be located/presented, a request for relief from accountability for the loss of accountable forms shall be made.

We further recommend that Management stop paying honoraria in addition to their fixed salaries, allowances and benefits to officials and employees for services rendered which are deemed part of their regular functions to avoid audit disallowances.

Accounts Payable that remained unpaid for two or more years may be reverted, if these payables can be ascertained to be without valid claims, pursuant to Section 98 of PD 1445.

Require those employees who are still in the service but identified and determined as one or among the persons liable to settle the audit disallowance.
