Direct the Municipal Accountant to observe the following items of this COA Circular, to wit: 1.1 Item 4.0 – Coverage 1.2 Item 6.0 – General Guidelines 1.3 Item 7.0 – Specific Guidelines 1.4 Item 8.0 – Procedures in the write-off of dormant accounts; 37. Enjoin compliance of the above-mentioned COA Circular.
observations.managementAction: The balance of P718,340.14 shall be reclassified to advances for officers and employees and the LGU shall determine the liable persons for it to be properly charged.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the
Municipal Treasurer, secure all stale checks and determine those requiring issuance of
replacement checks. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer cancel
the stale checks and furnish the Accounting Office with the original disbursement
vouchers as basis for the preparation of journal entry vouchers debiting the Cash in
Bank, and crediting Accounts Payable and Prior Years’ Adjustment for stale checks
less than two years and for those more than two years, respectively. We
recommended further that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant regularly
monitor all outstanding checks, send written notices to payees a month before
these become stale and prepare the necessary adjustments for the reconciling
items totaling P11,041,126.31.

We recommended that Management:
(d) Require all concerned accountable officers to immediately settle their
cash advances in accordance with the pertinent sections of COA
Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997;
(e) Direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor and strictly enforce the
liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period, and see to it that
no additional cash advance shall be allowed unless the previous ones are
settled or properly accounted for; and
(f) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular evaluation/
validation of all outstanding accounts; send demand letters to officers
and employees with long outstanding accounts, and impose sanctions
when necessary, such as withholding of their salaries as provided in the
Circular; and if warranted, request for write-off of long-overdue cash
advances in accordance with the procedures under COA Circular No.
2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct (a) the Inventory
Committee to conduct a physical count of inventories and movable properties of the
Municipality; (b) the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the report on the results of
the count using the prescribed form and reconcile the results with the accounting
records; and (c) the Municipal Treasurer, as property custodian, and the Municipal
Accountant to maintain stock/property cards and ledgers cards, respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the adjusting entries
to present the accurate balance of the RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET
Receivables in the financial statements. We further recommended that, henceforth,
the Municipal Accountant recognize discounts for advance and prompt payment of
RPT and the additional one percent tax accruing to the SEF in the year the taxes are
due in accordance with Section 22 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant capitalize all major repairs
or improvements of assets in accordance with generally accepted accounting
principles and COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 to present fairly
the financial statements of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management explain in writing why there appeared
to be splitting in the procurement of medicines and medical, dental and laboratory
supplies. Henceforth, we recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC)
refrain from splitting requisitions and procure goods through competitive bidding
in accordance with Section 10, Rule IV of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184, save only in
cases provided under Rule XVI of the IRR.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of spending for
catering services in relation to street dancing, games and sports competitions and
other activities for entertainment to highlight fiesta celebration to avoid incurring
unnecessary expenditures.

We recommended that Management return immediately to the source
agency the amount representing the full settlement of the audit disallowance on the
excessive use of the transferred funds in accordance with Section 6.7 of COA
Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer strictly observe the PS
limitation which shall not exceed 55% of the total annual income from regular
sources realized in the next preceding fiscal year pursuant to Section 325(a) of R.A.
No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer
to coordinate closely with the DENR for the immediate release of the SLUP and
CNC. Henceforth, ensure that required permits/clearances from other government
agencies are secured prior to project implementation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) consider the immediate
hiring of a Municipal Bookkeeper; (b) provide personnel assistance to the Office of
the Municipal Accountant; and (c) require the Municipal Accountant to submit
immediately to the Auditor the financial statements and supporting schedules as of
December 31, 2019.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain GLs and SLs in
accordance with Section 04 (e) of the Manual on the NGA) for Use in LGUs, Volume
I, to facilitate the preparation of accurate financial statements and verification
thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer remit taxes withheld from
compensation as well as from purchases of goods and services within ten (10) days
after at the end of each month in accordance with BIR Revenue Regulations No. 2-
98 dated April 17, 1998.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
The Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment and attach property tag/sticker for easy identification of government property and ensure its proper usage. Thereafter, submit the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Auditor for verification on the accuracy, ownership and existence of the reported PPE. Also, the inventory report should be reconciled with the accounting and property records and adjust records based on valid documents to reflect the correct balances of the PPE accounts.
The Municipal Accounting Office to continue tracing back the details of prior years’ acquisitions to support the recorded PPE.
The General Services Office (GSO) to maintain property cards to facilitate the physical count of PPE items. Determine the officials/employees to whom the equipment were issued and issue the corresponding Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) to pinpoint the direct person responsible thereof. Moreover, renew all ARE every three years to update the information of the end-user of the equipment listed in the RCPPE.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Treasury Office to monitor regularly the balances of cash in bank accounts before the preparation and issuance of checks to avoid the incurrence of abnormal balances and possible bank charges due to insufficiency of funds. Moreover, it is recommended that at least one month before a check becomes stale, the Municipal Treasurer send a written notice to the payee of the existence of the check to facilitate the replacement thereof. We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to take up the previous years’ reconciling items and cancellation of the stale checks.

We recommended that Management exert all efforts to locate the supporting documents or records to support the recorded receivables and enforce its collection. However, if remedies had been resorted to and the account remained uncollected, the Local Chief Executive may request authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off those dormant accounts from the books pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016. Moreover, for those that were just recently granted, the Management must be resolute in implementing the provisions of the Memorandum of Agreement especially the penalty clause to avoid the continuing accumulation of outstanding receivables.

We recommended that Management submit a written justification on the deficiencies noted. Moreover, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Engineering Office to conduct detailed engineering before approval of the Program of Work for proper identification of the items of work to be done to establish the reasonableness of cost estimates for materials and labor.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned contractors who undertook the implementation of the infrastructure projects to post a warranty security as prescribed under pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 to guarantee the performance of their responsibilities in case of structural defects and failures from the final acceptance of the infrastructure projects.

We recommended that Management procure common-use supplies and equipment which are necessary in its operations from the Procurement Service-Department of Budget and Management (PS-DBM) as required under Administrative Order No 17 dated July 28, 2011 not only to contribute to the attainment of a modernized and standardized government procurement system but also to allow personnel involved in procurement to devote their time in performing their regular functions instead of attending to the various stages of procurement from other sources. We also recommended that prior to procurement of common-use supplies from private suppliers, certifications indicating that the supply requirements of the Municipal Government are not available be secured from the depot.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Enforce the payment of fuel, oil and lubricants directly to the supplier in observance of sound principle of internal control on the disbursement process and in order to withhold the taxes due to the government;
Instruct the General Services Officer to assign a knowledgeable personnel to check the details or review the propriety and completeness of information on the trip tickets prior to official trips and to regularly submit Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to the Auditor thru the Municipal Accountant for purposes of verification on the reasonableness of fuel consumption; and
Require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to review and verify the completeness of documentary requirements for claims before affixing her signature on Box A of each disbursement voucher to ascertain the validity and propriety of disbursements made.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly enforce direct payments to suppliers/dealers in the procurement of goods in observance of sound internal control on the disbursement process and in order to withhold the amount of taxes due to the government as required in the  above cited revenue regulations.

We recommended that Management submit justification on the deficiencies noted and the lacking documents to determine the urgency and necessity of the rendered overtime services, otherwise, these will be suspended or disallowed in audit. Also, the Municipal Accountant should see to it that future transactions are supported with complete and proper documentation to establish their validity and propriety.
We also recommended that Management formulate internal rules and policies on the rendition of overtime services, which shall be circumscribed with the policies and procedures under CSC-DBM Joint Circular No. 1 dated November 25, 2015 to avoid the incurrence of irregular expenditures particularly on the granting of overtime pay to the department heads. Except for the entitlement to overtime pay, they are however not precluded from   rendering   overtime   services   whenever   the   exigency of the service requires.

We recommended that Management cause the immediate submission of a written justification on the observed deficiencies. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive request the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a resolution establishing a clear policy for each type of assistance in accordance with DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 02 dated February 17, 2014 to rationalize disbursements of funds allocated for this purpose.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the concerned officials of Ilocos Sur Polytechnic State College (ISPSC), Sta. Maria to submit the utilization/liquidation reports and relevant documents to ensure that the financial assistance given were used for the intended purposes, otherwise, these will be suspended in audit.  Moreover, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that financial assistance granted to other government agencies and non-government organizations are covered with a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) or its equivalent indicating the conditions, requirements and responsibilities of the recipients on the use of government funds. The grant of additional financial assistance should be based on the recipient’s compliance with the provisions of the MOA.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer to cause the immediate submission of the sixteen (16) disbursement vouchers and strictly observe the timely submission of accounts to facilitate review thereon and avoid the imposition of sanctions as provided under Section 39 (3) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and Awards Committee to comply with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 regarding the submission of copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders with their supporting documents to the Auditor within five (5) working days from execution and issuance thereof for review and evaluation so that management will be immediately informed of defects and deficiencies noted, if any and to facilitate the inspection of completed projects and deliveries of goods.

We recommended that Management render a written justification on the non-implementation of the programs/projects in accordance with the scheduled implementation period. Moreover, we recommended the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise tax and reappropriate the savings from the previous years’ implemented programs/projects in accordance with the authorized uses as prescribed in Republic Act Nos. 7171 and 8240 in order to give the constituents, especially the tobacco farmers the early benefits that could be derived therefrom.
We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive advise the Local Development Council to ensure that only feasible programs/projects/activities are included in the Annual Investment Program and require regular monitoring of implementation as basis in taking appropriate action.

We recommended that Management submit a written justification on the noted deficiencies. Moreover, we recommended that Management ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund to attain desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes of the Municipality and intended beneficiaries.
We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and other implementing offices to review the composition of the 20% Development Fund (DF), identify the savings from fully implemented projects and those which are no longer prioritized and, subject to the provisions of the Local Government Code and the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1, reappropriate to current/existing priority development projects. It is also recommended that Management ensure that the fund will not be used for any purpose beyond those expressly prescribed therein to avoid possible sanctions.

We recommended that Management submit a written justification on the noted deficiencies. Moreover, we recommended that Management ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund to attain desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes of the Municipality and intended beneficiaries.
We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and other implementing offices to review the composition of the 20% Development Fund (DF), identify the savings from fully implemented projects and those which are no longer prioritized and, subject to the provisions of the Local Government Code and the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1, reappropriate to current/existing priority development projects. It is also recommended that Management ensure that the fund will not be used for any purpose beyond those expressly prescribed therein to avoid possible sanctions.

We recommended that Management submit a written justification on the non-implementation of the programs/projects/activities. Moreover, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Person to closely monitor the implementation of programs/projects/activities under the GAD Plan in order to fully address gender issues/problems of the constituents in the municipal level and to help the national government attain its goal of addressing gender issues in the country.

We recommended that Management submit a written justification on the non-implementation of the programs/projects/activities. Moreover, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the LCPC to fully implement programs/projects/activities funded under the one percent (1%) internal revenue allotment geared towards the promotion and protection of the interest and welfare of children.

We recommended that Management establish GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation in order to respond effectively to the identified issues through the implementation of appropriate programs and projects.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the LDRRMO to submit monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund within the prescribed period to facilitate the evaluation of the utilization of the said fund.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive, through the LDRRMO, agreed to include the utilization of unexpended balance for DRRM, which is under the Trust Liability – DRRM, in the LDRRMFIP for programs/projects/activities related to disaster management and prevention.

We recommended that Management enforce the immediate settlement of the audit suspensions and disallowances as prescribed in the RRSA under COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009. Moreover, exhaust all available remedies in enforcing the immediate settlement of the unsettled PEI of retired employees. Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that supporting documents of transactions are complete, proper and valid in order to prevent the same from being suspended/ disallowed in post-audit.

We have recommended that the accounting and reporting guidelines on the Local Roads Asset Management System prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 be strictly complied. Thus, require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network. Likewise, require the General Services Officer or in his absence, the municipal treasurer to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components. Moreover, create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System.

We have recommended to the Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE) to create the Bids and Awards Committee with members’ qualifications basically compliant to the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184. The Head of the Procuring Entity shall see to it that the assignment order creating the BAC shall specify the designation of the Chairman and the Vice-Chairman who shall at least possess the required basic qualification.

We have recommended the following to the Management
To stop the practice of procuring and payment of goods and services through cash advance;
To enforce that all procurement of goods and services be paid by checks directly to suppliers/ contractors/ service providers except for petty disbursements out of petty cash fund in pursuance of COA Circular No. 97-002 so that appropriate taxes could be withheld from suppliers and could be remitted to the BIR Section 37 of Republic Act No. 10963; and
To instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly abide by the procurement processes provided under the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 such as the mandatory conduct of competitive bidding or the alternative methods of procurement subject to the prior approval of the HoPE and whenever justified by the conditions provided in the said Act.

We have recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to immediately comply with the prompt submission of  all  paid Disbursement Vouchers and the supporting documents to the Auditor for verification and timely conduct of audit as required in Section 100 of PD 1445 and Section 7.2.1.a of COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 to avoid the issuances of Notice of Suspension and/or Disallowance.

We have recommended the management to strictly adhere to the provisions of PD 1177 that all expenses are duly authorized, adequately funded and documented; and to allow expenditures only within the limit of the appropriation to avoid incurrence of overdraft.

We have recommended the Municipal Accountant to submit the supporting documents establishing the impairment of Property Plant and Equipment totaling P391,852.05. Otherwise, reversal of impairment loss shall be effected.

We have recommended to the management the following:
Ensure that the PCF custodian closely monitor the utilization of the PCF and  replenishment  is made on a monthly basis or when it reaches the 25% or the need requires,  in accordance  with COA Circular 97-002 in order to avoid delays in the payment of obligations involving small amount.
The Municipal Accountant to adhere to the provisions of Section 48, Chapter 3, Volume I of the NGAS Manual in setting up and maintenance of Petty Cash Fund using the Imprest System. Upon replenishment, the expenses paid out of the petty cash fund shall be debited and credit to Cash in Bank.  The amount of the check to be issued shall be equal to the total amount disbursed out of the amount set up as Petty Cash Fund for the year.

We have recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity direct the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report and approved by him and submit to the GPPB in compliance with Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We have recommended that the management comply with all the requirements pertaining to procurements set forth under COA Circular No. 2009-001 and Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act No.  9184
We further recommended the BAC to:
Post to PhilGEPS bulletin board all procurement opportunities, results of bidding such as Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and approved contract and other required information within the reglementary period; and
Invite observers in all staged of Bidding Process

We have recommended and the management agreed to furnish the Auditors copies of contracts together with all the supporting documents to the COA within five (5) working days from execution thereof pursuant to COA Circular No. 87-278 and COA Memorandum No. 2005-027 as reiterated in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We have recommended the following:
The accountable officers strictly adhere to the above cited provision to avoid the possible fraudulent use of accountable forms and ensure that all accountable forms in the custody of the accountable officers are kept diligently; and
The Municipal Mayor direct the accountable officer to locate the missing Plates and Stickers. In the event that these can no longer be located/presented, a request for relief from accountability for the loss of accountable forms shall be made.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer should discontinue the practice of charging prior year’s expenditure to current year appropriation and that proper matching of expenses against appropriation should always be observed pursuant to the above-cited regulations.

We have recommended that Management require Ms. De Gracia to refund the amount of P15,000.00 as the payment of her honoraria is not in consonance with DBM Local Budget Circular No. 62 and Section 58 of the General Provisions of Republic Act No. 11260 or the General Appropriations Act for Fiscal Year 2019.
We further recommend that Management stop paying honoraria in addition to their fixed salaries, allowances and benefits to officials and employees for services rendered which are deemed part of their regular functions to avoid audit disallowances.

We have recommended that Municipal Accountant to continue tracing and locating relevant documents to serve as basis in analyzing prior years’ reconciling items, and prepare necessary adjustment, if any and perform regular reconciliation of reciprocal accounts to prevent recording discrepancies.

We have recommended that the management exhaust all remedies to determine the details and whereabouts of supporting documents of Accounts Payable and Due to Officers and Employees accounts to ensure the accounts’ accuracy and legitimacy reflected in the financial statements.  Accounts Payable that remained unpaid for two or more years may be reverted, if these payables can be ascertained to be without valid claims, pursuant to Section 98 of PD 1445.

We have recommended that the Management require the Municipal Assessor to coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) on the immediate adoption of resolutions on the tax revisions of real property, to ensure higher RPT collections for the municipality to increase the needed funds for the budgeted programs, projects and activities.

We have recommended for the Management to closely coordinate with the District Supervisor for the implementation of the plans so as not to deprive the schools and students of their immediate beneficial use.

We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Accountant  prepare
the Monthly Bank Reconciliation  Statements  and  submit  the  same  to  the
Auditor within the period prescribed.  In view thereof, Bank  Statements  or
Snapshots, as the case may be, should be requested  immediately  to  reflect
the true and correct cash balance as of reporting date.

We recommended that the Management through the  Municipal  Assessor  provide
the Municipal Treasurer the assessment  roll  as  basis  for  the  Municipal
Treasurer in  collecting  the  real  property  tax.   Accordingly,  we  also
recommended that the  Municipal  Treasurer  update  its  real  property  tax
account register, and render collection notices to the concerned  taxpayers,
and  provide the Municipal Accountant a certified list of the taxpayers,  as
well as its total  amount  due  and  collectible  for  the  year  2019,  and
thereafter, provide such duly certified  list  at  the  beginning  of  every
year.

Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal  Accountant  record  immediately
the RPT and SET receivables  which  are  1%,  respectively,  of  the  annual
collectibles based from the assessed values of all realty subject to tax  as
soon as the Municipal Assessor and  the  Municipal  Treasurer  provide  such
list.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer deposit all
collections regularly and intact to avoid the accumulation of cash in her
hands and the  exposure  of  the  same  to  possible  risk  of  loss  and
misappropriation.

We recommended that the Bids  and  Awards  Committee  (BAC)  through  the
Technical Working Group (TWG) of the LGU  conduct the  post-qualification
following the prescribed procedures to serve its  purpose,  thus,  ensure
that all projects are awarded to and  undertaken  by  bidders/contractors
which do not only offer the lower or lowest bid but which are also  found
complying  and  responsive  to  all  the   pertinent   requirements   and
conditions.

We recommended that the Management through the BAC Secretariat submit the
purchase orders  and  government  contracts  including  their  supporting
documents to the Office of the Auditor within the period prescribed under
COA Circular No. 2009-001 to  facilitate  the  systematic  and  effective
review thereof.

We recommended that Management through the  Municipal  Accountant  ensure
that payments of honoraria to contracted consultants  be  supported  with
complete documentation to prove  the  validity,  legality  and  propriety
thereof. As regards the payments made to the three  consultants  for  the
period  January  to  June  2019,  Management  submits  their  Record   of
Attendance/Service and their specific  duties  and  responsibilities  for
verification.

We recommended that Management through the  Municipal  Accountant  ensure
that all procurements of the LGU be supported with complete documentation
as required by existing rules and  regulations.  It  is  emphasized  that
completeness of documentation  means  not only  the  submission  of  the
documents but also  the  completeness  and  correctness  of  the  details
thereof.

In this regard, the lacking and necessary explanatory documents  for  the
procurement  of  medicine  amounting  to  P198,700.00  be  submitted  for
verification, otherwise,  the disbursement may be suspended or disallowed
in audit.

We recommended that Management through the  Municipal  Accountant  ensure
that all  procurements  of  the  Municipality  charged  against  the  20%
Development Fund be supported with complete documentation as  prescribed.
In this  regard,  the  lacking  documents  for  the  final  payments  re:
infrastructure projects  amounting  to  P1,597,284.56  be  submitted  for
verification, otherwise, the disbursements may be suspended or disallowed
in audit.

We recommended that Management through the Sangguniang Bayan refrain from
reimbursing meals and snacks incurred  during  the  SB  member’s  regular
sessions   as  these  are  supposedly   covered   by   their   respective
Representation Allowances (RA).

As  regards  the  reimbursements  of  meals  and  snacks   amounting   to
P42,816.00, the concerned members of the SB  should be  required  by  the
Management to refund the same, otherwise, a Notice of  Disallowance  (ND)
will be issued.

We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Accountant  take
up funds granted to schools for specified purposes as “Due from NGAs” for
fair presentation of accounts in  the  books  as  well  as  to  establish
accountability and demand for liquidation.

We recommended that the Management through the  Local  School  Board  and
Municipal accountant ensure that the utilization of the Special Education
Fund (SEF) should be supported with complete documentation as  prescribed
to prove the validity, legality and propriety thereof.  In  this  regard,
the lacking documents, including the corresponding liquidation documents,
amounting to P60,000.00 be submitted  for  verification,  otherwise,  the
transaction may be suspended or disallowed in audit.

Recommendation:

We recommended that Management prioritize the filing-up of  the  position
of  Municipal  Treasurer.  Once  a  full-time  Municipal   Treasurer   is
appointed, Ms. Panganiban will resume as  full-time  Municipal  Assessor.
Having personnel to perform duties and responsibilities  on  a  full-time
capacity may  facilitate  the  flow  of  operations  and  strengthen  the
internal control system of the LGU.

We recommended that Management  revisit  the  opening  of  the  new  bank
account for and in the  name  of  the  Offices  of  the  Vice  Mayor  and
Sangguniang Bayan of the LGU, thus, consider what is more  practical  and
efficient for the LGU.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal
Accountant to: (a) issue the appropriate demand letters to the persons responsible
for the unauthorized disbursement; and, (b) prepare the necessary adjusting entries
thereof.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a.) Direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounting records with
supplies and inventory records of the General Services Office; and,
b.) Require the Municipal Accountant to undertake the necessary adjusting
entries to reflect the result of reconciliation with the GSO.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to compute for the proper depreciation
of the PPE items through the preparation of lapsing schedule and to
subsequently prepare the corresponding adjusting entry, and;
b. Require the Municipal Accounting Office to maintain Property Ledger
Cards.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
c. Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and
the General Services Officer with the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects; and,
d. Direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize the cost of each component
of the local roads with identified cost and subsequently provide the
proper depreciation thereof in the books of accounts.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a.) Direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounting records with
property records of the General Services Office; and,
b.) Require the Municipal Accountant to undertake the necessary adjusting
entries to reflect the result of reconciliation with the GSO.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to review the monthly computation and remittance of the Retirement
and Life Insurance Premiums (50103010) account to ensure compliance with existing
regulations.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal
Accountant to provide for the required disclosures under the Philippine Public
Sector Accounting Standards in the preparation of the Notes to the Financial
Statements.

observations.managementAction: AAR were already submitted.

Contract duration days were properly filled-up
A Disbursing Officer was already designated.
Cash in Vault balances were already adjusted.

Reports were not submitted monthly.
Financial reports were not submitted on time and supported with incomplete schedules.
Per letter received from the District Supervisor, the subject expenditures were allowable charges to the SEF.

RPT shares were remitted to the barangays with respective barangays.

observations.managementAction: The SWO I was designated as GAD Focal Person.

observations.managementAction: The grant of the allowance was already stopped and partial settlements were already made.
observations.reason: Unsettled disallowances belong to former employees who were either deceased or separated from the service.

observations.managementAction: Repayments were deducted from the payroll.
observations.reason: Unsettled disallowances belong to former employees who were either deceased or separated from the service.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of physical count of the PPE as of December 31 of every year, reconcile the difference between the financial statements and inventory report of properties and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to the Auditor for verification. Likewise, the prescribed records/forms for PPE accounts should be maintained by the Property Officer and the Accounting Office to conform to the provisions on the Supply and Property Management in the LGUs.
observations.managementAction: RPCPPE was submitted and reconciliation of records was made.
observations.reason:
Physical count of PPE was not conducted during the year, thus the reliability of the RPCPPE with total amount of P1,122,607,130.82 is doubtful. 

Reiterated in Observation No. 1, page 35 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit to the Auditor all the disbursement vouchers listed in Annex “B”, duly supported with complete and valid documents pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2009-006 and 95-006 for post-audit of same.
observations.managementAction: Five (5) DVs were submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: One (1) DV was not submitted yet for post-audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly adhere to the above provisions of law in handling of government funds. Collections must be deposited daily and intact or not later than the next banking day to safeguard the funds from the risks of possible misuse/ misappropriation/ loss.
observations.managementAction: Some collections were still not deposited daily
observations.reason: This observation will be reiterated upon completion of the cash examination conducted in December 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, Liquidating Officer and collectors perform strictly their duties and functions in the receipt, remittance and verification of collections and accountable forms to avoid delayed remittances of collections.
observations.managementAction: Some collections were still not recorded and remitted promptly.
observations.reason: This observation will be reiterated upon completion of the cash examination conducted in December 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly observe the proper handling of collections and the issuance of official receipts to the collectors to avoid incurrence of cash shortage. Likewise, she should strictly adhere to the laws, rules and regulations in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer still failed to strictly observe the proper handling of collections and the issuance of official receipts to the collectors.
observations.reason: Cash shortages for collections and advances to officers and employees in the amount of P495,517.49 and P1,619,725.00 were discovered on the cash examination conducted on December 10, 2018 for the period December 21, 2017 to December 10, 2018.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

Require the concerned officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances to settle immediately their accounts; otherwise, consider the imposition of sanctions such as the withholding of their salaries as provided for in Section 9.3.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002;

Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a single disbursement voucher for cash advance granted to an accountable officer for a certain program/activity; and

Adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The amount of P4,457,853.99 or 93.11% of the total unliquidated cash advances was liquidated during the year.
observations.reason: Partially complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce that all payments must be made by check directly to suppliers/ contractors in the disbursement of government funds, except for petty disbursements out of the petty cash fund.
observations.managementAction: Some purchases were still made on reimbursement basis.
observations.reason:
Thirty-eight (38) purchases of goods with an aggregate amount of P740,193.62 were procured on reimbursement basis.

Reiterated in Observation No. 9, page 53 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce direct payment to suppliers or dealers in the procurement of goods in order to withhold the amount of taxes due to the government pursuant to Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007. As one of the withholding agents, the tax should be computed and reflected on the face of the disbursement voucher and the corresponding Certificate of Withholding Tax Form be issued to concerned suppliers.
observations.managementAction: Taxes were not withheld from such transactions.
observations.reason:
Management was not able to comply with the audit recommendation.

Reiterated in Observation No. 9, page 53 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Chairman, who is also the Municipal Engineer, require the BAC Secretariat to furnish the Auditor a copy of the contracts and all supporting documents within five (5) working days after execution thereof.
observations.managementAction: Contracts were submitted beyond the prescribed period. On the other hand, purchase orders were only submitted upon submission of the disbursement vouchers to which these were attached as supporting documents.
observations.reason:
Sixty-three (63) contracts amounting to P122,124,740.32 and purchase orders together with their supporting documents were not submitted to the Auditor within five (5) days from execution and issuance thereof.

Reiterated in Observation No. 14, page 63 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality adhere strictly to the provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184 to ensure transparency, accountability, efficiency and economy in its procurement process.
observations.managementAction: There were still pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 which were not complied with.
observations.reason: Partially complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant see to it that disbursement vouchers are duly supported with the required basic documents to ensure their legality, validity and propriety.
observations.managementAction: Some claims were still processed and paid despite the absence of complete documentation.
observations.reason: Notices of Suspension were issued for the non-submission of requisite documents of post-audited transactions for the months of January to September 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU adhere to the provisions of the existing laws, rules and regulations regarding the payment of RATA of members of the local sanggunian and other municipal officials and employees. We further recommended that the RATA received during the absences of the concerned Sangguniang Bayan Members be refunded.
observations.managementAction: Out of the total disallowance of P217,040.00, the amount of P82,640.00 was refunded by the concerned officials of the Municipality.
observations.reason:
Partially complied with the audit recommendation.

Notices of Disallowance were issued for the excessive payments of RATA during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly impose the provisions of the Revised Market Code of the Municipality of Sta. Maria, Ilocos Sur particularly on the collection of the market rental fees. Strictly impose the collection of the 20% surcharge/ penalty on late payments so that the stallholders are compelled to pay their obligations on time. The collection of the 20% surcharge/ penalty means additional income to the LGU. Furthermore, the Municipal Treasurer, together with the Market Committee, should be more responsible in the administration and management of the operations of the public market to attain the stated declared policy in the Revised Market Code of the Municipality of Sta. Maria, Ilocos Sur which is to make the public market more responsive and effective instrument of public service, as well as dynamic and viable enterprise to strengthen its economic potential, with the ultimate goal of contributing significantly to local and national development.
observations.managementAction: Stallholders are required to pay their unpaid rentals before they can renew their permits in January of each year.
observations.reason: Partially complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the prompt implementation of all projects/ programs funded out of the tobacco excise taxes in order for the tobacco farmers to enjoy the benefits they rightfully deserve.
observations.managementAction: Several projects funded in CY 2018 were implemented in CY 2019.
observations.reason:
There were still thirty-two (32) unimplemented programs/ projects with a total appropriation of P115,957,205.00 from RA 7171 funds received in CY 2018.

Reiterated in Observation No. 15, page 64 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal officials and employees concerned implement fully all the programs/ projects embodied under the 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: Implemented four programs/ projects during the year.
observations.reason:
The amount of P32,164,358.46, which represents 58.68 percent of the total appropriations of P54,813,135.09 for the said fund was not implemented as of year-end.

Reiterated in Observation No. 16, page 66 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Chairperson of the LCPC to fully implement programs/ projects/ activities funded under the one percent (1%) internal revenue allotment geared towards the promotion and protection of interest and welfare of children in the country.
observations.managementAction: Funds were not fully utilized during the year.
observations.reason:
Five (5) out of the eight (8) programs/ projects/ activities under the one percent (1%) of the internal revenue allotment for the programs of the LCPC for CY 2019 were either partially or not implemented at all.

Reiterated in Observation No. 18, page 73 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Chairperson of the GAD Focal Point System to fully implement programs/ projects/ activities under the GAD Plan in order to fully address gender issues/ problems of the constituents in the municipal level and to help the national government attain its goal of addressing gender issues in the country.
observations.managementAction: Funds were not fully utilized during the year.
observations.reason:
Programs/ projects/ activities totaling P2,895,300.00 or 53.91% of the total appropriation for GAD were not implemented during the year.

Reiterated in Observation No. 17, page 70 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRM Council pass a resolution reverting the CYs 2011 and 2012 LDRRMF unspent balances to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund, for adoption by the Sangguniang Bayan. Further, that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer prepare the necessary transfer of funds and accounting reports to facilitate the said reversion and certify to the availability of said funds for subsequent reappropriation for social services.
observations.managementAction: Complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MDRRMO submit monthly Report on Source and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on time in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.
observations.reason:
The required report was not submitted monthly.

Reiterated in Observation No. 20 page 76 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management exhaust all available remedies provided under the laws, rules and regulations in enforcing the immediate compliance to the audit suspensions and the settlement of the disallowances as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.
observations.reason: Balances remained unsettled as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the municipality conduct physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment and submit a copy of the report to the Audit Team. To facilitate the conduct of physical count of properties, the inventory committee may consider performing physical count of newly acquired properties before moving to those that were acquired in the prior years. Inventory tagging should also be made in order to prevent double counting.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant explained that they forgot to the conduct the physical inventory last January 2019. But as of February 2019, they already sent each office of the municipality checklists of assets to check their existence and commit themselves to submit the Report on the Physical Count of Plant, Property and Equipment (RCPPE) immediately.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the management discontinue granting cash advances to the Local Chief Executive for purposes other than for official travelling expenses as stated in Item 4.1.4 of COA Circular 97-002. Likewise, the management is advised to refrain from granting the same cash advance to other elected officials in strict observance to said rules.
observations.managementAction: The municipality will no longer allow the LCE to be granted cash advance except for traveling purposes
observations.reason: The agency has stopped granting cash advances, except for traveling, to anyone except for bonded personnel

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the municipality comply with the law particularly on the remittance of priority liabilities so the municipality could avoid interest, penalties and surcharges for not remitting the full amount withheld from their employees.
observations.managementAction: To see to it that taxes withheld, GSIS, Pagibig and Philhealth  contributions are remitted accordingly on the subsequent month they are collected  .
observations.reason: Due to lack of available records, there are still dormant accounts in their financial statements for verification

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that SB members should not be allowed to claim gasoline subsidy because they are already receiving monthly RATA in compliance with Section 343 of RA 7160 and Item 3.13, Annex B of COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012, prohibiting unauthorized expenditures.
observations.managementAction: The municipality accepted the audit recommendation to no longer allow the members of Sanggunian Bayan to claim gasoline subsidy

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Municipal officials assigned with government service vehicles should no longer be allowed payment of Transportation Allowance as provided under Item 8.2.1 of Local Budget Circular No. 103.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant explained that the government service vehicles issued to an official/employee are only for safekeeping of the vehicle and not as their own personal service. Anybody in the municipality may use the   government service vehicles when needed. The municipal mayor then instructed the municipal officials to inform the concerned employees to comply to our audit recommendation.
observations.reason: The Official vehicle of the Sangguniang Bayan and the vehicle of the LDRRMF were returned to the custody of the property custodian.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended management to comply with the guidelines provided by PPSAS 1, “Presentation of Financial Statements” and record the honorarium of the traffic enforcers in their proper classifications to avoid misleading representation of the accounts. It was further recommended that the LGU strictly comply with the policy and guidelines under Section 119 of PD 1445, requiring that all lawful expenditures and obligations incurred during the year shall be taken up in the accounts of that year.
observations.managementAction: The journal entries that were plotted as Other MOOE in the journals, thus not the same with what the entries written in the Disbursement Vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the management thru its Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant charge only allowable items against the Fund following the guidelines under the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 on the allocation and utilization of the 5% LDRRMF. As the payment of the honorarium paid was inappropriately charged against the LDRRM, this could be subjected to disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The municipality will follow the guidelines provided under certain circulars.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the municipality should practice prudence in disbursing government funds by refraining the use of government funds and resources for personal purposes. The salary of the employee of the cooperative must be charged to the funds of the cooperative and not on the municipality. It was further recommended that the total amount of the salary of Ms. Merlita Soriano should be returned to the LGU by the cooperative otherwise a Notice of Disallowance will be issued.
observations.managementAction: The municipality will reassign the employee to another department of the lgu.
observations.reason: The concerned employee was already assigned in economics department but the Cooperative still did not pay the first installment as their action to return the amount paid to the LGU Employee

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Accountant ensure that travelling expenses are made within the prescribed limits in accordance with EO 298. The municipality should find ways to minimize the cost and optimize the performance of duties of its officers and employees for an effective and efficient organization.
observations.managementAction: The municipality will implement the EO 77 strictly
observations.reason: They have adopted the provisions of EO 77 in line with the IRR issued by DILG and formulated an internal guide to strictly comply to the said guidelines

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the management to:

Strictly adhere to Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of EO 292 on the guidelines on Monetization of Leave Credits to avoid future Notice of Suspensions and/or Disallowances.

In granting special monetization, follow strictly the conditions that the monetization applied for was 50% or more of the accumulated sick leave balances and presence of any of the valid and justifiable reasons.
observations.managementAction: To adhere to the provisions of EO 292 in granting monetization of Sick and vacation leave benefits.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the public officials or employees of the municipality to avoid conflicts of interest at all times as required under Section 9 of Republic Act No. 6713 by not engaging their cooperative to participate in the procurement within its agency.
observations.managementAction: To limit the purchase of supplies from the cooperative to a certain threshold and only on emergency cases
observations.reason: The agency limits their purchases to the cooperative to a P2,000.00 per transaction. Other Office  and food supplies purchases were bought from outside suppliers

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer discontinue the practice of charging prior year’s expenditure to current year appropriation and that proper matching of expenses against appropriation should always be observed pursuant to the above-cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: Verify the accounting records to ascertain the correct account used on those type of transactions

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to thoroughly review payroll of employees availing loans before certifying documentary requirements for loan applications.  They have to ensure that the minimum monthly net take home pay of employees as required under the annual GAA is met so that the said employees/ officials could still provide a few thousand pesos to spend on the family’s needs.
observations.managementAction: To adhere the recommendation that employees should receive their monthly salary within the minimum threshold of P5,000.00
observations.reason: For January to June 2019, all employees were receiving net take home pay of at least P5,000.00

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the management furnish the Auditors copies of contracts together with all the supporting documents to the COA within five (5) working days from execution thereof pursuant to COA Circular No. 87-278 and COA Memorandum No. 2005-027 as reiterated in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: The Bid and Awards Committee to facilitate the immediate preparation of documents immediately after bidding to be able to submit the perfected contracts on time.
observations.reason: Records of this office showed that the municipality did not yet submit contracts entered into by the Municipality for CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the management to exert more effort to fully implement the GAD plans, programs, and activities to obtain the maximum benefit that can be derived for the agency’s constituents.
observations.managementAction: To assign personnel to specifically monitor the implementation of GAD programs and activities
observations.reason: GAD implementation will be verified on yearend.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountable Officer deposit all collections regularly and intact to avoid the accumulation of cash in her hands and the exposure of the same to possible risk of loss and misappropriation.
observations.reason: During the cash examination, it was found that the designed collectors did not remit some of their collections. However, upon demand, the collectors committed

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly observe the pertinent provisions of COA Circular Nos. 97-002 on the granting and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.reason: It is noteworthy that all of the outstanding cash advances as of December 31, 2019, except for the account of Mr. Masigan, pertain to 2019. However, these were not liquidated within the supposed date of liquidation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Management look into the nature and condition of the receivable account and prioritize the collection thereof. If warranted, the Municipal Mayor may request for authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off the receivable following the specific guidelines and procedures as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005, thus, present fairly the affected accounts in the books of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Management created a fact-finding investigative committee to look into the account. A report was made with recommendation that Management requests authority to write off the account.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant identify the component transactions and set up a subsidiary ledger each for the accounts Donations and Other MOOE amounting to P17,802,608.79 and P8,070,083.21, respectively, to establish the reliability thereof and to present fairly the accounts in the books of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management prioritize the regular and timely payment of its loan amortization with DOF-MDFO to avoid the possible incurrence of surcharge and/or penalties and to establish good credit standing for the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Updated payment as of Sept 2019 per billing statement (payment was before due date)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that all disbursements for supplemental feeding be supported with complete documentation as prescribed to prove the validity and accuracy thereof. In this regard, the required lacking documents for the disbursements amounting to P1,284,976.00 be submitted for verification. Should the documents be not submitted or found not in order, the transactions may be suspended or disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management ensure that the utilization of the Special Education Fund (SEF) should be in accord with the approved budget and are supported with complete documentation as prescribed to prove the validity, legality and propriety thereof. In this regard, the lacking documents including the corresponding liquidation documents for the fund transfers amounting to P461,586.00 be submitted for verification. Should the documents be not submitted or found not in order, the transactions may be suspended or disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management install and maintain a system of internal control for the procurement, recording and monitoring of supplies and property, plant and equipment to ensure that these are duly accounted for.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the preparation of plan/budget and reporting of LDRRMF. Accordingly, to ensure effective and timely audit of the fund, the LDRRMO: prepare the LDRRMFIP following the prescribed format; prepare the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the prescribed format (certified correct by the Municipal Accountant); and submit copy of the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to COA within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: The Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF in 2019 were submitted to the Office of the Auditor, though not timely. However, still the LDRRMFIP for 2019 did not show the itemized or detailed programs, projects, and activities (PPAs) for the unused fund in prior years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prepare the LGU’s Annual GAD Plan and Budget using the prescribed format, i.e. to include information on the gender issues or gender mandates, GAD objective, and attributed Programs. Also, the gender issues must be based on the LGU’s sex disaggregated data (SDD).

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant ensure timely submission of accounts. Accordingly, she should cause the immediate submission of the unsubmitted accounts of the LGU for 2018 to facilitate the audit of the transactions as well as to avoid the possible suspension or disallowance of the same and the imposition of administrative disciplinary sanctions.
observations.reason: The timeliness of submission of the LGU’s accounts in 2019 improved. However, still, their accounts in some months were not submitted on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management revisits the grant of financial assistance to other LGUs especially to those belonging to higher income classification considering the financial capacity of the LGU. If warranted, Management may also make proper representation with the other LGUs in the Province of Isabela for the evaluation of the practice on exchange of financial assistance between and among the LGUs in the province.

We recommended the following courses of action: 1. Require the Accountant to submit monthly Trial Balance and its supporting documents and reports within the deadline as stated in the above Circular to give ample time for the audit team to conduct necessary audit procedures on time and provide the agency of the needed information which could be vital in their decision making; 2. Enjoin compliance on the existing laws, rules and regulations pertaining to the submission of financial reports and other reports relevant to the operation of the Municipality, otherwise, failure on the part of the officials concerned to submit the documents and reports mentioned herein shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission (Presidential Decree 1445 Section 122).
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepares bank recon at month end while the bank statements from the depository bank is released 2 to 3 months from the statements date thus making the reconciliation with the bank is further delayed.
observations.reason: Delayed bank statements

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following courses of actions: 5. Reprimand the person responsible to regularly prepare and submit the mentioned Utilization Reports to the Office of the State Auditor for audit purposes; 6. Ensure submission of the reportorial requirements set forth under existing laws, rules and regulations affecting financial affairs of the government, otherwise, failure on the part of the officials concerned to submit the documents and reports mentioned herein shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission (Sec. 122, P.D 1445).
observations.managementAction: The LGU has prepared the utilization of DRRMF, SEF and 20% LDF quarterly and the same were uploaded to DILG portal for SGLG, thus the LGU shall furnish copy.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the following courses of action: 7. Apply the qualification standard requirement in governing contract of services and job order worker in the government, is optional to the LGU. 8. Adopt the Rules Implementing Wage Order No. RB XI – 20 dated July 16, 2018 immediately and any issuances that updates the same; and 9. The application of CSC/COA/DBM Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 dated June 15, 2017, pertaining to the new rules and regulations governing contract of services and job order worker in the government, is optional to the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has raised its hiring rates of the Job Orders from P370.00 to P396.00 starting January 2019 for its old hired employees identifying the rightful worker to paid with the minimum wage pay as defined in the above CSC Resolution; Adopt the Rules Implementing Wage Order No. RB XI – 20 2018 immediately and any issuances that updates the same; and The application of CSC/COA/DBM Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 dated June 15, 2017, pertaining to the new rules and regulations governing contract of services and job order worker in the government, is optional to the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following courses of actions: 10. Give utmost emphasis on prioritizing the construction of the Sanitary Landfill and management which is cheaper than construction of SLF. 11. Prepare and submit to this Office the approved Solid Waste Management Plan and observe the submission of Utilization Reports on the prescribed period; and 12. Enjoin strict adherence to the provisions of the R.A. No. 9003 in the operation and maintenance of the sanitary landfill.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO has come up with alternative option in its garbage operation by implementing shredding machine for residual waste and construct diversion facility which is cheaper than construction of SLF.
observations.reason: Has to purchase shredding machine for residual waste and construct diversion facility which is a possible solution to its residual waste

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following courses of actions: 13. Direct the Municipal Accountant and the General Service Officer to perform the following: a. maintain the property cards and property ledger cards to all LGU’s properties and the Accounting Office, respectively, b. expedite the process of reconciliation of both records and reports in order to arrive at the correct carrying balances of this account; c. identify the unaccounted and accounted but not existing properties to satisfy its verifiability and reliability; and d. determine the person responsible to the properties not already existing so that appropriate remedies to settle their respective accountability may be applied immediately. 14. Ensure agreement of Accounting Records and General Services Records with respect to the result of conducted physical inventory count of stocks and properties; 15. Regularly update the property card and the property ledger card maintained in General Services Office and Accounting Office, respectively, so that monitoring of the properties’ whereabouts is regularly made; and 16. Perform the necessary yearly physical inventory count of properties, and submit the necessary reports to this Office for monitoring and compliance purposes.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has maintain its ledger cards but needs to update this 2019 procurement of property and implemented
observations.reason: To update 2019 PPE ledger cards

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following courses of action: 17. Furnish this Office the Copies of the Contracts and the corresponding documents that supports the regularity and legality of the contracts entered by the parties; and 18. Regularly furnish this Office the copies of Contracts and all documents relative thereto within five (5) working days after execution thereof for review and evaluation purposes.
observations.managementAction: The General Support Services already submitted its contracts with the LGU purchases and the corresponding MOA with its suppliers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following courses of actions: 19. Establish time frame for the concerned Municipal personnel to supply the Accounting Section with the necessary documents pertaining to the implementation of Municipal projects, programs and activities immediately after its completion; 20. Provide the Accounting Section an ample time to scrutinize the documents for regularity, legality and completeness before submitting to this Office after recording the transactions to the Municipality’s books of account on 10th day of the ensuing month; and 21. Enjoin compliance on the above-mentioned law to preclude this Office from the issuance of appropriate notice.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already submitted the supporting documents with its lacking vouchers to the commission, furthermore the vouchers are reviewed and forwarded to receiving departments for check issuance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following courses of actions: 22. Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately the Bank Reconciliation Statements of all accounts affected herein and cause prompt submission of Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) for all bank accounts of the Municipality to the COA Audit Team onwards; 23. Secure the monthly Bank Statements and the copies of Credit and/or Debit Memos from the bank to be attached to the JEVs as supporting documents; and 24. Effect the recording of all new accounts in the books.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepares bank recon at month end while the bank statements from the depository bank is released 2 to 3 months from the statements date thus making the reconciliation with the bank is further delayed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following courses of actions: 25. Establish time frame for the concerned Municipal personnel to supply the Accounting Section with the necessary documents pertaining to the implementation of Municipal projects, programs and activities immediately after its completion; 26. Provide the Accounting Section an ample time to scrutinize the documents for regularity, legality and completeness before submitting to this Office after recording the transactions to the Municipality’s books of account on 10th day of the ensuing month; 27. Appoint a personnel to oversee this deficiency considering the OIC-Municipal Accountant is still undergoing medication as to date; and 28. Enjoin compliance on the above-mentioned law to preclude this Office from the issuance of appropriate notice.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has submitted the disbursement vouchers to the commission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the immediate remittance of RPT shares to the Provincial Government of Davao Occidental including the Municipality’s Barangays shares; We also recommended the proper coordination between the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant which is needed to efficiently address the issue on the delayed remittances; Lastly, we recommended to institute a system that could improve the process of remitting RPT shares to concerned LGUs in a systematic manner.
observations.managementAction: The RPT shares were remitted a month after calendar year to the barangays and Provincial Government of Davao Occidental.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following courses of action: 32. Direct the Municipal Accountant to secure any documents that would be considered as evidence to claim ownership of the assets derived from the fund transfers; 33. Draw a Journal Entry Voucher(JEV) to capitalize these assets as part of the economic resources of the Municipality; 34. Any evidence showing that the ownership of these assets would be the source agency return or transfer these assets to them; and 35. Enjoin compliance of the above-mentioned rules and regulations to avoid understatement of entity’s economic resources at year-end.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant shall recognize the fund transfers from different agencies after utilizing 100% thus in progress projects were still it in progress accounts and not transferred to its proper accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following courses of action: 38. Facilitate the immediate processing of transfer of ownership to government’s programs and projects; 39. observations.managementAction: The LGU had already secured the deed of donation before proceeding in its implementation of programs and projects.

Ensure that subsequent purchases are initially recorded to its inventory account and charged to expense account only upon issuance to end-users.

This enables the Municipality to plot efficient and effective methods in waste management within its jurisdiction to provide a safe, clean and green environment to the constituents of this Municipality.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant to provide and maintain PPE Ledger Cards for each class or category of assets which provide details of the property recorded in the General Ledger and to analyze and reconcile discrepancies between the Accounting Office and General Services Office records and make the necessary adjustments, if any; and the Municipal General Services Officer to number each type of supplies and maintain Stock Cards per stock number, provide and maintain Property Cards per PPE and reconcile the results of physical count with the Ledger Cards of the accounting records to establish the reliability and existence of the PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to correctly classify the outstanding balance of the long-term loans payable by presenting the portion of loans due for settlement within twelve months as part of the current liabilities and the remaining which is due for settlement beyond one year as non-current liabilities in the Statement of Financial Position.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken on the recommendation.
observations.reason: Loans Payable – Domestic were all classified under Financial Liabilities – Non Current.

observations.recommendation: Issue an executive order for the creation of the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of all the properties of the municipality to establish the actual existence of these assets, for the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records and to effect the necessary adjustments in the book and to submit the inventory report to COA.
observations.managementAction: 2017 No action initiated by Management
observations.reason: There was no inventory report submitted.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to hasten the preparation of the CY 2017 financial reports and submit these to COA within the deadlines. We further recommend that, henceforth, Management closely monitor compliance on the submission of monthly and quarterly financial reports with complete schedules in accordance with the requirement of pertinent laws and regulations. Otherwise, we recommend for the withholding of salaries of concerned officials pursuant to Section 122 of P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction: 2017 No action initiated by Management
observations.reason: Year-end financial statements and reports were not submitted timely.

Initiate relending of said assistance to qualified applicants duly screened by a committee created for that purpose. Make representation with the local law-making body for possible write-off of accounts deemed uncollectible.
observations.managementAction: 2002 No bills of collections sent to borrowers due to the absence of list of borrowers.
observations.reason: Review of financial records confirmed that there was no subsidiary ledger to support verifiable records to identify the program beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation: Immediately organize an Internal Audit Service (IAS) to comply with Section 4 of AO No. 70.
observations.managementAction: 2013 Organization of the Internal Audit Service (IAS) was not initiated by the Municipality.
observations.reason: No IAS unit is currently functioning in the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to make a quarterly reconciliation of their cash balances so that discrepancies can be adjusted immediately to present accurate and reliable cash balances.
observations.managementAction: 2013 No reconciliation of cash balances has been initiated by the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Account.
observations.reason: Review of financial records/ reports still showed an unreconciled balances between cash book and ledger balances.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Supply Officer designee to furnish the auditor with the approved purchase orders and notify her of the deliveries of supplies within 24 hours from issuance of such purchase orders and acceptance of deliveries, so that surprise and selective inspection of the deliveries be made.
observations.managementAction: 2006 There was no action on the part of the Management specifically on the part of the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Auditor with approved POs.
observations.reason: No approved POs submitted hence no delivery has been inspected.

observations.managementAction: The management will give priority in construction Sanitary Land Fill this budget year 2020, and shall submit Solid waste management utilization reports
observations.reason: Not considered in the Preparation of 2019 budget.

observations.managementAction: Bid Awards Committee commits to adhere with the RA 9184 regarding the procurement of Fuel

We recommend that the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant should closely coordinate to each other so that physical count and tracing of the PPEs will be made.

	We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant must compute and provide depreciation for all property, plant and equipments under special education fund, in the same manner on how his PPEs in general fund and trust fund were depreciated, in order to present the accounts fairly in the financial statements.
