However, he promised to facilitate the tracing of those assets so that depreciation will be provided and the financial statement will be fairly presented.
observations.reason: No set up of depreciation, accounts were still verified

observations.recommendation:
1. Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain special accounts in the General Fund for the operation of the different economic enterprises of the municipality, as required in Section 313 of RA 7160and Sections 105-110 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Units. 

2. The guidelines in Sections 65-66 of COA Circular No. 92-382 should be strictly followed so that the true financial status of these income-generating activities can be identified and evaluated and generation of financial reports pertaining thereto could be facilitated.
observations.managementAction: No action taken
observations.reason: Eco Enterprise is not yet institutionalized

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant requested Inventory stock cards from GSO for proper accounting of inventory using the Perpetual Method.
observations.reason: GSO has not forwarded stock cards due to all stocks purchased were issued to requesting department.

observations.recommendation:
Make representations with the Home Mutual Development Fund (HDMF) office for any arrangements and agreements that could still save the project from its total loss since the foreclosed properties can still be redeemed within one (1) year period.

Determine the officials and employees adjudged liable for the failure to comply with the requirements set by HDMF in order to pinpoint responsibility and liability and appropriate legal and administrative action be instituted against them.
observations.managementAction: No action taken
observations.reason: The LGU is avoiding addition loss on settling its obligation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of physical count of the PPE as of December 31 of every year, reconcile the difference between the financial statements and inventory report of properties and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to the Auditor for verification. Likewise, the prescribed records/forms for PPE accounts should be maintained by the Property Officer and the Accounting Office to conform to the provisions on the Supply and Property Management in the LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit to the Auditor all the disbursement vouchers listed in Annex “B”, duly supported with complete and valid documents pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2009-006 and 95-006 for post-audit of same.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly adhere to the above provisions of law in handling of government funds. Collections must be deposited daily and intact or not later than the next banking day to safeguard the funds from the risks of possible misuse/misappropriation/loss.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, Liquidating Officer and collectors perform strictly their duties and functions in the receipt, remittance and verification of collections and accountable forms to avoid delayed remittances of collections.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly observe the proper handling of collections and the issuance of official receipts to the collectors to avoid incurrence of cash shortage. Likewise, she should strictly adhere to the laws, rules and regulations in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that management:
Require the concerned officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances to settle immediately their accounts; otherwise, consider the imposition of sanctions such as the withholding of their salaries as provided for in Section 9.3.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002;
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a single disbursement voucher for cash advance granted to an accountable officer for a certain program/activity; and
Adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce that all payments must be made by check directly to suppliers/contractors in the disbursement of government funds, except for petty disbursements out of the petty cash fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce direct payment to suppliers or dealers in the procurement of goods in order to withhold the amount of taxes due to the government pursuant to Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007. As one of the withholding agents, the tax should be computed and reflected on the face of the disbursement voucher and the corresponding Certificate of Withholding Tax Form be issued to concerned suppliers.

We recommended that the BAC Chairman, who is also the Municipal Engineer, require the BAC Secretariat to furnish the Auditor a copy of the contracts and all supporting documents within five (5) working days after execution thereof.
We recommended that the Municipality adhere strictly to the provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184 to ensure transparency, accountability, efficiency and economy in its procurement process.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant see to it that disbursement vouchers are duly supported with the required basic documents to ensure their legality, validity and propriety.

We recommended that the LGU adhere to the provisions of the existing laws, rules and regulations regarding the payment of RATA of members of the local sanggunian and other municipal officials and employees. We further recommended that the RATA received during the absences of the concerned Sangguniang Bayan Members be refunded.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly impose the provisions of the Revised Market Code of the Municipality of Sta. Maria, Ilocos Sur particularly on the collection of the market rental fees. Strictly impose the collection of the 20% surcharge/penalty on late payments so that the stallholders are compelled to pay their obligations on time. The collection of the 20% surcharge/penalty means additional income to the LGU. Furthermore, the Municipal Treasurer, together with the Market Committee, should be more responsible in the administration and management of the operations of the public market to attain the stated declared policy in the Revised Market Code of the Municipality of Sta. Maria, Ilocos Sur which is to make the public market more responsive and effective instrument of public service, as well as dynamic and viable enterprise to strengthen its economic potential, with the ultimate goal of contributing significantly to local and national development.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise taxes in order for the tobacco farmers to enjoy the benefits they rightfully deserve.
We recommended that the municipal officials and employees concerned implement fully all the programs/projects embodied under the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Chairperson of the LCPC to fully implement programs/projects/activities funded under the one percent (1%) internal revenue allotment geared towards the promotion and protection of interest and welfare of children in the country.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Chairperson of the GAD Focal Point System to fully implement programs/projects/activities under the GAD Plan in order to fully address gender issues/problems of the constituents in the municipal level and to help the national government attain its goal of addressing gender issues in the country.

We recommended that the LDRRM Council pass a resolution reverting the CYs 2011 and 2012 LDRRMF unspent balances to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund, for adoption by the Sangguniang Bayan. Further, that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer prepare the necessary transfer of funds and accounting reports to facilitate the said reversion and certify to the availability of said funds for subsequent reappropriation for social services.

We recommended that the MDRRMO submit monthly Report on Source and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on time in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Management exhaust all available remedies provided under the laws, rules and regulations in enforcing the immediate compliance to the audit suspensions and the settlement of the disallowances as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We have recommended that the municipality conduct physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment and submit a copy of the report to the Audit Team. To facilitate the conduct of physical count of properties, the inventory committee may consider performing physical count of newly acquired properties before moving to those that were acquired in the prior years. Inventory tagging should also be made in order to prevent double counting.

We have recommended that the management discontinue granting cash advances to the Local Chief Executive for purposes other than for official travelling expenses as stated in Item 4.1.4 of COA Circular 97-002. Likewise, the management is advised to refrain from granting the same cash advance to other elected officials in strict observance to said rules.

We have recommended that the municipality comply with the law particularly on the remittance of priority liabilities so the municipality could avoid interest, penalties and surcharges for not remitting the full amount withheld from their employees.

We have recommended that SB members should not be allowed to claim gasoline subsidy because they are already receiving monthly RATA in compliance with Section 343 of RA 7160 and Item 3.13, Annex B of COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012, prohibiting unauthorized expenditures.

We have recommended that Municipal officials assigned with government service vehicles should no longer be allowed payment of Transportation Allowance as provided under Item 8.2.1 of Local Budget Circular No. 103.

We have recommended management to comply with the guidelines provided by PPSAS 1, “Presentation of Financial Statements” and record the honorarium of the traffic enforcers in their proper classifications to avoid misleading representation of the accounts. It was further recommended that the LGU strictly comply with the policy and guidelines under Section 119 of PD 1445, requiring that all lawful expenditures and obligations incurred during the year shall be taken up in the accounts of that year.

We have recommended that management thru its Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant charge only allowable items against the Fund following the guidelines under the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 on the allocation and utilization of the 5% LDRRMF. As the payment of the honorarium paid was inappropriately charged against the LDRRM, this could be subjected to disallowance.

We have recommended that the municipality should practice prudence in disbursing government funds by refraining the use of government funds and resources for personal purposes. The salary of the employee of the cooperative must be charged to the funds of the cooperative and not on the municipality. It was further recommended that the total amount of the salary of Ms. Merlita Soriano should be returned to the LGU by the cooperative otherwise a Notice of Disallowance will be issued.

We have recommended that the Accountant ensure that travelling expenses are made within the prescribed limits in accordance with EO 298. The municipality should find ways to minimize the cost and optimize the performance of duties of its officers and employees for an effective and efficient organization.

We have recommended the management to:
Strictly adhere to Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of EO 292 on the guidelines on Monetization of Leave Credits to avoid future Notice of Suspensions and/or Disallowances.
In granting special monetization, follow strictly the conditions that the monetization applied for was 50% or more of the accumulated sick leave balances and presence of any of the valid and justifiable reasons.

We have recommended the public officials or employees of the municipality to avoid conflicts of interest at all times as required under Section 9 of Republic Act No. 6713 by not engaging their cooperative to participate in the procurement within its agency.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer discontinue the practice of charging prior year’s expenditure to current year appropriation and that proper matching of expenses against appropriation should always be observed pursuant to the above-cited regulations.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to thoroughly review payroll of employees availing loans before certifying documentary requirements for loan applications.  They have to ensure that the minimum monthly net take home pay of employees as required under the annual GAA is met so that the said employees/ officials could still provide a few thousand pesos to spend on the family’s needs.

We have recommended that the management furnish the Auditors copies of contracts together with all the supporting documents to the COA within five (5) working days from execution thereof pursuant to COA Circular No. 87-278 and COA Memorandum No. 2005-027 as reiterated in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
We have recommended the management to exert more effort to fully implement the GAD plans, programs, and activities to obtain the maximum benefit that can be derived for the agency’s constituents.

We recommended that  the  Accountable  Officer  deposit  all  collections
  regularly and intact to avoid the accumulation of cash in her  hands  and
  the exposure of the same to possible risk of loss  and  misappropriation.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the pertinent  provisions
  of COA Circular Nos. 97-002 on  the  granting  and  liquidation  of  cash
  advances. Accordingly:

      • No additional cash advance should be  granted  unless  the  previous
        cash advance has already been liquidated or accounted for.; and


      • A cash advance must be liquidated immediately  after  the  purpose/s
        for which it was granted has been served or completed.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management look into the nature and
   condition  of  the  receivable  account  and  prioritize  the  collection
   thereof. If warranted, the Municipal Mayor may request for authority from
   the Commission  on  Audit  to  write-off  the  receivable  following  the
   specific guidelines and procedures as prescribed under COA  Circular  No.
   2016-005, thus, present fairly the affected accounts in the books of  the
   LGU.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal  Accountant  identify
   the component transactions and set up a subsidiary ledger  each  for  the
   accounts  Donations  and  Other  MOOE  amounting  to  P17,802,608.79  and
   P8,070,083.21, respectively, to establish the reliability thereof and  to
   present fairly the accounts in the books of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management prioritize the regular and timely  payment
  of its loan amortization with DOF-MDFO to avoid the  possible  incurrence
  of surcharge and/or penalties and to establish good credit  standing  for
  the LGU.

We  recommended  that  Management  ensure  that  all  disbursements   for
  supplemental  feeding  be  supported  with  complete   documentation   as
  prescribed to prove the validity and accuracy thereof.

   In this regard, the required  lacking  documents  for  the  disbursements
   amounting to P1,284,976.00 be  submitted  for  verification.  Should  the
   documents be not submitted or found not in order, the transactions may be
   suspended or disallowed in audit.

We recommended that  Management  ensure  that  the  utilization  of  the
   Special Education Fund (SEF) should  be  in  accord  with  the  approved
   budget and are supported with complete documentation  as  prescribed  to
   prove the validity, legality and propriety thereof.

   In this  regard,  the  lacking  documents  including  the  corresponding
   liquidation documents for the fund transfers amounting to P461,586.00 be
   submitted for verification. Should the documents  be  not  submitted  or
   found not in order, the transactions may be suspended or  disallowed  in
   audit.

We recommended that Management ensure that all procurement  of  goods  be
  supported with complete documentation as prescribed to prove the validity
  and accuracy thereof.

   In  this  regard,  the   required   lacking   documents   and   necessary
   corrective/explanatory documents for the inconsistencies noted, including
   an  explanation  why  the  purchases  were  split,   be   submitted   for
   verification. Should the documents be  not  submitted  or  found  not  in
   order, the disbursements may be suspended or disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management install and maintain a system of  internal
  control for the procurement, recording and  monitoring  of  supplies  and
  property, plant and equipment to ensure that  these  are  duly  accounted
  for.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the pertinent  provisions
  of COA Circular No.  2012-002  on  the  preparation  of  plan/budget  and
  reporting of LDRRMF. Accordingly, to ensure effective and timely audit of
  the fund, the LDRRMO:

      • prepare the LDRRMFIP following the prescribed format;
      • prepare the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization  of  DRRMF
        using the prescribed format  (certified  correct  by  the  Municipal
        Accountant); and
      • submit copy of the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization  of
        DRRMF to COA within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management prepare the  LGU’s  Annual  GAD  Plan  and
  Budget using the prescribed format, i.e. to include  information  on  the
  gender issues or gender mandates, GAD objective, and attributed Programs.
  Also, the gender issues must be based on the LGU’s sex disaggregated data
  (SDD).

We reiterated our recommendation that  the  Municipal  Accountant  ensure
   timely  submission  of  accounts.  Accordingly,  she  should  cause   the
   immediate submission of the unsubmitted accounts of the LGU for  2018  to
   facilitate the audit of the transactions as well as to avoid the possible
   suspension  or  disallowance  of  the  same   and   the   imposition   of
   administrative disciplinary sanctions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. OIC-Municipal Accountant to provide and maintain PPE Ledger Cards for
each class or category of assets which provide details of the PPE account
and to analyze and reconcile discrepancies between the Accounting Office
and General Services Office records and make the necessary adjustments,
if any; and

b. Municipal General Services Officer to number each type of supplies and
maintain Stock Cards per stock number, provide and maintain Property
Cards per PPE and reconcile the results of physical count with the Ledger
Cards of the accounting records to establish the reliability and existence of
the PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to prepare a lapsing schedule and compute for the depreciation
expenses of all the recorded PPE for fair presentation of the PPE accounts in
the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
recommend measures to address the manpower constraints and ensure that the
required Bank Reconciliation Statements are prepared in compliance with
Section 74 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011.

We reiterated our audit recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to determine and
address the discrepancies in the Cash-Local Treasury and Cash in Bank
accounts and effect the necessary adjustments/corrections.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-
Municipal Accountant to disclose the required financial information in the Notes
to Financial Statement to assist the users in understanding the financial
statements and to provide them with additional relevant information on the
affected accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal General
Services Officer to facilitate the transfer of titles of the acquired land in the name
of the Municipality to protect the interest of the government and to avoid third
party claims and possible land disputes. Also, request the Office of the
Sangguniang Bayan to appropriate fund for the expenses that will be incurred
in the process.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and
OIC-Municipal Accountant to determine the causes of the noted discrepancies
and effect the necessary adjustment of the affected accounts to ensure fair
presentation of the Real Property Tax Receivables and Special Education Tax
Receivables, and Deferred Real Property Tax Income and Special Education Tax
Income accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-
Municipal Accountant to correctly classify the outstanding balance of the long-
term loans payable by presenting the portion of loans due for settlement within
twelve months as part of the current liabilities and the remaining which is due
for settlement beyond one year as non-current liabilities in the Statement of
Financial Position.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to submit the Financial Statements to the Audit Team within the
period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2010-001 to facilitate the timely review
of the financial position and performance as well as the cash flows from
operating, investing and financial activities and analysis of estimated against
actual revenues and expenditures.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant
to:

a. Submit immediately the DVs and RCD covering the period from October to
December 2018 and November to December 2018, respectively, to facilitate
the timely conduct of post audit and analysis of accounts; and

b. Submit to the Audit Team DVs enumerated in Appendix H.

We likewise recommended that available remedies provided under Paragraph 2,
Section 122 and Paragraph 3, Section 39 of P.D. No. 1445, both on the non-
submission of accounts maybe resorted to.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Chairperson and
the General Services Officer (GSO) to furnish the Auditor with copies of
contracts and purchase orders with their supporting documents within five
working days from their perfection or issuance in compliance with COA Circular
No. 2009-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant
to submit all the necessary documents to support the paid claims on
infrastructure projects and goods and the Bids and Awards Committee to
submit a copy of contract for the garbage disposal.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor refrain from payment of various
expenses through cash, instead issue check directly to the suppliers/dealers.

We also recommended that appropriate procurement procedure provided under
RA No. 9184 be observed for procurement of goods and services.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to
appropriate at least one per cent of the Municipality’s IRA for the strengthening
and implementation of the programs, projects and activities (PPAs) of the LCPC
pursuant to Section 15 of RA No. 9344.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal
Accountant through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council to submit to
the COA Auditor the monthly report on the sources and utilization of DRRM
Fund in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-
Municipal Accountant to transfer all the unexpended balance of the Quick
Response Fund and LDRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund of the Trust
Fund books.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Point Person
and all other concerned officials to prepare the GPB and GAD Accomplishment
Report using the format prescribed in Annexes D and E of the JMC No. 2013-01
of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA and submit the reports to the Provincial Planning
Development Office and DILG Provincial Office for review and endorsement as
required under Section 4.0 of the JMC No. 2016-01 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Executive Committee
and GAD Technical Working Group to provide technical assistance to the Local
Planning and Development Offices (LPDO) to establish and maintain GAD
Database and ensure that it is operational. Likewise, provide the funds
necessary for its establishment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to
complete the conduct of physical count of inventories and properties and to
reconcile results thereof with accounting records. Direct the Municipal Accountant
and the Acting Treasurer to observe proper procedures on the preparation,
recording and updating of Subsidiary Ledgers/Property Ledger Cards and Property
Cards for PPEs in accordance with NGAS for LGUs.

We recommended that Management furnish the Audit Team with the copies of
Memorandum of Agreement (MOA)/ Trust Agreement and the necessary
documents enumerated in the NS issued within the period stipulated. Henceforth,
ensure strict compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and
other pertinent regulations.

We recommended that the Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to review and
verify the completeness of supporting documents before payments be made. Ensure
that submitted supporting documents are in compliance with applicable rules, laws
and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC and TWG to prepare
and review the checklist in all biddings conducted and ensure that all the official
requirements indicated therein are in order to preserve the integrity of the bidding
process as mandated under R.A. 9184.

We recommended that Management require the contractors to post Warranty
Security in the amount provided under Section 62.2.3.3 of the Revised Implementing
Rules and Regulations of the Government Procurement Act (RA 9184) in the
procurement of infrastructure projects otherwise, structural defects and or failure
within the warranty period not covered by the surety bond and not repaired by the
contractor shall be borne by persons liable. Any claims for repair of structures
within the warranty period will be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer to submit the Report on Sources and
Utilization of Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) on
or before the 15th day after the end of each month in compliance with COA Circular
No. 2012-002 for proper evaluation and monitoring.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the form of contracts for
infrastructure project be properly filled-up most specifically Item 5 which contained
the contract duration, to ensure that projects are completed within the timeframe
stipulated in the perfected contract and any unjustifiable delay in the completion
thereof be properly charged with liquidated damages as required under Section 8,
Annex “E” of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor designate a Disbursing Officer to
perform disbursing functions so that collection and disbursement activities are
segregated to avoid lapping and or juggling of funds and other possible
irregularities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned LGU official to
submit within the reglementary period approved copy of the GAD plan and Budget
and ensure that the annual accomplishment report was submitted on time.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
submit immediately the financial statements and supporting schedules as of
December 31, 2018. Initiate disciplinary action for continuous inaction for the
submission of the required financial and budgetary reports such as administrative
sanctions and suspensions of salary pursuant to Section 122 of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant carefully analyze the entries made
on the accounts, trace it to transaction documents/records and effect/record the
necessary adjusting entries.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect adjustments to reflect the correct
account balances and present reliable data on the Municipality’s financial position
as of a given period of time.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following courses of actions:
Require the Accountant to submit monthly Trial Balance and its supporting documents and reports within the deadline as stated in the above COA Circular to   give ample time for the audit team to conduct the necessary audit procedures on time and provide the agency of the needed information which could be vital in their decision making; and
Enjoin compliance on the existing laws, rules and regulations pertaining to the submission of financial reports and other reports relevant to the operation of the Municipality, otherwise, failure on the part of the officials concerned to submit the documents and reports mentioned herein shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission (Presidential Decree 1445 Section 122).

Recommendations:
We recommended the following courses of action:
Require the persons liable to immediately settle audit disallowances while still in the service to avoid difficulty of collecting the amount due in the case of retirement or separation from the service or transfer to other agencies;
Require those employees who are still in the service but identified and determined as one or among the persons liable to settle the audit disallowance.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following course of actions:
Reprimand the person responsible to regularly prepare and submit the mentioned Utilization Reports to the Office of the State Auditor for audit purposes;
Ensure submission of the reportorial requirements set forth under existing laws, rules and regulations affecting financial affairs of the government, otherwise, failure on the part of the officials concerned to submit the documents and reports mentioned herein shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission (Sec. 122, P.D 1445).

Recommendations:
We recommended the following courses of actions
Apply the qualification standard requirement in identifying the rightful worker to paid with the minimum wage pay as defined in the above CSC Resolution;
Adopt the Rules Implementing Wage Order No. RB XI – 20 dated July 16, 2018 immediately and any issuances that updates the same; and
The application of CSC/COA/DBM Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 dated June 15, 2017, pertaining to the new rules and regulations governing contract of services and job order worker in the government, is optional to the LGU.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following courses of action:
Give utmost emphasis on prioritizing the construction of the Sanitary Landfill and Leachate Treatment facility;
Prepare and submit to this Office the approved Solid Waste Management Plan and observe the submission of Utilization Reports on the prescribe period; and
Enjoin strict adherence to the provisions of the R.A. No. 9003 in the operation and maintenance of the sanitary landfill.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following courses of actions:
Direct the Municipal Accountant and the General Service Officer to perform the following:
maintain the property ledger cards and property cards to all LGU’s properties
expedite the process of reconciliation of both records and reports in order to arrive at the correct carrying balances of this account;
identify the unaccounted and accounted but not existing properties to satisfy its verifiability and reliability; and
determine the person responsible to the properties not already existing so that appropriate remedies to settle their respective accountability may be applied immediately.
Ensure agreement of Accounting Records and General Services Records with respect to the result of conducted physical inventory count of stocks and properties;
Regularly update the property card and the property ledger card maintained in General Services Office and Accounting Office, respectively, so that monitoring of the properties’ whereabouts is regularly made; and
Perform the necessary yearly physical inventory count of properties, and submit the necessary reports to this Office for monitoring and compliance purposes.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following courses of actions:
Furnish this Office the Copies of the Contracts and the corresponding documents that supports the regularity and legality of the contracts entered by the parties; and
Regularly furnish this Office the copies of Contracts and all documents relative thereto within five (5) working days after execution thereof for review and evaluation purposes.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following courses of actions:
Establish time frame for the concerned Municipal personnel to supply the Accounting Section with the necessary documents pertaining to the implementation of Municipal projects, programs and activities immediately after its completion;
Provide the Accounting Section an ample time to scrutinize the documents for regularity, legality and completeness before submitting to this Office after recording the transactions to the Municipality’s books of account on 10th day of the ensuing month; and
Enjoin compliance on the above-mentioned law to preclude this Office from the issuance of appropriate notice.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following courses of action:
Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately the Bank Reconciliation Statements of all accounts affected herein and cause prompt submission of Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) for all bank accounts of the Municipality to the  Audit Team onwards;
Secure the monthly Bank Statements and the copies of Credit and/or Debit Memos from the bank to be attached to the JEVs as supporting documents; and
Effect the recording of all new accounts in the books.

Recommendations:
We recommended the immediate remittance of RPT shares to the Provincial Government of Davao Occidental including the Municipality’s Barangays shares.
We also recommended the proper coordination between the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant which is needed to efficiently address the issue on the delayed remittances.
Lastly, we recommended to institute a system that could improve the process of remitting RPT shares to concerned LGUs in a systematic manner.

Recommendations
We recommended the following courses of action:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to observe the following items of this COA Circular, to wit:
Item 4.0 – Coverage
Item 6.0 – General Guidelines
Item 7.0 – Specific Guidelines
Item 8.0 – Procedures in the write-off of dormant accounts; and
Enjoin compliance of the above-mentioned COA Circular.

Recommendations
We recommended the following courses of action:
Facilitate the immediate processing of transfer of ownership to government’s full control;
Submit to this Office the proof of deeds showing there was an intention to transfer the titles or rights over the lands where the projects are situated to the government; and
Enjoin compliance on the above-mentioned COA Circular.

Recommendations:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary correcting/adjusting entries in the books to record the procurement of supplies and materials to its appropriate inventory accounts.  Promote the usage and maintenance of supply ledger cards. Ensure that subsequent purchases are initially recorded to its inventory account and charged to expense account only upon issuance to end-users.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following courses of action:
Strictly comply the provisions of the IRR of R.A. No. 9184 to ensure transparency, equity, efficiency, and economy in government procurement and to avoid sanction on the circumvention of the rules and regulations. Henceforth, persons responsible for the conduct of procurement not in conformity with R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR shall explain in writing why fuel payments totalling to
P 3, 265,169.83 will not be subjected to appropriate notices; and
Strengthen the procurement function of the Bids and Awards Committee to ensure that all the processes and procedures in the procurement of goods are strictly followed and to preclude irregular transactions.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to obtain Transfer Certificate of Title of all the parcels of land acquired by the Municipality as required under Section 449, Volume I of GAAM and Section 39(2) of PD 1445 to serve as proof of absolute ownership over these properties.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 | During the exit conference, the Municipal Assessor mentioned that the Sub-division Plan of the parcels of land was already forwarded to the DENR Regional Office for approval and the other required documents were already complied.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Engineer facilitate the posting of signboards consisting all on-going PPAs of the Municipality and the posting of tarpaulin signboards for infrastructure projects in their correct specifications at the venue where the PPA is located or carried out. This will enable the public to have knowledge regarding the relevant information of the PPA which promotes good governance through transparency. It also enables them to assess whether the PPA were completed justifiably as to contract amount and duration. The Municipal Engineer should include the cost of the tarpaulin signboard in the PPA’ program of works.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer intensify the collection of Real Property and Special Education Taxes.  Notices of collection should be sent to the delinquent taxpayers to remind them of their obligation to pay their taxes. Likewise, conduct massive tax campaign in all the barangays and offer tax amnesty in order to motivate delinquent taxpayers to avail of the offer.  In addition, seek the cooperation of the barangay officials, anyway, the barangays have also shares in the collection of real property taxes.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 | Collections increased by P390,539.78 during the year.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that copies of all contracts, preparatory requirements, and their supporting documents of procurement activities should be submitted as separate set of documents to our office within five (5) working days upon execution of the contract in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: The agency assured that they shall see to it that the complete set of project documents are submitted to the office of the Auditor for the review and evaluation of their procurement transactions.
observations.reason: It is noteworthy to say that the municipality submits all PR/PO to this office

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the accountant ensure that travelling expenses are made within the prescribed limits in accordance with EO 298.
observations.managementAction: The management has admitted that they indeed claim meals in excess of what is allowed by the law stating that the amount is not sufficient to cover for their meals, and that no food chain could offer a decent meal for that amount.
observations.reason: It is worth mentioning that the municipality already did some actions to lower the excessive claims of municipal officials and employees. However, we noticed that uniformity in applying the rules and regulations were not equitably applied to all employees. There were still municipal officials and employees who were allowed to reimburse actual cost of meals and hotel incurred despite the absence of certification by the head of the agency that those expenses were absolutely necessary for the performance of their assignments as required in the above provisions.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the management to observe that every time there is unexpended balance of the LDRRMF as of yearend, it should be transferred to Special Trust Fund under the account Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Account Code: 2-04-01-020) in the Trust Fund books.
observations.managementAction: The unexpended amount of QRF-MOOE was already transferred to the Special Trust Fund, Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund in February, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the management to make sure that all expenses are duly authorized, adequately funded and documented; and to allow expenditures only within the limit of the appropriation pursuant to the provisions of PD 1177.
observations.managementAction: The management assures the Audit team that they shall strictly monitor all their expenses as charged to their respective funds to ensure that nothing shall result into an overdraft.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the management to find ways to utilize the idle second floor of market building to obtain the benefits that may be derived from it.

	We have further recommended the management to conduct a feasibility study to assure the necessity and success of the operation of the project first before starting their future project endeavors to avoid idle assets.
observations.managementAction: The management assured that they shall find ways to utilize the second floor of the market building, and that they have already formulated plans to attract business entities for the utilization of the said asset.
observations.reason: The municipality already executed a lease agreement for Second Floor of the New Public Market Building
