benefits be supported with
complete documentation as
prescribed to prove the
validity and accuracy of
the disbursement.
Correspondingly, the
lacking supporting
documents for the partial
payment of the terminal
leave benefits of Ms.
Laggui be submitted to
avoid the possible
suspension or disallowance
thereof.

We recommended that
Management ensure that
procurements of
infrastructure projects
through pakyaw contracts be
justified and supported
with complete documentation
as prescribed to prove the
validity and accuracy
thereof.
In this regard, for the
procurements totalling
P1,964,053.00, Management
explain why these were made
through pakyaw contracts
and submit the necessary
supporting documents to
avoid the possible
suspension or disallowance
thereof.

For the above mentioned
transactions, we
recommended that the
required lacking documents
be submitted for
verification. Otherwise,
the same may be suspended
or disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the
Municipal Accountant ensure
timely submission of
accounts. Accordingly, he
should cause the immediate
submission of the
unsubmitted accounts of the
LGU for 2017 to facilitate
the audit of the
transactions as well as to
avoid the possible
suspension or disallowance
of the same and the
imposition of
administrative disciplinary
sanctions.

We recommended that
Management submit the
purchase orders, government
contracts and their
supporting documents to the
Office of the Auditor
within the period
prescribed under COA
Circular No. 2009-001 to
facilitate the systematic
and effective review
thereof.

We recommended that
Management designate only
permanent employees as
collecting/disbursing
officers and ensure that
all designated
collecting/disbursing
officers are duly bonded to
safeguard them and the
entire Municipality from
risk of loss or
misappropriation of public
funds.

We recommended that
Management strictly observe
the minimum monthly net
take home pay of the
officials and employees of
the LGU as required under
Section 47 of the General
Provisions of the GAA.

We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor direct the
Municipal Treasurer to sign
the disbursement vouchers,
thus, certify the
availability of funds for
the disbursements of the
LGU for consistency and
control purposes.

We recommended that
Management monitor and
ensure the completion of
all the LGU’s
infrastructure projects to
immediately serve the
intended purpose or use
thereof.
In the case of the
construction of the
recovery room, operating
room and Basilio SWIP, the
Municipal Engineer should
submit copies of the duly
approved suspension orders
for verification. Also, the
construction of these
structures be resumed
immediately as soon as the
conditions would allow so
as not to further the delay
of completion.

We recommended that
Management require all
persons responsible and
liable to immediately
settle the suspensions and
disallowances amounting to
P25,775,919.27 and
P19,145,020.69,
respectively, to clear them
from their accountabilities.
For the audit suspensions,
the accountable officials
submit the requirements
indicated in the Notice of
Suspension (NS). For the
disallowances, the
accountable officials and
employees be required to
refund or pay the same.

We recommended that the
Municipal Accountant update
and prepare regularly the
bank reconciliation
statements at the end of
each month for the five
bank accounts of the
municipality to ensure
accuracy of the Cash
account in compliance with
Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of COA
Circular No. 96-111.

We recommend the following:

That management comply with
the rule and regulation on
the granting, utilization
and liquidation of cash
advances
That cash advances be
liquidated immediately
after utilization. Also, the
proper recording of cash
advances should be strictly
implemented
That management exert
extra effort to demand the
liquidation of the cash
advances and if not
utilized, to demand the
refund of the amount.

We recommended that the
Honorable Chief Executive to
instruct the Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal
Accountant to submit the
duly cancelled staled
checks for the verification
with its original
disbursement vouchers.

We recommended that the
General Service Officer
requires the submission of
duly accomplished daily
trip tickets before the use
of government vehicles.

We recommended that the
municipality initiate
activities to pursue the
formulation and
operationalization of its
own Gender and Development
(GAD) Code.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare a lapsing schedule and compute for the depreciation expenses of all the recorded PPE for fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant will prepare the lapsing schedule and compute for the depreciation expenses of the Municipality’s PPE. A copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) taking up the depreciation expenses shall be furnished the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: Depreciation Expense was not provided and lapsing schedule was not prepared by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the balances between treasury records and accounting records and to determine and address the discrepancies in the Cash-Local Treasury and Cash in Bank accounts and to effect any adjustments/corrections in compliance with Section 181 (c) of the GAAM, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Treasurer sees to it that any reconciling items between treasury records and cashbook is reflected in the cashbook.
observations.reason: Discrepancies between accounting and treasury records were not yet reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement in compliance with Section 74 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 and maintain subsidiary ledger for each bank account to facilitate the preparation of the monthly bank reconciliation statement and ensure that the Cash in Bank – LCCA balance is in agreement between records.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer did not maintain cashbook – cash in bank for the two bank accounts of the Municipality; the Municipal Treasurer was required to provide and maintain a cashbook for each bank accounts of the Municipality for proper recording and monitoring of all transactions involved in the cash in bank account and to strengthen the internal control system of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Subsidiary Ledgers for each bank account were still not maintained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to disclose the required financial information in the Notes to Financial Statements to assist the users in understanding the financial statements and to provide them with additional relevant information on the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: Only Payable and Receivable Accounts were disclosed in the Notes to 2018 Financial Statements.
observations.reason: Some accounts were still not presented in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to provide and maintain cashbook for each bank accounts of the Municipality for proper recording and monitoring of all transactions involved in the cash in bank account and to strengthen the internal control system of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Cashbook for the 20 per cent Development Fund and Philhealth-Professional Fee were already maintained by the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: Cashbook for 20 per cent Development Fund and Philhealth-Professional Fee were already maintained by the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit the Financial Statements to the Audit Team within the period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2010-001 to facilitate the timely review of the financial position and performance as well as the cash flows from operating, investing and financial activities and analysis of estimated against actual revenues and expenditures.
observations.managementAction: Financial Statements were submitted on May 16, 2019.
observations.reason: Still delayed in the submission of 2018 FS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the DVs and RCD covering the period required to facilitate the timely conduct of post audit and analysis of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Disbursement Vouchers were submitted on April 29, 2019.
observations.reason: Still delayed in the submission of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate at least one per cent of the Municipality’s IRA for the strengthening and implementation of the programs, projects and activities (PPAs) of the LCPC pursuant to Section 15 of RA No. 9344.
observations.managementAction: Obligations incurred for the PPAs intended for the LCPC for the Municipality’s IRA for CY 2018 were all charged under the 20 per cent Development Fund.
observations.reason: One per cent of the Municipality’s IRA for CY 2018 was not appropriated for LCPC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the documents required under COA Circular No. 2012-001 for the procurement of real property.
observations.managementAction: The required documents were submitted and verified.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the documents required under COA Circular No. 2012-001 for the procurement of drugs and medicines and a certification that the medicines purchased fall within and conform to the Essential Drug List, PNDF Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The required documents were submitted and verified.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to submit all the necessary documents to justify the paid claims on infrastructure projects and to strictly comply with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and his staff are gathering all the necessary documents to support the payments of all the projects of the Municipality. He assured that all documents will be submitted to the Audit Team. Moreover, the Municipal Mayor and the BAC Chairperson assured to submit the contract for garbage disposal and all the required bidding documents to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person and all other concerned officials to prepare the GPB and GAD Accomplishment Report using the format prescribed in Annexes D and E of the JMC No. 2013-01 and submit the reports to the Provincial Planning Development Office and DILG Provincial Office for review and endorsement.
observations.managementAction: GAD Plan and Budget was prepared by the GAD Focal Person.
observations.reason: Annual GAD Plan and GAD Accomplishment Report were not submitted to the designated oversight government agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain a subsidiary ledger for the 20 per cent Development Fund to facilitate the preparation of the required Financial Statements in compliance with Sections 105, 107 and 110 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Subsidiary ledger for the 20 per cent Development Fund was already maintained by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to transfer all the unexpended balance of the Quick Response Fund and LDRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund of the Trust Fund books.
observations.managementAction: The balance of the unexpended balance of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) was already transferred to the Special Trust Fund of the Trust Fund book on May 20, 2019 per check No. 430065.

observations.recommendation: Stop the practice of charging administrative expenses to the 20% Development Fund and ensure that projects implemented under said fund are in compliance with DILG/DBM JMC 2017-001.
observations.managementAction: 2017 Administrative Expenses no longer charged to the 20% Development Fund
observations.reason: Review of expenditure charges to the 20% Development Fund confirmed that the practice of charging administrative expenses to said fund was already stopped.

observations.recommendation: Stop the practice of charging honorarium/financial incentive against the LDRMMF funds. Henceforth, ensure that all disbursements be limited to expenditure items as provided under Section 5 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 2013-01 dated March 25, 2013.
observations.managementAction: 2017 This expenditure was not charged to the LRRMF but rather to the GF of the LGU.
observations.reason: Verification of charges to the LDRRMF confirmed that the management’s allegation.

observations.recommendation: Require the Accountant to stop the practice of recording as outright expense aid to finance various activities of the Department of Education and to record these as cash advances subject to liquidation.
observations.managementAction: 2017 Releases of SEF to the DepEd were no longer treated as outright cash advances subject for liquidation.
observations.reason: Validation of charges to the SEF confirmed that the funds released to the DepEd out of SET funds were recorded as cash advances subject for liquidation.

observations.recommendation: Comply strictly with Section 15 of Republic Act No. 9344 specifically in the inclusion of the youth representative in the composition of the MCPC to ensure that youth sensitive issues were addressed in the formulation of policies and prioritized in the plans/programs and activities.
observations.managementAction: 2017 A representative from the youth sector was already included in the MCPC.
observations.reason: Verification of programs implemented under the Office of the Social Works Officer, Municipal Health Office, Municipal Mayor’s Office showed programs that cater to activities for the youth sector.

observations.recommendation: Comply strictly with Section 15 of Republic Act No. 9344 specifically in the appropriation of funds to support implementation of MCPC prioritized plans/programs and activities. We also recommend that activities pertaining to MCPC be reported as accomplishments thereto.
observations.managementAction: 2017 Though not recorded as a separate expense item in the annual budget, such however was included in the budget of the SWO, MHO and other Departments of the LGU.
observations.reason: Verification of programs implemented under the Office of the Social Works Officer, Municipal Health Office, Municipal Mayor’s Office showed programs that cater to activities for the youth sector.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that a proportionate deduction as recoupment of the 15% advance payment be made in every progress billing claimed by contractors as provided under Section 4.3 Annex “E” of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Government Procurement Act (RA 9184).
observations.managementAction: 2017 The whole amount for recoupment was automatically deducted in first billing.
observations.reason: Verification of disbursements for infrastructure projects of the Municipality showed that the 15% advance payment was deducted outright from first billing.

observations.recommendation: Require the persons liable for the immediate settlement/refund of the amount disallowed. Henceforth, ensure the conduct of price evaluation/ market survey before the procurement of non-recurring equipment/supplies.
observations.managementAction: 2017 The whole amount of disallowance was paid and motion for withdrawal of appeal was filed.
observations.reason: The motion for withdrawal of appeal was filed with the legal office.

observations.recommendation: Comply strictly with Section 18 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 that specifications for the procurement of goods shall be based on relevant characteristics and/or performance requirements and that reference to brand names shall not be allowed.
observations.managementAction: 2017 Postings of Invitation to Bid made no reference to brand names.
observations.reason: Review and validation of ITBs and RFQs showed that these do not contain brand names.

observations.recommendation: Direct the BAC/Municipal Accountant to require/submit the lacking documents to support claims for honoraria. Henceforth, adhere strictly to the provisions of DBM Circular No. 2004-5A as amended by DBM Circular No. 2007-03 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 to avoid suspension or disallowance of transaction.
observations.managementAction: The required documents were already submitted to the Auditor. for further review and verification
observations.reason: The required documents were already submitted to the Auditor. for further review

observations.recommendation: Stop the practice of programming/charging expenditures which are not development projects against the 20% development fund and instead strictly implement developmental projects identified under the DILG/DBM JMC No. 2011-1.
observations.managementAction: 2016 There were no more charges for the operations of municipal cemetery to the 20% Development Fund.
observations.reason: Review of charges to the operations of municipal cemetery showed no more charges for non-developmental projects.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately the required documents and henceforth, ensure that disbursements paid thru cash advance are supported with complete documents.
observations.managementAction: 2016 Required documents were already submitted to the liquidation of cash advance for “Lubi” festival.
observations.reason: The required documents were submitted to the Auditor for further review and verification

observations.recommendation: Require the Local School Board to explain and submit the legal basis of including the expense items under the SEF budget. Henceforth, ensure that only those priority expense items as enumerated under Section 4.0 of DEPED/DBM/DILG JMC No. 1 series of 2017 are included in the SEF budget approved and enacted.
observations.managementAction: 2017 Per DepEd letter, the questioned expenditures were allowable charges to Special Education Fund.
observations.reason: Verification showed that the same expenditures were still charged to the SEF.

observations.recommendation: Require the contractors to post Warranty Security in the amount provided under Section 62.2.3.3 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Government Procurement Act (RA 9184) in the procurement of infrastructure projects otherwise, structural defects and or failure within the warranty period not covered by the surety bond and not repaired by the contractor shall be borne by persons liable. Any claims for repair of structures within the warranty period will be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: 2017 Some of the infrastructure projects were supported with correct warranty security.
observations.reason: Verification showed that there were still projects with deficient warranty security.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit all suspension orders to the Office of the Auditor or any document that would warrant the non-imposition of liquidated damages to the concerned contractors, otherwise, direct the Municipal Accountant to compute the liquidated damages.
observations.managementAction: 2017 Liquidated damages were charged to delayed projects.
observations.reason: The contract maturity was not specifically stipulated in the contract hence, liquidated damages were not strictly imposed.

observations.recommendation: Cause the immediate refund of the amount of P290,000.00 paid to all appointed officials/employees of the municipality considering that the disbursement of funds was devoid of any legal basis.
observations.managementAction: 2017 The payment of the same allowance was already stopped and repayment thereof already initiated.
observations.reason: The disallowance was not yet settled.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to update and submit on time the monthly bank reconciliation statements as provided under Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 21, 1996 and for the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to perform a periodic reconciliation of their records.
observations.managementAction: 2015 Partial submission of BRS was initiated.
observations.reason: 2018 BRS were partially submitted.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to release immediately the barangays’ shares of prior years and succeeding shares on a quarterly basis within five (5) days after the end of each quarter.
observations.managementAction: 2016 RPT shares for barangays were remitted quarterly.
observations.reason: Verification of reports showed RPT barangay shares were already remitted.

observations.recommendation: Create the GAD Focal Point System in the municipality in accordance with Section 37, Rule VI of R.A. 9710 to ensure that the programs, projects, activities in the Municipality are responsive to gender issues and concerns. Direct the designated GAD Focal Person to initiate activities for the establishment of a local GAD code based on the gender issues and concerns through consultation with women constituents. Coordinate with the Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) and Philippine Commission on Women (PCW) in the formulation and development of said Code. Make representation with the local law-making body for the eventual passage and approval of the Local GAD code
observations.managementAction: 2015 The MSWO has been designated as GAD Focal Person.
observations.reason: Designation of the MSWO as GAD focal person was duly validated by the Audit Team. However, codification of the GAD code was not yet initiated.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Accountant to carefully analyze the entries made on the accounts, trace it to transaction documents/ records and effect/record the necessary adjusting entries.
observations.managementAction: 2015 Cash in Vault balances were already adjusted and abnormal balances in Liabilities accounts were already adjusted.
observations.reason: Verification and review of year-end financial statements showed that there were still balances in the subsidiary accounts showing negative balances.

observations.recommendation: Direct the LDRRMO to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the preparation of the Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRM Fund for submission to the COA Auditor within the prescribed period for proper monitoring and audit.
observations.managementAction: 2015 The LDRRMFIP was prepared and submitted to the Auditor.
observations.reason: The LDRRMFIP was prepared and given to the Auditor only upon demand. However, utilization report of the LDRRM fund was not submitted.

observations.recommendation: Direct all persons held liable therefore to immediately and fully settle the disallowances in audit pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006 to enable the Municipality to utilize the funds for its priority development projects.
observations.managementAction: 2009 Minimal repayments were deducted from the payroll.
observations.reason: Post-audit of payroll showed the deduction for repayment of COLA differential but the whole amount has not been fully settled.

observations.recommendation:  Direct all officials and employees with outstanding cash advance to immediately liquidate the same.  Stop granting additional cash advance if the previous ones have not been settled/liquidated yet pursuant to Section 89 of PD 1445.  Exert strong effort to collect or cause the liquidation of cash advances made by employees not anymore in the service. Coordinate from other government agencies in case of transfer by former employees.
observations.managementAction: 2011 Officials/Employees with Outstanding cash advance were encouraged to monetize to liquidate outstanding cash advances.
observations.reason: Outstanding cash advances showed reduction as compared with prior year’s balance.

observations.recommendation: Direct all those who received the PEI to refund the amount of P10,000.00 each, the payment of which is not legally due them, the PS limitation provided under Section 325(a) of RA 7160, having been not observed by the municipality. Henceforth, management shall strictly observe the fifty-five percent (55%) limitation on personal services to prevent disallowances in audit.
observations.managementAction: 2011 Minimal repayments were deducted from the payroll.
observations.reason: Post-audit of payroll showed the deduction for repayment of excess PEI.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following courses of action:

1.	Ensure compliance with the allocation requirement of at least 1% of LGU’s IRA solely for the purpose of strengthening and implementation of programs, projects and activities of the LCPC; and

2.	Furnish this Office the copy of LCPC Comprehensive Plan, the GAD Accomplishment Report for the calendar year 2017 and database relating to LCPC clientele.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has strengthen its LCPC through supporting early childhood care development program by construction of ECCD Centers and support workers. Comprehensive plan which is prepared by our focal shall be submitted to your office, while the GAD accomplishment report of 2017 were already submitted electronically.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following courses of actions:

1.	Furnish this Office the Copies of the Contracts and the corresponding documents that supports the regularity and legality of the contracts entered by the parties; and

2. 	Regularly furnish this Office the copies of Contracts and all documents relative thereto within five (5) working days after execution thereof for review and evaluation purposes.
observations.managementAction: The Human Resource Management Officer shall furnish copies of all contracts and supporting documents to your good office

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following courses of actions:

1. 	Apply the qualification standard requirement in identifying the rightful worker as defined in the above CSC Resolution;
2. 	Adopt the RTWPB – Region XI Wage Order No. RB XI – 19 dated November 4, 2016 starting January 2018 and any issuances that updates the same; and
3. 	The application of CSC/COA/DBM Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 dated June 15, 2017, pertaining to the new rules and regulations governing contract of services and job order worker in the government, is optional to the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has approved the minimum salary wage per DOLE circular through Sangguniang Bayan resolution adopting and the minimum salary wage to PHP 396.00

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that following courses of action:

1. 	Refrain from allowing private lending institutions to deduct the monthly loan amortization to employees’ compensation who availed the loan;   
2. 	Direct the Municipal Accountant to exercise extra vigilance in allowing deductions to employees’ payroll to ensure that the take home pay is within the minimum mandated amount; and
3. 	Enjoin compliance with the cited laws, rules and regulations to avoid the issuance of appropriate notices.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Human Resource Management Officer shall no longer allow and approve restructuring the existing loans to private banks but instead advice the municipal employees to transact with the government owned financial institutions which offer lower rates and longer terms to finance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following courses of actions:

1. 	Submit any proof of exposure to danger or any evidences satisfying the conditions given by the above laws and its implementing rules and regulations in claiming the other benefits intended to public social workers;

2. 	Submit a document showing the approval of the Local Chief Executed allowing the grant of hazard pay and subsistence allowances to all public social workers and public social welfare and development workers included in the list to be approved by him;

3. 	Direct the Municipal Accountant to determine the correct amount supported with complete documentation for the purpose of excluding the same in the  issuance of Notice of Disallowance on the following accounts:

a. 	Hazard Pay; and
b. 	Subsistence Allowance, net of traveling expenses and per diem granted in every official travel made to avoid double claim [IRR-RA No. 9433, Section 16(16.2.3.3)]; and

4. 	Enjoin compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations relative to this transaction to preclude the issuance of appropriate notices.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Social Worker has submitted documents and certifications as evidence to exposure to danger.
observations.reason: Awaiting for COA response whether documents forwarded are sufficient to allow to continue the disbursement of allowances

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the concerned agency personnel to conduct the appropriate gender analysis or gender audit to support the necessity of implementing any GAD programs, projects and activities and furnish this office the copy of results of any gender analysis or gender audit conducted.

	We also recommend the management to develop the required Local GAD Code.

Finally, we recommend the management to enjoin compliance with the mentioned laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The GAD focal person shall prepare gender sensitivity analysis and implement programs and projects based on approved GAD plan and budget
observations.reason: Proposed programs and projects were not fully implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following courses of action:

1. 	Direct the Municipal Engineer to replace the dilapidated signboards with a new one or provide the prescribe tarpaulin signboards to projects completed; and
2. 	Ensure that all projects, programs or activities are compliant with the policy of full public disclosure and pertinent existing laws, rules and regulations applicable thereto.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer replaced dilapidated signage’s and were charged to their source funds
observations.reason: Not all worn out signages were replaced.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following courses of action:

1. 	Require the Collectors/Collector Designate to regularly remit intact the collections made to the Liquidating Officer or Municipal Treasurer upon arrival to Office or on a weekly basis as the case maybe;

2. 	Direct the Municipal Treasurer to establish a system to properly monitor the prompt remittance of the collection to the Liquidating Officer likewise his establishment of schedule to deposit the collections to the authorized depository banks; and

3. 	Ensure that the collection involving material in amount shall be deposited immediately to the authorized depository bank and compliance with the above-mentioned COA Circular to properly safeguard the government fund against its exposure to theft or misuse.
observations.managementAction: The management made corrective measures to minimize the risk of exposure of the employees handling cash and cash equivalents by adopting daily turnover of accountabilities by officers and deposit daily to depository banks the collections.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following courses of actions:

1. 	Direct the Municipal Accountant and the General Service Officer to perform the following:
a.	expedite the process of reconciliation of both records and reports in order to arrive at the correct carrying balances of this account;
b.	identify the unaccounted and accounted but not existing properties to satisfy its verifiability; and
c.	determine the person responsible to the properties not already existing so that appropriate remedies to settle their respective accountability may be applied immediately.

2. 	Ensure agreement of Accounting Records and General Services Records with respect to inventories of stocks and properties;

3. 	Update the stock/property card and the stock/property ledger card maintained in General Services Office and Accounting Office, respectively, so that monitoring of the supplies and properties whereabouts is regularly made; and

4. 	Perform the necessary yearly physical inventory count of supplies and properties, and submit the necessary reports to this for monitoring and compliance purposes.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal accountant and GSO made efforts for the reconciliations of accounts and still on the process
observations.reason: Still on progress

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following courses of actions:

1. 	Require the Accountant to submit all the required reports within the deadline   mandated by existing rules and regulations in order to give ample time for the auditor to provide timely results of audit and also to provide the agency of the needed information which could be vital in their decision making; and

2. 	Enjoin compliance on the existing laws, rules and regulations pertaining to the submission of financial reports and other reports relevant to the operation of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant shall follow auditors recommendation
observations.reason: Not all reports were submitted on time

observations.managementAction: The Municipal accountant and GSO made efforts for the reconciliations of accounts and still on the process
observations.reason: Still on progress

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following courses of action:

1. 	Request the contractor concerned to continue the implementation of the projects despite the existence of delays or else any liquidating damage that Municipality would claim in the case of further default; and

2. 	Direct the concerned Municipal personnel to ensure the proper monitoring of projects implementation to avoid delays in the performance of Municipality’s basic services to benefit its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has compel the contractor to continue and explain what causes the delay in the implementation of the SALINTUBIG projects, but to date the almost all SALINTUBIG projects were completed

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the following courses of action:

1. 	Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to cause the prompt submission of Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) for all bank accounts of the Municipality to the COA Audit Team;

2. 	Secure the monthly Bank Statements and the copies of Credit and/or Debit Memos from the bank to be attached to the JEVs as supporting documents; and

3. 	Effect the recording of all new accounts in the books.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal accountant has provided your agency the bank reconciliation on all LGU bank accounts FY 2017

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that following courses of action:

1. 	Direct the Municipality Treasurer to establish a system on remitting the collections to the Liquidating Officer to ensure compliance of safeguarding the government money;

2. 	Direct the Collectors to regularly prepare the Daily Statement of Collections and Accountable Forms at the close of each business day and effect remittance of their collections daily, particularly, when collections exceed P 5,000.00; and

3. 	Ensure the recognition of appropriate asset and revenue accounts on the collection made later part of the year to avoid understatement of both accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has reminded the Municipal treasury regarding the previously issued memo regarding daily liquidation of accountabilities and monies and deposit the same to depository bank daily through reissuance of the same memo.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following courses of action:

1. 	Submit written explanation justifying the occurrence of the transactions despite absence of the cited documents and reports above;

2. 	Submit the documents and reports above for validation purposes of SEF transactions for calendar year 2017 and for the ensuing year; and

observations.recommendation:
We recommend to:

Require those persons responsible in the transactions suspended in audit to give preferential attention in settling the same for these may mature into disallowance.

Require the persons liable to immediately settle audit disallowances while still in the service to refrain from experiencing the difficulty of collecting the same in the case of employees’ retirement or separation from the service or transferred to other agencies.

Coordinate with other agencies to assists in the collection of audit disallowance of employees who have transferred their employment in the said Agency.  If possible, insists to the agency that clearance shall first be secured by the transferee from the municipal government before he/she be employed in their Agency.

Require those employees who are still in the service but identified and determined as one or among the persons liable to immediately settle the audit disallowances.

observations.recommendation:
In order to foster public awareness and monitoring of the procurement process and implementation of perfected and awarded infrastructure contracts and other procurement activities of the Municipal Government, we recommend the following:

To require the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) for all types of procurements at the end of each semester and have it approved by the HOPE.

To submit the duly approved PMR, furnishing a copy to the Audit Team’s Office, to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.

Conduct adequate planning so that programmed projects in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) be reasonably attained in the current year to obtain optimum utilization of the 20% Development Fund.

Ensure that unexpended balances of the current year be reprogrammed/re-appropriated in the ensuing year as prescribed in Sec 4 of DILG MC No. 97-30.

Supporting documents of this discrepancy such as resolution on the supplemental budget or any pertaining documents should be furnished to this Audit Team.

In compliance with the above-stated COA Circular, we recommended transfer and reclassification of accounts as follows:

We further recommend submitting to the Audit Team the complete documentation on the purchase of rescue vehicle such as the authority to purchase, copy of the SB Resolution, and such other documents relating to this transaction for auditorial review.

We recommend that the provisions of Sec. SEC. 325. of RA 7160 should be strictly observed when preparing budget/appropriations for PS. The attached Annex C-11will serve as a guide on completeness and timely submission in compliance with each specific legal basis for submission of the same.

             We further recommend that appropriate records/logbooks or transmittal documents be used or maintained by each Office for proper identification of responsible persons who caused the delay, and to establish effective internal control system and sound fiscal administration.

Make representations with the Home Mutual Development Fund (HDMF) office for any arrangements and agreements that could still save the project from its total loss since the foreclosed properties can still be redeemed within one (1) year period.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require the Municipal Treasurer  and the Municipal Accountant to  submit all disbursement vouchers duly supported with complete and valid documents within ten (10)  days after the end  of the month.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to obtain Transfer Certificate of Title of all the parcels of land acquired by the Municipality as required under Section 449, Volume I of GAAM and Section 39(2) of PD 1445 to serve as proof of absolute ownership over these properties.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require the Municipal Treasurer   to deposit collections intact with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day.  The amount collected for the day should be the amount to be deposited in order not to expose the funds for possible misuse or misappropriation and at the same time this will enable the Municipality to earn additional income in the form of interest on bank deposits.

We have recommended that management:
require the concerned officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances to settle immediately their accounts; otherwise, consider the imposition of sanctions such as the withholding of their salaries as provided for under Section 9.3.2 of COA Circular 97-002;
exert effort to locate the present address of the AOs whose  whereabouts are unknown, and
adhere strictly with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We have recommended that the BAC Chairman who is also the Municipal Engineer to require the BAC Secretariat to  furnish the Auditor a copy of the  contracts and all supporting documents within five (5) working days after execution thereof.

We have recommended that the Municipal Engineer  facilitate the posting of signboards consisting all on-going PPAs of the Municipality and the posting of tarpaulin signboards for infrastructure projects in their correct specifications at the venue where the PPA is located or carried out. This will enable the public to have knowledge regarding the relevant information of the PPA which promotes good governance through transparency. It also enables them to assess whether the PPA were completed justifiably as to contract amount and duration. The Municipal Engineer should include the cost of the tarpaulin signboard in the PPA’ program of works.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor  facilitate the creation of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to be headed by him and the preparation of the Municipality’s Solid Waste Management Plan consistent with the National Solid Waste Management Framework (Section 15 of R.A. 9003). This enables the Municipality to plot efficient and effective methods in waste management within its jurisdiction to provide a safe, clean and green environment to the constituents of this Municipality.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer  intensify the collection of Real Property and Special Education Taxes.  Notices of collection should be sent to the delinquent taxpayers to remind them of their obligation to pay their taxes. Likewise, conduct massive tax campaign in all the barangays and offer tax amnesty in order to motivate delinquent taxpayers to avail of the offer.  In addition, seek the cooperation of the barangay officials, anyway, the barangays have also shares in the collection of real property taxes.

We have recommended that that the Municipal Mayor cause the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise taxes  in order to give the tobacco farmers who worked hard for the generation of the funds the benefits they rightfully deserve.
We have recommended that the municipal officials and employees concerned to implement fully all the programs/ projects / activities embodied under the 20% Development Fund.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor to require the Chairperson of the LCPC to fully implement programs/projects funded under the one percent of the internal revenue allotment geared towards the promotion and protection of interest and welfare of children in the country.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor to require the Chairperson of the GAD Focal Point System to fully implement programs/projects funded under the GAD Plan in order to fully address gender issues/problems of the constituents in the municipal level and to help the national government attain its goal of addressing gender issues in the country.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all disbursements are supported with valid and complete documents. Further, management should adhere to Sections 9.4 and 10.4 of COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 regarding the settlement of suspensions and disallowances.  Audit suspensions should be settled within ninety (90) calendar days from receipt of the NS to avoid maturity of these suspensions to disallowances and disallowances should be settled within six (6) months from receipt of the ND by the persons liable.

We have recommended that copies of all contracts, preparatory requirements, and their supporting documents of procurement activities should be submitted as separate set of documents to our office within five (5) working days upon execution of the contract in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to enjoined all employees of the municipality to comply with the requirements set forth under Section 47 of the General Appropriations Act 2017 and adopt control measures to ensure that the employee’s monthly net take home pay not be lower than Four Thousand Pesos (P 4,000.00) after deducting mandatory contributions and other obligations.
We have recommended that the accountant ensure that travelling expenses are made within the prescribed limits in accordance with EO 298.

We have recommended the officials to refrain from reimbursing gasoline expenses had they used private vehicles for their travels. We have also recommended the Accounting unit to strictly assess the validity of the claims, making sure that the claims are in accordance with Paragraph 3.1.1.8 of COA Circular No. 96-004.

We have recommended the management to observe that every time there is unexpended balance of the LDRRMF as of yearend, it should be transferred to Special Trust Fund under the account Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Account Code: 2-04-01-020) in the Trust Fund books.

We have recommended the management to make sure that all expenses are duly authorized, adequately funded and documented; and to allow expenditures only within the limit of the appropriation pursuant to the provisions of PD 1177.

We have recommended the management to find ways to utilize the idle second floor of market building to obtain the benefits that may be derived from it.
We have further recommended the management to conduct a feasibility study to assure the necessity and success of the operation of the project first before starting their future project endeavors to avoid idle assets.
We have recommended the management to exert more effort to fully implement the GAD plans, programs, and activities to obtain the maximum benefit that can be derived for the agency’s constituents.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor issue an executive order for the creation
of the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of all the
properties of the municipality to establish the actual existence of these assets, for the
Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records and to effect the necessary
adjustments in the book and to submit the inventory report to COA.

We recommend that Management stop the practice of charging administrative
expenses to the 20% Development Fund and ensure that projects implemented
under said fund are in compliance with DILG/DBM JMC 2017-001.

We recommend that Management stop the practice of charging
honorarium/financial incentive against the LDRMMF funds. Henceforth, ensure
that all disbursements be limited to expenditure items as provided under Section 5
of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 2013-01 dated March 25, 2013.

We recommend that the Local School Board explain and submit the legal basis of
including the expense items under the SEF budget. Henceforth, ensure that only
those priority expense items as enumerated under Section 4.0 of
DEPED/DBM/DILG JMC No. 1 series of 2017 are included in the SEF budget
approved and enacted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Accountant to stop the
practice of recording as outright expense aid to finance various activities of the
Department of Education and to record these as cash advances subject to
liquidation.

We recommend that Management strictly comply with Section 15 of Republic Act
No. 9344 specifically in the inclusion of the youth representative in the composition
of the MCPC to ensure that youth sensitive issues were addressed in the formulation
of policies and prioritized in the plans/programs and activities.

We recommend that Management strictly comply with Section 15 of Republic Act
No. 9344 specifically in the appropriation of funds to support implementation of
MCPC prioritized plans/ programs and activities. We also recommend that
activities pertaining to MCPC be reported as accomplishments thereto.

We recommend that Management require the contractors to post Warranty
Security in the amount provided under Section 62.2.3.3 of the Revised Implementing
Rules and Regulations of the Government Procurement Act (RA 9184) in the
procurement of infrastructure projects otherwise, structural defects and or failure
within the warranty period not covered by the surety bond and not repaired by the
contractor shall be borne by persons liable. Any claims for repair of structures
within the warranty period will be disallowed in audit.

We recommend that the Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that a
proportionate deduction as recoupment of the 15% advance payment be made in
every progress billing claimed by contractors as provided under Section 4.3 Annex
“E” of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Government
Procurement Act (RA 9184).

We recommend that the Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit all
suspension orders to the Office of the Auditor or any document that would warrant
the non-imposition of liquidated damages to the concerned contractors, otherwise,
direct the Municipal Accountant to compute the liquidated damages.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the persons liable for the
immediate settlement/refund of the amount disallowed. Henceforth, ensure the
conduct of price evaluation/ market survey before the procurement of non-
recurring equipment/supplies.

We recommend that Management strictly comply with Section 18 of the Revised
IRR of RA 9184 that specifications for the procurement of goods shall be based on
relevant characteristics and/or performance requirements and that reference to
brand names shall not be allowed.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC/Municipal Accountant to
require/submit the lacking documents to support claims for honoraria. Henceforth,
adhere strictly to the provisions of DBM Circular No. 2004-5A as amended by DBM
Circular No. 2007-03 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 to avoid suspension or
disallowance of transaction.

We recommend that Management cause the immediate refund of the amount of
P290,000.00 paid to all appointed officials/ employees of the municipality
considering that the disbursement of funds was devoid of any legal basis.

We recommend that the Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to hasten the
preparation of the CY 2017 financial reports and submit these to COA within the
deadlines. We further recommend that, henceforth, Management closely monitor
compliance on the submission of monthly and quarterly financial reports with
complete schedules in accordance with the requirement of pertinent laws and
regulations. Otherwise, we recommend for the withholding of salaries of concerned
officials pursuant to Section 122 of P.D. 1445.

We recommended the following courses of action:
Ensure compliance with the allocation requirement of at least 1% of LGU’s IRA solely for the purpose of strengthening and implementation of programs, projects and activities of the LCPC; and
Furnish this Office the copy of LCPC Comprehensive Plan, the GAD Accomplishment Report for the calendar year 2017 and database relating to LCPC clientele.

We recommended the following courses of actions:
Furnish this Office the Copies of the Contracts and the corresponding documents that supports the regularity and legality of the contracts entered by the parties; and
Regularly furnish this Office the copies of Contracts and all documents relative thereto within five (5) working days after execution thereof for review and evaluation purposes.

We recommended the following courses of actions:
Apply the qualification standard requirement in identifying the rightful worker as defined in the above CSC Resolution;
Adopt the RTWPB – Region XI Wage Order No. RB XI – 19 dated November 4, 2016 starting January 2018 and any issuances that updates the same; and
The application of CSC/COA/DBM Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 dated June 15, 2017, pertaining to the new rules and regulations governing contract of services and job order worker in the government, is optional to the LGU.

We recommended that following courses of action:
Refrain from allowing private lending institutions to deduct the monthly loan amortization to employees’ compensation who availed the loan;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to exercise extra vigilance in allowing deductions to employees’ payroll to ensure that the take home pay is within the minimum mandated amount; and
Enjoin compliance with the cited laws, rules and regulations to avoid the issuance of appropriate notices.

We recommended the following courses of actions:
Submit any proof of exposure to danger or any evidences satisfying the conditions given by the above laws and its implementing rules and regulations in claiming the other benefits intended to public social workers;
Submit a document showing the approval of the Local Chief Executed allowing the grant of hazard pay and subsistence allowances to all public social workers and public social welfare and development workers included in the list to be approved by him;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to determine the correct amount supported with complete documentation for the purpose of excluding the same in the  issuance of Notice of Disallowance on the following accounts:
Hazard Pay; and
Subsistence Allowance, net of traveling expenses and per diem granted in every official travel made to avoid double claim [IRR-RA No. 9433, Section 16(16.2.3.3)]; and
Enjoin compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations relative to this transaction to preclude the issuance of Notice of Disallowance.

We recommend the concerned agency personnel to conduct the appropriate gender analysis or gender audit to support the necessity of implementing any GAD programs, projects and activities and furnish this office the copy of results of any gender analysis or gender audit conducted.
We also recommend the management to develop the required Local GAD Code.
Finally, we recommend the management to enjoin compliance with the mentioned laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended the following courses of action:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to replace the dilapidated signboards with a new one or provide the prescribe tarpaulin signboards to projects completed; and
Ensure that all projects, programs or activities are compliant with the policy of full public disclosure and pertinent existing laws, rules and regulations applicable thereto.

We recommended the following courses of actions:
Require the Collectors/Collector Designate to regularly remit intact the collections made to the Liquidating Officer or Municipal Treasurer upon arrival to Office or on a weekly basis as the case maybe;
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to establish a system to properly monitor the prompt remittance of the collection to the Liquidating Officer likewise his establishment of schedule to deposit the collections to the authorized depository banks; and
Ensure that the collection involving material in amount shall be deposited immediately to the authorized depository bank and compliance with the above-mentioned COA Circular to properly safeguard the government fund against its exposure to theft or misuse.

We recommended the following courses of actions:
Direct the Municipal Accountant and the General Service Officer to perform the following:
expedite the process of reconciliation of both records and reports in order to arrive at the correct carrying balances of this account;
identify the unaccounted and accounted but not existing properties to satisfy its verifiability; and
determine the person responsible to the properties not already existing so that appropriate remedies to settle their respective accountability may be applied immediately.
Ensure agreement of Accounting Records and General Services Records with respect to inventories of stocks and properties;
Update the stock/property card and the stock/property ledger card maintained in General Services Office and Accounting Office, respectively, so that monitoring of the supplies and properties whereabouts is regularly made; and
Perform the necessary yearly physical inventory count of supplies and properties, and submit the necessary reports to this for monitoring and compliance purposes.

We recommended the following courses of actions:
Require the Accountant to submit all the required reports within the deadline   mandated by existing rules and regulations in order to give ample time for the auditor to provide timely results of audit and also to provide the agency of the needed information which could be vital in their decision making; and
Enjoin compliance on the existing laws, rules and regulations pertaining to the submission of financial reports and other reports relevant to the operation of the Municipality.

We recommended the following courses of action:
Request the contractor concerned to continue the implementation of the projects despite the existence of delays or else any liquidating damage that Municipality would claim in the case of further default; and
Direct the concerned Municipal personnel to ensure the proper monitoring of projects implementation to avoid delays in the performance of Municipality’s basic services to benefit its constituents.

We recommended the following courses of action:
Strictly comply the provisions of the IRR of R.A. No. 9184 to ensure transparency, equity, efficiency, and economy in government procurement and to avoid sanction on the circumvention of the rules and regulations. Henceforth, persons responsible for the conduct of procurement not in conformity with R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR shall explain in writing why fuel payments totalling to P2,845,113.13 will not be subjected to appropriate notices; and
Strengthen the procurement function of the Bids and Awards Committee to ensure that all the processes and procedures in the procurement of goods are strictly followed and to preclude irregular transactions.

We recommended for the management to exert the necessary effort in collecting their share from the proceeds on the development and utilization of the national wealth from the period where the Municipality is expecting to collection.
It is further recommended for the management to coordinate with the collecting agencies concerned, the DBM, and the Bureau of Treasury to facilitate the issuance of SARO from the DBM Central Office and to credit directly the amount representing their share to the account of the Municipality.

We recommend the following courses of action:
Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to cause the prompt submission of Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) for all bank accounts of the Municipality to the  Audit Team;
Secure the monthly Bank Statements and the copies of Credit and/or Debit Memos from the bank to be attached to the JEVs as supporting documents; and
Effect the recording of all new accounts in the books.

We recommended that following courses of action:
Direct the Municipality Treasurer to establish a system on remitting the collections to the Liquidating Officer to ensure compliance of safeguarding the government money;
Direct the Collectors to regularly prepare the Daily Statement of Collections and Accountable Forms at the close of each business day and effect remittance of their collections daily, particularly, when collections exceed P 5,000.00; and
Ensure the recognition of appropriate asset and revenue accounts on the collection made later part of the year to avoid understatement of both accounts.

We recommended the following courses of action:
Submit written explanation justifying the occurrence of the transactions despite absence of the cited documents and reports above;
Submit the documents and reports above for validation purposes of SEF transactions for calendar year 2017 and for the ensuing year; and
Enjoin compliance with the existing SEF laws, rules and regulations to preclude this Office from issuing Notice of Suspension or Disallowance.

observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | Management started the physical count of PPE; however, the same was not completed.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant  exert extra effort to locate records of these receivables. If such efforts exerted are futile, the Municipal Mayor may
file the request for authority to write –off said accounts to the COA Audit Team Leader and/or Supervising Auditor.  The request shall be supported by complete documents enumerated in Section 8.3 of COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.  As regards the liabilities, the Sangguniang Bayan may pass a resolution recommending the dropping of these accounts from the books.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | The long outstanding payables were already written off from the books of accounts.  Management is in the process of gathering data for the request for write off of the receivables not substantiated with records.

observations.recommendation: We  have recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require the Municipal Treasurer  and the Municipal Accountant to  submit all disbursement vouchers duly supported with complete and valid documents within ten (10)  days after the end the month.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | The Municipal Treasurer  claimed that some payees  did not yet claim their checks.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan appropriate funds for the titling of parcels of land acquired by the Municipality.  Thereafter,  the Municipal Mayor should require the Municipal Assessor to obtain Transfer Certificates of Title as required under Section of 449, Volume I of GAAM and Section 39(2) of PD 1445 to serve as proof of absolute ownership over these properties.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | The Municipal Assessor already started the processing of Transfer of Certificates of Title.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require the Municipal Treasurer to abide by the provisions of Section 29, Volume I of the New Government Accounting System for LGUs.  At the close of each business day, all collections should be reported in the RCD and the same must be deposited intact not later than the next banking day.  The Municipal Treasurer should not issue another booklet of official receipt to any collector unless the booklet previously issued to him/her had been fully accounted for.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | Complied with the recommendation

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require the Municipal Treasurer   to deposit collections intact with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day.  The amount collected for the day should be the amount to be deposited in order not to expose the funds for possible misuse or misappropriation and at the same time this will enable the Municipality to earn additional income in the form of interest on bank deposits.  Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer should update her cashbook and reconcile regularly the balances of cash accounts with the records of the Accounting Office.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | None
AAR 2016 | None

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management  strictly comply with Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001. A copy of the contract and all supporting documents enumerated in Section 3.1.2 thereof should be furnished to the Auditor within five (5) working days after execution of the contract in order for us to perform timely review of the same and conduct inspection of the completed projects so that deficiencies could immediately be detected and corrected.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | None

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management  strictly comply with Section 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001. A copy of the purchase order irrespective of the amount, and each and every supporting documents shall be furnished to the Auditor within five (5) working days from the issuance thereof in order for the Auditor to perform timely review of the same and conduct inspection of the items delivered.   Moreover, all the vital information in the PO such as the date of delivery should be indicated to serve as reckoning date in the computation of penalty in case the supplier fails to deliver the items on the specified date.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | There were few POs which were submitted to COA on time.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant  submit duly accomplished supporting documents of disbursements as required by regulations.  Succeeding transactions should be properly supported with complete documentation in order to establish validity of claims.  The Municipal Accountant should not process claims that are not properly documented.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | The Municipal Accountant was not present during the exit conference.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the officials and employees concerned prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels   and the Report of Fuel Consumption for all vehicles/heavy equipment owned by the municipality and submit the same to the Auditor within the first ten days of the succeeding month.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | Complied with the recommendation

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant  submit to the Audit Team all cancelled checks for inspection and proper monitoring.  Likewise, cancelled checks shall be attached to the Report of Checks Issued and reported chronologically with the other checks issued and the word “cancelled” shall be indicated on the report.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | Complied with the recommendation
AAR 2016 | Management complied with some of the recommendations and made a commitment to fully implement the same.

observations.recommendation: We   have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer  intensify the collection of Real Property and Special Education Taxes.  Notices of collection should be sent to the delinquent taxpayers to remind them of their obligation to pay their taxes. Likewise, conduct massive tax campaign in all the barangays and offer tax amnesty in order to motivate delinquent taxpayers to avail of the offer.  In addition, seek the cooperation of the barangay officials, anyway, the barangays have also shares in the collection of realty property taxes.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | None

observations.recommendation: We have recommended  that the Municipal Mayor  cause the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise taxes in order to give the tobacco farmers who worked hard for the generation of the funds the early enjoyment of benefits they rightfully deserve.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | Management made an assurance to comply with the recommendation this 2018.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the municipal officials and employees concerned  implement fully all the programs/ projects / activities embodied under the 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | Management made a commitment to  address the problem.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended   that the Municipal Mayor  cause the prompt implementation of all funds received from other national government agencies in order to deliver on time the benefits due to the intended beneficiaries and to avoid returning the unutilized funds to the donor agencies.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | Complied with the recommendation

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require the Chairperson of the GAD Focal Point System to fully implement programs/projects funded under the GAD Plan in order to fully address gender issues/problems of the constituents in the municipal level and to help the national government attain its goal of addressing gender issues in the country.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | The Chairperson of the GAD Focal Point System assured the Audit Team to comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all disbursements are supported with valid and complete documents. Further, management should adhere to Sections 9.4 and 10.4 of COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 regarding the settlement of suspensions and disallowances.  Audit suspensions should be settled within ninety (90) calendar days from receipt of the NS to avoid maturity of these suspensions to disallowances and disallowances should be settled within six (6) months from receipt of the ND by the persons liable.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | The Municipal Accountant was not present during the exit conference.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the management to follow strictly the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) concerning Government procurement to ensure that the government and the people get maximum benefits and quality of service from the contracts. They may resort to the alternative modes of procurements only in the highly exceptional cases and if they have satisfied the requisites provided in the IRR of R.A. 9184. Strict compliance to the requirements of public bidding construes the call for transparency of government transactions and accountability of public officers.
observations.managementAction: Complied
observations.reason: The Audit team has not seen the same deficiency for the year 2017, thus the recommendation is deemed to be fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the management to furnish the Auditors copies of contracts together with all the supporting documents  to the COA within five (5) working days from execution thereof pursuant to COA Circular No. 87-278 and COA Memorandum No. 2005-027 as reiterated in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: We are duly aware    that once government contracts have been perfected, they should be submitted for audit review. If there are delays in the submission is perhaps beyond the capacity of the BAC’s personnel in charge for the perfection of the contracts.  Nonetheless, we shall see to it that these documents be submitted within the prescribed period.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Finding No. 1, Page 46.

The scrutiny of the Audit team on the agency’s programs, projects, and activities revealed that the same deficiency still exist as of the year 2017.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the accountant ensure that travelling expenses are made within the prescribed limits in accordance with EO 298.
observations.managementAction: The claims of travel   expenses in most cases, are in consonance with EO 298, but we have added a matrix as basis for travels within the 50-km radius, that is not within the provision of the EO. The law is already old that and the amounts specified therein are no longer viable in today’s standards, thus, the introduction of the matrix scheme.  However, the scheme was discontinued in the middle of 2017 and effective January 1, 2018, all claims of travel expenses shall be based purely on EO 298.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Finding No. 3, Page 49.

The Audit team still noted the same deficiency. The law may already be old, however, it is still the law.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive, together with the Department of Social Welfare and Development, grant donations only to qualified people and/or public group organized for public purposes in compliance with Section 335 of Local Government Code of 1991, General Appropriations Act (GAA) of 2016, and DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 4, S2015.
observations.managementAction: We have adhered to the recommendation that donations to private or religious purposes should not be allowed and that they should be allowed only for public purposes. Donations to private individuals requiring medical, death or some form of cash assistance always pass through the DSWD for evaluation purposes
observations.reason:
The Audit team has not seen the same deficiency for the year 2017.  

Therefore, we deemed that this recommendation is fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan Members refrain from granting donations because such activity is ultra vires to their duties and responsibilities as the Legislative Branch of the municipality. Also, the letters of request for donations that they are receiving should be endorsed to the Local Chief Executive or DSWD.
observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan members are no longer allowed to reimburse any financial donations even if those are within the parameters of a legitimate contribution to the public. They can only identify the recipients but not to actually release the funds themselves and reimburse it later on. They are also not allowed from giving or approving cash donations in lieu of a request for assistance in kind.
observations.reason:
The Audit team has not seen the same deficiency for the year 2017.

Therefore, we deemed that this recommendation is fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that all officials receiving transportation allowance for travels within 50 km to refrain from reimbursing gasoline expenses. We have also recommended that officials who are assigned to use government vehicles, and officials who use private vehicles, to refrain from reimbursing gasoline expenses in adherence with 3.13, Annex “B” of COA Circular No. 2012-003, Section 8 of Article IX(B) of the Constitution, and COA Circular No. 96-004 respectively.
observations.managementAction: Basically, those officials receiving RATA are not allowed to reimburse travel expenses within the municipality but are allowed to reimburse travel or gasoline expenses outside of their area of responsibility even if their travel is within 50 kilometers. Due to inadequacy of government vehicles, we allow them to use private vehicles and they are allowed to claim for gasoline expenses but only commensurate to the travel distance to their  destination and back. Effective January 1, 2018, claims of gasoline for private vehicles is no longer allowed.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Finding No.4, Page 51.

The recommendation is partially implemented because we still noted that some still reimburse gasoline expenses while using their private vehicle.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Accountant strictly observe that all claims against government funds be supported with complete documentation and that all documents/forms forming part thereof be properly accomplished in accordance with accounting and auditing rules and regulations. We further recommend that the Municipal Treasurer pay only claims that are duly approved/certified and supported with complete documentation as required under Section 4(6) of PD 1445. Also, we recommend the agency to avoid reimbursement as much as possible.
observations.managementAction: Normally, the accounting office will not transmit disbursement  vouchers to treasury for payment if they are incomplete with supporting documents.  Perhaps the supporting documents of some  claims were overlooked that they are not there or some signatories have not affixed their signatures in the documents during verification by the accountant. Rest assured that we shall exert more efforts to overcome these lapses.
observations.reason: The Audit team has not noted the same infringement, thus we deemed that the recommendation is fully implemented as of 2017.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the management to observe that every time there is unexpended balance of the LDRRMF as of yearend, it should be transferred to Special Trust Fund under the account Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Account Code: 2-04-01-020) in the Trust Fund books.
observations.managementAction: The unobligated balance of LDRRMF have been transferred into the special trust fund in March 2018 including the balance for 2017 while the balance for 2012 was reverted back to government Equity account. In summary, LDRRMF for 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016 and 2017 are now in the special Trust fund account.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No.5, Page 52.
