observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following courses of action:

Submit written explanation to justify the unavailability of aforementioned documents and reports that support the disbursement of funds transferred from National Government Agencies;

Ensure the compliance of Item 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 12, 2014 and the submission of documents outlined by existing laws, rules and regulations; and

3. 	Enjoin compliance of Item 6 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 and Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant submits liquidation as well as pertinent documents as supporting, contracts or MOA and bidding documents relating to project implementation were by the GSO regularly.
All National Funded Projects were already implemented by the LGU and were awarded to the contractors through competitive bidding, most of the projects were already 90% or more.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management would ensure all programs, projects, or activities incorporated in the annual budget for 20% Local Development Fund will be fully implemented during the year and the identification of these projects are derived from the product of proper planning employed, availability of project identification mechanism, and project monitoring so that the objectives of promoting social, economic and environmental management services is attained.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal mayor has fast tract the implementation of 20% development fund through coordination with planning and engineering department.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following courses of action:

Submit written explanation to justify the occurrence of the said transactions and to preclude this Office from issuance of appropriate notices;

Ensure that the public calamity requirement is attained before any action to realign the approved budgets just to finance another projects; and

Enjoin compliance with the above-mentioned law, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The realignment were done through the MDC meeting and were enacted through appropriation ordinance by the Sangunian, also consultation with the 22 barangay captains were done to properly address the immediate need of the constituents of the municipality. Having done and approved, the LGU through the effort of the municipal mayor implemented the realignments made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to LDRRMO the following courses of action:

Formulate a comprehensive and integrated LDRRMFIP that clearly specifies the projects, programs, and activities to be undertaken as incorporated in the local development plan and annual work and financial plan; and

Prepare a DRRM Implementing Plan containing the courses of actions before, during and after the calamity.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor designated a Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer task to formulate the integrated LDRRMFIP, also the LGU formed a responder Team task to assist and facilitate in emergency situation as well as in times of calamity.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that in the allocation and utilization of Special Education Fund, the Local School Board shall give priority to programs, activities and projects enumerated in Sec. 272 of LGU Code, Art. 363(C) of IRR-LGU Code, DECS/DBM/DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01, series of 1998 dated April 14, 1998 and 01-A as amended.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has prioritize the implementation of SEF to all level of schools (primary,secondary) based on their prioritized proposals, mostly infra projects and equipment.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Accountant to make the necessary correcting/adjusting entries in the books to record the procurement of supplies and materials to its appropriate inventory accounts.  Promote the usage and maintenance of supply ledger cards. Ensure that subsequent purchases are initially recorded to its inventory account and charged to expense account only upon issuance to end-users.
observations.managementAction: The GSO and the Office of the Municipal Accountant have come to understanding the process necessary to implement the perpetual method of accounting

Henceforth, refrain from disbursing government funds without Disbursement Vouchers duly supported with required documents to avoid suspension in audit.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend to:

Require those persons responsible in the transactions suspended in audit to give preferential attention in settling the same for these may mature into disallowance.

Require the persons liable to immediately settle audit disallowances while still in the service to refrain from experiencing the difficulty of collecting the same in the case of employees’ retirement or separation from the service or transferred to other agencies.

Coordinate with other agencies to assists in the collection of audit disallowance of employees who have transferred their employment in the said Agency.  If possible, insists to the agency that clearance shall first be secured by the transferee from the municipal government before he/she be employed in their Agency.

Require those employees who are still in the service but identified and determined as one or among the persons liable to immediately settle the audit disallowances.
observations.managementAction: The LGU collected the disallowance on General Fund, Trust Fund disallowance shall follow.
observations.reason: The disallowance has not fully collected.

observations.recommendation:
In order to foster public awareness and monitoring of the procurement process and implementation of perfected and awarded infrastructure contracts and other procurement activities of the Municipal Government, we recommend the following:

To require the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) for all types of procurements at the end of each semester and have it approved by the HOPE.

To submit the duly approved PMR, furnishing a copy to the Audit Team’s Office, to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.
observations.managementAction: The Bac Secretariat prepare the procurement monitoring report for 2015 and 2016 for submission.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the concerned Municipal Officials should review thoroughly the cited programs and projects of the remaining fund of P11,280,350.50 and prioritize it to the assessed needs of the locality through the Municipal Planning and Development Council.

Conduct adequate planning so that programmed projects in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) be reasonably attained in the current year to obtain optimum utilization of the 20% Development Fund.

Ensure that unexpended balances of the current year be reprogrammed/re-appropriated in the ensuing year as prescribed in Sec 4 of DILG MC No. 97-30.
observations.managementAction: The LGU continues to implement its 20% of unutilized portion, and has increase its utilization sake, mostly the projects were done on succeeding periods.
observations.reason: Unused funds were supplemented to other prioritized projects for the succeeding year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that LCE should instruct the Municipal DRRMC Head Secretariat and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit prescribed and complete report on the monthly utilization of the LDRRM fund for CY 2015 to the COA Team. Submit also documentary requirement or support to use of the QRF, which is a stand-by fund, such as the SB Resolution or National declaration of disaster in the area.

We also recommend that the Municipal DRRMC Head Secretariat and the Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile their reports regarding discrepancy of LDRRMF in the Annual Budget versus LDRRM Plan. Supporting documents of this discrepancy such as resolution on the supplemental budget or any pertaining documents should be furnished to this Audit Team.

	In compliance with the above-stated COA Circular, we recommended transfer and reclassification of accounts as follows:   

(a) In GF books:   DR: Due to Other Funds (20301010)    	756,812.10
		      CR: Cash in Bank (10102010)      		756,812.10

(b) In TF books:   DR: Cash in Bank (10102010)        	756,812.10
		     CR: Trust Liability-DRRM (20401020)             	756,812.10

	A subsidiary account by year shall be maintained to ensure the clear monitoring of the funds and that after the 5-year period any unutilized amount shall be reverted back to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund and shall be available for use in the disaster risk reduction and management activities and other social services after subsequent enactment by the Local Sanggunian.

We further recommend submitting to the Audit Team the complete documentation on the purchase of rescue vehicle such as the authority to purchase, copy of the SB Resolution, and such other documents relating to this transaction for auditorial review.

For the collection services of segregated solid waste made available to entire Municipality, we recommend employing additional manpower and acquiring additional equipments that will be used in transferring all residual wastes from all barangays to a long term or disposal facility or sanitary landfill.

	We also recommend monitoring and maintaining accessible roads and fast-track any on-going rehabilitation of roads as it is essential to be able for the garbage trucks to collect the segregated solid waste to remote areas.

	We also recommend formalizing the creation of Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office (MENRO) to act as a primary responsible office in the monitoring and execution of the strategies and plans embodied in the 10-year ESWMP and also address any noted concerns upon implementation.

Submit also copy SB Resolution authorizing such additional PS expenditure, and a written explanation/justification why an additional or extra bonus was granted despite excess in appropriation already on the PS items, in order for the Audit Group to arrive at a final and appropriate auditorial basis for decision and recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the transaction documents, financial statements and supporting schedules, the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements, inventory of government property of the LGU, and the related accounting reports such as those mentioned in the provisions of PD 1445 and prescribed in the NGAS Manual for LGUs, and in pursuance to the provisions of Sec. 347 of RA 7160, be submitted to COA Audit Team within the prescribed time.  The attached Annex C-11will serve as a guide on completeness and timely submission in compliance with each specific legal basis for submission of the same.

              We further recommend that appropriate records/logbooks or transmittal documents be used or maintained by each Office for proper identification of responsible persons who caused the delay, and to establish effective internal control system and sound fiscal administration.
observations.managementAction: The LGU shall follow COA’s recommendation
observations.reason: Submission of reports were delayed due to transition from semi computerized to full computerization of FS, documents were still delayed its submission due to  incomplete authorized signature of some DVs..

observations.recommendation: Management must adhere strictly with the regulations set forth under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 for COA’s timely review and to avoid undue delay in the post-audit of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Such documents were attached to paid DVs transmitted.

5)Set-up the Liability Account for the owner of the land used in the housing project based on the Memorandum of Agreement. Adjust all charges to Land Account to appropriate liability account.

observations.recommendation: Consider transferring the deposits maintained with First Consolidated Bank to authorized government depository banks, to eliminate exposure of government funds from certain risks particularly on insolvency and bank closure.

observations.reason: Review of financial statements submitted showed that the FCB account was still maintained by the municipality.

observations.recommendation: Immediately organize an Internal Audit Service (IAS) to comply with Section 4 of AO No. 70.
observations.managementAction: 2013 AAR Organization of the Internal Audit Service (IAS) was not initiated by the Municipality.
observations.reason: No IAS unit is currently functioning in the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to make a quarterly reconciliation of their cash balances so that discrepancies can be adjusted immediately to present accurate and reliable cash balances.
observations.managementAction: 2013 AAR No reconciliation of cash balances has been initiated by the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Account.
observations.reason: Review of financial records/reports still showed an unreconciled balances between cash book and ledger balances.

observations.recommendation: Direct all officials and employees with outstanding cash advance to immediately liquidate the same. Stop granting additional cash advance if the previous ones have not been settled/liquidated yet pursuant to Section 89 of PD 1445. Exert strong effort to collect or cause the liquidation of cash advances made by employees not anymore in the service. Coordinate from other government agencies in case of transfer by former employees.

observations.reason: Demand letters were sent to those with long outstanding cash advances.

observations.recommendation: Direct all those who received the PEI to refund the amount of P10,000.00 each, the payment of which is not legally due them, the PS limitation provided under Section 325(a) of RA 7160, having been not observed by the municipality. Henceforth, management shall strictly observe the fifty-five percent (55%) limitation on personal services to prevent disallowances in audit.
observations.managementAction: 2001 AAR Minimal repayments were deducted from the payroll.
observations.reason: Post-audit of payroll showed the deduction for repayment of excess PEI.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Supply Officer designee to furnish the Auditor with the approved purchase orders and notify her of the deliveries of supplies within 24 hours from issuance of such purchase orders and acceptance of deliveries, so that surprise and selective inspection of the deliveries be made.
observations.managementAction: 2006 AAR There was no action on the part of the Managements specifically on the part of the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the approved POs.
observations.reason: No approve POs submitted hence no deliveries has been inspected.

observations.recommendation: Initiate appropriate measures for recovery of individual records of borrowers. Intensify collection of KKK loan repayment by issuing individual bills of collections. Initiate relending of said assistance to qualified applicants duly screened by a committee created for that purpose. Make representation with the local law-making body for possible write-off of accounts deemed uncollectible.
observations.managementAction: 2002 AAR No bills of collections were sent to borrowers due to the absence of subsidiary ledger to support list of borrowers.
observations.reason: Review of financial records confirmed that there was no subsidiary ledger to support list of borrowers.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant  exert extra effort to locate records of these receivables.  If such efforts exerted are futile, the Municipal Mayor may file the request for authority to write –off said accounts to the COA Audit Team Leader and/or Supervising Auditor.  The request shall be supported by complete documents enumerated in Section 8.3 of COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.  As regards the liabilities, the Sangguniang Bayan may pass a resolution recommending the dropping of these accounts from the books.

We  have recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require the Municipal Treasurer  and the Municipal Accountant to  submit all disbursement vouchers duly supported with complete and valid documents within ten (10)  days after the end the month.

We have recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan appropriate funds for the titling of parcels of land acquired by the Municipality.  Thereafter,  the Municipal Mayor should require the Municipal Assessor to obtain Transfer Certificates of Title as required under Section of 449, Volume I of GAAM and Section 39(2) of PD 1445 to serve as proof of absolute ownership over these properties.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require the Municipal Treasurer to abide by the provisions of Section 29, Volume I of the New Government Accounting System for LGUs.  At the close of each business day, all collections should be reported in the RCD and the same must be deposited intact not later than the next banking day.  The Municipal Treasurer should not issue another booklet of official receipt to any collector unless the booklet previously issued to him/her had been fully accounted for.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require the Municipal Treasurer   to deposit collections intact with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day.  The amount collected for the day should be the amount to be deposited in order not to expose the funds for possible misuse or misappropriation and at the same time this will enable the Municipality to earn additional income in the form of interest on bank deposits.  Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer should update her cashbook and reconcile regularly the balances of cash accounts with the records of the Accounting Office.

We have recommended that management  strictly comply with Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001. A copy of the contract and all supporting documents enumerated in Section 3.1.2 thereof should be furnished to the Auditor within five (5) working days after execution of the contract in order for us to perform timely review of the same and conduct inspection of the completed projects so that deficiencies could immediately be detected and corrected.

We have recommended that management  strictly comply with Section 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001. A copy of the purchase order irrespective of the amount, and each and every supporting documents shall be furnished to the Auditor within five (5) working days from the issuance thereof in order for the Auditor to perform timely review of the same and conduct inspection of the items delivered.   Moreover, all the vital information in the PO such as the date of delivery should be indicated to serve as reckoning date in the computation of penalty in case the supplier fails to deliver the items on the specified date.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant  submit duly accomplished supporting documents of disbursements as required by regulations.  Succeeding transactions should be properly supported with complete documentation in order to establish validity of claims.  The Municipal Accountant should not process claims that are not properly documented.

We have recommended that the officials and employees concerned prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels   and the Report of Fuel Consumption for all vehicles/heavy equipment owned by the municipality and submit the same to the Auditor within the first ten days of the succeeding month.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant  submit to the Audit Team all cancelled checks for inspection and proper monitoring.  Likewise, cancelled checks shall be attached to the Report of Checks Issued and reported chronologically with the other checks issued and the word “cancelled” shall be indicated on the report.

We   have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer  intensify the collection of Real Property and Special Education Taxes.  Notices of collection should be sent to the delinquent taxpayers to remind them of their obligation to pay their taxes. Likewise, conduct massive tax campaign in all the barangays and offer tax amnesty in order to motivate delinquent taxpayers to avail of the offer.  In addition, seek the cooperation of the barangay officials, anyway, the barangays have also shares in the collection of realty property taxes.

We have recommended  that the Municipal Mayor  cause the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise taxes in order to give the tobacco farmers who worked hard for the generation of the funds the early enjoyment of benefits they rightfully deserve.
We have recommended that the municipal officials and employees concerned  implement fully all the programs/ projects / activities embodied under the 20% Development Fund.

We have recommended   that the Municipal Mayor  cause the prompt implementation of all funds received from other national government agencies in order to deliver on time the benefits due to the intended beneficiaries and to avoid returning the unutilized funds to the donor agencies.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require the Chairperson of the GAD Focal Point System to fully implement programs/projects funded under the GAD Plan in order to fully address gender issues/problems of the constituents in the municipal level and to help the national government attain its goal of addressing gender issues in the country.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all disbursements are supported with valid and complete documents. Further, management should adhere to Sections 9.4 and 10.4 of COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 regarding the settlement of suspensions and disallowances.  Audit suspensions should be settled within ninety (90) calendar days from receipt of the NS to avoid maturity of these suspensions to disallowances and disallowances should be settled within six (6) months from receipt of the ND by the persons liable.

We have recommended the management to follow strictly the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) concerning Government procurement to ensure that the government and the people get maximum benefits and quality of service from the contracts. They may resort to the alternative modes of procurements only in the highly exceptional cases and if they have satisfied the requisites provided in the IRR of R.A. 9184. Strict compliance to the requirements of public bidding construes the call for transparency of government transactions and accountability of public officers.

We have recommended the management to furnish the Auditors copies of contracts together with all the supporting documents  to the COA within five (5) working days from execution thereof pursuant to COA Circular No. 87-278 and COA Memorandum No. 2005-027 as reiterated in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
We have recommended that the accountant ensure that travelling expenses are made within the prescribed limits in accordance with EO 298.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive, together with the Department of Social Welfare and Development, grant donations only to qualified people and/or public group organized for public purposes in compliance with Section 335 of Local Government Code of 1991, General Appropriations Act (GAA) of 2016, and DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 4, S2015.

We have recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan Members refrain from granting donations because such activity is ultra vires to their duties and responsibilities as the Legislative Branch of the municipality. Also, the letters of request for donations that they are receiving should be endorsed to the Local Chief Executive or DSWD.

We have recommended that all officials receiving transportation allowance for travels within 50 km to refrain from reimbursing gasoline expenses. We have also recommended that officials who are assigned to use government vehicles, and officials who use private vehicles, to refrain from reimbursing gasoline expenses in adherence with 3.13, Annex “B” of COA Circular No. 2012-003, Section 8 of Article IX(B) of the Constitution, and COA Circular No. 96-004 respectively.

We have recommended that the Accountant strictly observe that all claims against government funds be supported with complete documentation and that all documents/forms forming part thereof be properly accomplished in accordance with accounting and auditing rules and regulations. We further recommend that the Municipal Treasurer pay only claims that are duly approved/certified and supported with complete documentation as required under Section 4(6) of PD 1445. Also, we recommend the agency to avoid reimbursement as much as possible.

We have recommended the management to observe that every time there is unexpended balance of the LDRRMF as of yearend, it should be transferred to Special Trust Fund under the account Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Account Code: 2-04-01-020) in the Trust Fund books.

We recommend that Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee to conduct a
complete physical inventory of all the properties of the municipality to establish the
actual existence of these assets and submit a report thereon and reconcile this with
the records of the Municipal Accountant and consequently, effect the necessary
adjustments in the books to present the correct account balances.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
release immediately the barangays’ shares of prior years and succeeding shares on a
quarterly basis within five (5) days after the end of each quarter.

We recommend that Management closely monitor and direct the Municipal
Accountant’s compliance to the submission of monthly and quarterly financial reports
with complete schedules and supporting documents in accordance with pertinent laws
and regulations.

We recommend that Management submit the Municipal Engineer’s Report on the
progress billings of the projects to substantiate its actual status of accomplishment
in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14,
2012.

We recommend that Management stop the practice of programming/charging
expenditures which are not development projects against the 20% development
fund and instead strictly implement developmental projects identified under the
DILG/DBM JMC No. 2011-1.

We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit
immediately the required documents and henceforth, ensure that disbursements
paid thru cash advance are supported with complete documents.

We recommend that Management explain on why there appeared to be splitting in
the procurement of medicines. Henceforth, we recommend that the Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC) comply strictly with the provisions of RA 9184 on the
bidding/procurement process to ensure reasonableness of price.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant transfer the unexpended balance of
the LDRRMF to a special Trust Fund under the Trust Fund books to ensure that the
funds are utilized solely to support disaster risk reduction and management
activities.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require the Municipal Engineer to see to it that materials testing  for infrastructure projects  be done by  DOST/BRS accredited laboratories to uncover any possible sub-standard construction materials used or components supplied by the contractor  in order to ensure the quality of the projects.  Such cost of materials testing should be shouldered by the contractor as this is included in the computation of the ABC.  Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should not process claims of contractors without the materials test results.  Moreover, management should conduct inspection of the completed projects before the end of the one year defect liability period and before the issuance of the Final Certificate of Acceptance.  In case there are structural defects attributable to improper construction and/or use of inferior quality materials, the contractor should be held liable.

We have recommended that Management require future contractors to submit performance security with a validity period that include the one year defects liability period pursuant to Section 39 of the IRR of RA 9184.  The validity period shall commence from the effectivity of the contract up to after one year from the date of completion of the project or until the issuance of the Certificate of Acceptance.

In addition, seek the cooperation of the barangay officials, anyway, the barangays have also shares on the collection of realty property taxes.

Likewise he should require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to submit all disbursement vouchers and supporting documents to avoid the issuance of Notices of Suspensions. observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer continue their laudable effort of strictly withholding taxes due the government and remitting the same to the BIR
We have recommended that the officials concerned submit the reports on time. Strict compliance as to the prescribed period relative to the submission of monthly reports is to be observed.
observations.managementAction:
The agency’s time table for the submission of these documents is on or before the 20th day of the succeeding month. Though they have complied on some occasions, they have submitted them after the 20th but all are submitted within the month except for November and December 2015 which were submitted beyond the succeeding month. 

Rest assured, they guaranteed to exert more efforts to submit these documents on time.
observations.reason: Submission of these documents was made on time during the year.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Accounting Unit to record the purchases to its proper Inventory Account and adjust the account periodically based on the submitted report on the distribution of drugs and medicines inventory by the Inventory Officer. We also recommended the Inventory Officer to conduct periodic count. We further recommended the Accounting Unit not to use the account Drugs and Medicines expenses to record donations of medical financial assistance because it will misstate the said account.
observations.managementAction: In practice, purchase of drugs and medicines for consumption by the rural health unit are charged directly to an expense account because they are consumed within a fiscal period, however, if those medicines are kept by an inventory officer and are systematically issued upon request, they are regarded as inventory. Nonetheless, effective this fiscal year 2016, the management shall record them as inventories and coordinate with the local health office to maintain records of receipts and issuances to be periodically reported to the accounting office.
observations.reason:
The Accounting office has already coordinated with health office. They have already devised a non-accountable form to be used in monitoring those items.

Also, their journal entry vouchers show recordings of drugs and medicines inventory.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the management observed that every time that there is unexpended balance of the LDRRMF as of yearend, it should be transferred to Special Trust Fund under the account Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Account Code: 2-04-01-020) in the Trust Fund books.
observations.managementAction: The unexpended balance of Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund for the year 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014 and 2015 are currently under the continuing appropriation in the general funds. They are now considered Quick Response Funds at the beginning of fiscal year 2016 and can be readily used in case of calamity or disasters due to the existence of cash allocation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 8, page 58 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the accountant should ensure that travelling expenses are made within the prescribed limits in accordance with EO 298.
observations.managementAction:
The implementation of EO 298 is always an issues in our local government. Other municipalities or national government offices offers a different approach and our office is no exemption.

Basically, we classify our travel in 2 categories: beyond 50-km and within 50-km radius. For 50-km beyond, we believe we are within the bounds of the law but for the travel within 50-km, we deviated from the standard application because we adopted a fix-rate system which is contrary to the provisions of the executive order. As stated therein, only a maximum of P400.00 may be spent, but that may not be sufficient for some travel that is why we introduced the matrix system. 

We shall study further whether to revise the fixed rate or adopt a compromised rate of payment as we do not want to preclude anybody to cancel an important travel or appointment just to save an amount to the local government.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 3, page 50 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Sangguniang Bayan Secretary to monthly submit the actual attendance   of the regular sessions of the SB members to the Human Resource Management Office and require the SB members to accomplish application for leave if applicable, for recording in leave cards, and compute the proportionate reduction thereto in case of unauthorized absences in accordance with Section 7.5 of LBC No. 103.
observations.managementAction: They required the Sangguniang Bayan Secretariat to provide the Accounting office an attendance record of regular sessions and since then, they started deducting from those allowances when an SB member incur absences.
observations.reason: The agency’s payroll showed deductions to the concerned officials’ RATA corresponding to their absences.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Accounting unit to verify the journal entries to be attached to the disbursement vouchers. We have also recommended the management to notify the GSO for all its government purchases. We also recommended the GSO to maintain stock cards and property cards, and conduct periodic count on inventories and properties, and submit the report on the said count to the Accounting unit so the Accounting unit could reconcile them with the accounts recorded and to make the necessary adjustments if any.
observations.managementAction:
The capitalizable purchases are recorded to furniture and fixtures, or property, plant, and equipment as required. 

The affected accounts were adjusted accordingly.
observations.reason: The agency’s Journal entry voucher showed entries that capitalizable purchases were recorded to their respective asset accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Accountant strictly observe that all claims against government funds be supported with complete documentation and that all documents/forms forming part thereof be properly accomplished in accordance with accounting and auditing rules and regulations.

We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer pay only claims that are duly approved/certified and supported with complete documentation as required under Section 4(6) of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The management guaranteed to exert more efforts to fully comply with the provisions of PD 1445.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 7, page 57 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to enjoined all employees of the municipality to comply with the requirements set forth under Section 48 of the General Appropriations Act and adopt control measures to ensure that the employee’s monthly net take home pay not be lower than Three Thousand Pesos (P 3,000.00) after deducting mandatory contributions and other obligations.
observations.managementAction: The management makes sure that their employees will not incur new loans that would bring their net take home pay below the threshold.
observations.reason:
The agency’s payroll reveals that the net take home pays of municipal employees were all above the minimum net pay. 

For the year 2016, the net take home pays of all municipal employees were above P3,500.00.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management furnish the Auditors copies of contracts together with all the supporting documents to the COA within the period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-001 to avoid the aforementioned penalties.
observations.managementAction: As discussed during the exit conference, the management will submit within five (5) days all perfected contracts to the COA office.
observations.reason:
All required documents were submitted to our office on time except for the set of Project documents required for Auditorial Contract Review Analysis. 

Such was addressed in Observation No. 2 page 48 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the concerned employees strictly comply with the provisions of Joint Circular No. 2004-001 of DBM, NEDA, and NCRFW; and that they ensure that the plans, activities, and programs are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection, and other assistance that the GAD provision seeks to provide.
observations.reason: The GAD Accomplishment report submitted to us showed that the GAD fund was budgeted only for GAD-related Activities. Also, we verified that the GAD fund is not less than 5% of total Budget Appropriations.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Engineer/ SWMP Focal Person to initiate concrete steps to convert the current dumping site in compliance with the minimum considerations provided under Section 39 of R.A. 9003. We recommend the Sangguniang Bayan to allocate funds for the establishment of a sanitary landfill in compliance with Section 40 and 41 of the same Republic Act.

observations.reason: Inspection conducted by the Audit Team showed that garbage collected were duly covered with limestone.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately refund the unused amount. Henceforth, direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all cash advances granted are supported with specific legal purpose and of amounts within the estimated budget.
observations.managementAction: 2015 AAR Refund of the unused cash advances was already receipted.
observations.reason: Verification of liquidation reports submitted proved the total settlement of the cash advance.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) comply strictly with the provisions of RA 9184 on their procurement process, especially on the posting of the Invitation to Bid at the required places and the posting of the correct amount of bid security.
observations.managementAction: 2014 AAR Requirements for public bidding are now strictly followed.
observations.reason: Post-audit of disbursements showed that posting of Invitation to Bid, eligibility check and Notice of Award are now posted in the PhilGEPS website.

observations.recommendation: Require the Accountant to stop the practice of recording as outright expense aid to finance various activities of the Department of Education and to record these as cash advances subject to liquidation.

observations.reason: Disbursements are now paid/implemented by the municipal government.

observations.recommendation: Create the GAD Focal Point System in the municipality in accordance with Section 37, Rule VI of R.A. 9710 to ensure that the programs, projects, activities in the Municipality are responsive to gender issues and concerns. Direct the designated GAD Focal Person to initiate activities for the establishment of a local GAD code based on the gender issues and concerns through consultation with women constituents. Coordinate with the Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) and Philippine Commission on Women (PCW) in the formulation and development of said Code. Make representation with the local law-making body for the eventual passage and approval of the Local GAD code
observations.managementAction: 2015 AAR The MSWO has been designated as GAD Focal Person.
observations.reason: Designation of the MSWO as GAD focal person was duly validated by the Audit Team. However, codification of the GAD code was not yet initiated.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to update and submit on time the monthly bank reconciliation statements as provided under Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 21, 1996 and for the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to perform a periodic reconciliation of their records.
observations.managementAction: 2015 AAR The Municipal Accountant submitted BRS until November, 2015.
observations.reason: Bank Reconciliation Statements until November, 2015 has been verified.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Accountant to carefully analyze the entries made on the accounts, trace it to transaction documents/ records and effect/ record the necessary adjusting entries.
observations.managementAction: 2015 AAR Verification made by the Municipal Accountant reduced the amount of negative balances.
observations.reason: Accounts adjusted.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to comply strictly with the provision of Section 6.05 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 on the timely submission of the accounts. Otherwise, impose administrative disciplinary action provided under Section 122 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: 2015 AAR Monthly trial balances were submitted until June 2016 on November 2016.
observations.reason: There was considerable delay in the submission of July to December trial balances and year-end financial statements.

observations.recommendation: Direct the LDRRMO to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the preparation of the Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRM Fund for submission to the COA Auditor within the prescribed period for proper monitoring and audit.
observations.managementAction: 2015 AAR There was no submission of the LDRRMFIP and other reports to the Office of Auditor.
observations.reason: The LDRRMFIP was prepared and given to the Auditor only upon demand. However, utilization report of the LDRRM fund was not submitted.

observations.recommendation:
Enjoin the Chairman of the Local GAD Council to observe and comply with the provisions of Executive Order No. 273 and DBM/NEDA/NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1

Ensure that sufficient funds are provided for the programmed activities by including in the annual budget the 5% appropriation for Gender and Development.
observations.managementAction: 2013 AAR There was no provision for the GAD fund in the Annual Budget.
observations.reason: GAD activities were incorporated with the programs of the different departments of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: Direct all persons held liable therefore to immediately and fully settle the disallowances in audit pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006 to enable the Municipality to utilize the funds for its priority development projects.
observations.managementAction: 2009 AAR Minimal repayments were deducted from the payroll.
observations.reason: Post-audit of payroll showed the deduction for repayment of COLA differential.
